CLARKE, ROBERT LUCAS HYRAPIET, Indian C.S. (joint mag., United Prov.).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 25th Dec., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., July, 1898.

CLARKE, ROBERT THOMAS, B.A., LL.D., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Raphoe Royal School and Dublin Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 14th Dec., 1895, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; employed under the Kashmir Durbar as settl. offr., in Baltistan, July, 1898, to Dec., 1901; transf. to N.W. Frontier Prov., Jan., 1902; re-transf. to Punjab, Nov., 1903.

CLARKE, SYDENHAM CUBBON, Col., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Arrived, 9th Jan., 1862; apptd. asst. engr., Mysore, Oct., 1862; exec. engr., Sept., 1867; served at Chatham, March to Sept., 1874; acting prof. of surveying, Cooper's Hill Coll., Sept., 1874; returned to

Madras, Nov., 1876; served under the govt. of India on the Peshawar-Jalalabad state railway, Nov., 1879, and in Baluchistan; temporary supt. of works, Madras, April, 1883; offd. for various periods as suptg. engr., and in May, 1889, was in charge of public works, stores, and workshops; ret'd., March, 1891.

CLARKE, TREDWAY GEORGE, Col., I.S.C.—Ensign, Madras infantry, 1853; served in the Mysore commn. from Oct., 1857; dep. supt., Nov., 1862; dep. commr., 1873; sec. to chief commr., 1877; commr., Nagar divn., May, 1879; commr. of Coorg, 1881; offg. rest. in Mysore and chief commr. of Coorg, Dec., 1884, to March, 1885; and afterwards commr.; reverted to mil. duty, Jan., 1891.

CLARKE, WILLIAM EDWARD THOMPSON, B.A., Barr. (1872) (chief presy. mag., Madras).—Joined the service, 1st Feb., 1882, as subord. judge, Ootacamund; chief presy. mag., June, 1904.

CLARKE, WILLOUGHBY CHARLES STANLEY, Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Assam Commn.—First commn. as ensign, June, 1851; arrived, 15th Oct., 1851; was employed on mil. duty till 1862, including service in the Sonthal expedn., 1855, and in the China expedn., 1860-61; also served as supt. of police and mag., govt.-gen.'s camp, 1859-60; from June, 1862, served in the Assam commn.; dep. commr., July, 1867; returned to Europe, 1889.

CLARKSON, FRANK CECIL, Major, I.M.S. (sany. commr., Bengal).—Joined the service, 30th Sept., 1886; in mil. employ till May, 1893, since when he offd. as civil surgn. and dep. sany. commr., Bengal; confd. as civil surgn., July, 1898; sany. commr. and dep. inspr.-gen. of civil hospss., Bengal, March, 1903.

CLARKSON, JOHN WILKINS, Lieut.-Colonel, I.M.S., late Bombay Sanitary Dept.—Joined the service, 30th Sept., 1875; arrived, 17th May, 1876; dep. sanitary commr., May, 1881; sanitary commr., April, 1896; ret'd., March, 1903.

CLATWORTHY, PERCIVAL JOHN SANDERS, India Office (2nd class clerk, India Store Depôt).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 25th July, 1905.

CLAY, WILLIAM MCKINLEY, B.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 28th Oct., 1862; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., asst. commr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; on famine relief duty, 1873-74; mag. and collr., 1st grade, March, 1887; ret'd., 1888.

CLAYTON, ALFRED HARE, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1904.

CLAYTON, FRANK, F.C.H., A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1888, and posted to N.W.P. and O.; exec. engr., July, 1898.

CLAYTON, HAROLD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Marlborough and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived 10th Dec., 1897, and served in Burma as asst. commr., asst. settlt. offr. and settlt. offr.

CLAYTON, HUGH BYARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of

1900; arrived, 16th Dec., 1901, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and railway mag.

CLEGG, ROBERT BAILEY, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 12th Jan., 1885, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and head asst.; sub-collr. and joint mag. and principal asst. collr., July, 1897; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1899; collr. and mag., Oct., 1900.

CLEGHORN, JAMES, C.S.I., M.D., Surgn.-Gen., late Indian Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., March, 1865; arrived, 26th Sept., 1865; held various mil. med. charges, and offd. as civil surgn. at several stations; from Feb., 1870, served in the N.W. Prov., as civil surgn., at various stations; offg. surgn. supt., presidency gen. hosp., Calcutta, June, 1885, to March, 1886, when he was apptd. civil surgn. of Lucknow; inspr.-gen. of civil hospss., Punjab, Sept., 1891; in 1893 was employed on special duty in connection with the cantonmts. act; dir. gen., Indian Med. Serv., and sanitary commr. with govt. of India, March, 1895; C.S.I., June, 1897; ret'd., Oct., 1898; K.H.S., Oct., 1898.

CLEMENTS, ERNEST, Indian C.S. (1st asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Duwich Coll., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1893, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., Aug., 1900; asst. agent for sardars in Deccan, Oct., 1902 to Sept., 1903; 1st asst., Oct., 1905.

CLEMENTSON, EDWARD HENRY, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. from R.I.E. Coll., 1st Oct., 1876, and posted to Mysore as asst. engr.; transfed. to State Railways, July, 1880; exec. engr., Feb., 1890; ret'd., Nov., 1902.

CLENCH, ROBERT CHERRETT, Registry and Record Dept., India Office (supplemental clerk).—Apptd. supplemental clerk, 1st Jan., 1893.

CLERK, HUGH EDWARD, Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1880; exec. engr., Aug., 1893.

CLEVELAND, CHARLES RAITT, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (inspr.-gen. of police, Central Prov.).—Educ. at Christ's Coll., Finchley, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1887, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; asst. settlt. officer, Jan., 1890; asst. to commr. of settls. and agric., Nov., 1890; settlt. officer, Oct., 1891; commr. of excise, &c., Nov., 1894; dep. commr., April, 1897; inspr.-gen. of police, May, 1901; C.I.E., Jan., 1906.

CLIBBORN, JOHN, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., late Indian Army.—First commn., July, 1869; asst. engr., Oudh irrigation, March, 1872, exec. engr., Nov., 1881; principal Thomason Civil Engrg. Coll., Roorkee, Aug., 1892; C.I.E., Nov., 1901; ret'd., Dec., 1902; is the author of several works on Indian irrigation.

CLIFFORD, SAMUEL LE POER TRENCH, late Punjab Commn.—From 1st April, 1861, served in the subord. jud. dept.; in Jan., 1872, was apptd. registrar of the jud. commr.'s court in Oudh; dep. registrar of chief court, Punjab, Oct., 1872; offd. for various periods as judge, small cause court; jud. asst., Jan., 1884; dist. judge, Mar., 1895; divl. judge, Aug., 1898; ret'd., July, 1905; author of 'Notes on the Code of Criminal Procedure.'

CLIFFORD, WILLIAM WIGRAM, late Police Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the dept., 19th Sept., 1868, as acting asst. district supt.; confd., Feb., 1871; district supt., March, 1875; ret'd., Jan., 1900.

CLIFT, HENRY WILKINS, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. 8th Oct., 1868, and employed in buildings and roads branch in Mysore; exec. engr., Sept., 1871; served also in irrig. branch, and on famine relief works; transf. to state railways, July, 1879, and employed on Mysore and S. Mahratta lines, and on Bengal-Assam railway survey; E. Coast railway, Nov., 1889; ret'd., Dec., 1895.

CLIFTON, CHARLES NAPIER, Bombay Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1876, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1886; suptg. engr., Nov., 1903.

CLOETE, HENRY NEWMAN CUNNINGHAM, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., and posted to Bombay, 10th Aug., 1868; exec. engr., March, 1873; transf. to state railways, Dec., 1873, and employed on W. Rajputana railway survey, and on the Punjab and Kandahar state railways; transf. to Bengal, May, 1881; suptg. engr., May, 1893; ret'd., Oct., 1903.

CLOETE, JOSIA GORDON, Lieut.-Gen., late Rev. Survey Dept., Madras.—Ensign, Madras infantry, Feb., 1857; arrived, May, 1857; served in the Madras police as asst. supt., June, 1861, to June, 1863, and from the latter date in the rev. survey; dep. supt., Jan., 1872; ret'd. from the dept., 1888.

CLOSE, ALFRED HUDLESTON GRANVILLE, Punjab Police Dept. (dist. supt.).—Joined the dept. as offg. asst. dist. supt. of police, 8th March, 1877; confd., Sept., 1878; dist. supt., Sept., 1888.

CLOUGH, JOHN WILLIAM, India Office (2nd class clerk, Acctnt.-Genl.'s dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 25th July, 1892.

CLOWES, THOMAS HERBERT, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1883, and posted to Bengal; exec. engr., Nov., 1894; under sec., public works dept., Nov., 1897; lent to Egyptian govt., Oct., 1898; ret'd., Sept., 1903.

COATES, WILLIAM M.D., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service, Punjab (prof'r., Lahore Med. Coll.).—Joined the service March, 1877; in mil. employ. till Oct., 1881, since when he has held various civil surgeoncies; prof'r., Lahore med. coll., March, 1893.

COBB, HENRY VENN, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (resdt. in Gwalior).—Educ. at King's Sch., Canterbury, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 18th Nov., 1886, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst. collr., Feb., 1892; acting asst. rest., Mysore, and sec. to chief commr., Coorg, Aug.-Nov., 1893, and from April, 1895; in 1894 acted as dep. dir., land records, and as dep. commr. of salt, &c., rev., pol. agent, Ajmere, Nov., 1896; asst. resdt., Kashmir, Feb., 1899; resdt. Jaipur, Dec., 1900, to April, 1903; pol. agent in E. Rajputana States, April, 1904; resdt. in Gwalior, April, 1905.

COBB, ROBERT, M.D., Lt.-Col., Indian Med. Service (civil surgn., Midnapore).—First

commn., 31st Mar., 1876; civil surgn. at various stations from April, 1877; civil surgn., Midnapore, Sept., 1905.

COBB, WILLIAM HEY, M.A., Barr. (1884), Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at St. Peter's Sch., York, Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 25th Dec., 1884, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; city mag., Lucknow, July, 1889; also wasika officer, Oct., 1891; joint mag., Aug., 1893; dep. commr., Jan., 1896; mag. and collr., Nov., 1896.

COBDEN-RAMSAY, LOUIS EVELEIGH BAWTREE, Indian C.S. (regr. of co-operative credit societies, Bengal).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived 9th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. settl. offr. and supt. of survey, Mar., 1900; under sec. to govt. of Bengal in gen., rev., and stat. depts., April, 1901, to Dec., 1902; regr. of co-operative credit societies, May, 1905.

COBDEN-RAMSAY, VERE BAWTREE, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 9th Dec., 1899, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

COCKELL, WILLIAM FORBES, Postal Dept. (asst. dir.-genl. of Post Office).—Held appts. in the inland customs dept. from 27th Aug., 1871; joined the postal dept. as inspr., Feb., 1879; supt., Aug., 1880; asst. dir.-genl. of post-office, Oct., 1894.

COCKERELL, HORACE ABEL, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 31st Oct., 1853; served as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and mag. and collr., also as supt. of survey, Sept., 1859, offg. chairman, justices of the peace, Calcutta, and commr. of police, March, 1869, and Dec., 1872; commr. of divn., June, 1872; sec. to the govt. of Bengal, jud. and pol. depts., April, 1878; member of Bengal legisl. council, 1878, 1880, and 1882; board of rev., March, 1883; offg. lieut.-govr. of Bengal, Aug., 1885; ret'd., Aug., 1887.

COCKS, CHARLES SEBASTIAN SOMERS, C.M.G., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (special asst. sec.).—Apptd. clerk in foreign office, London, 16th April, 1894; sec. to British delegates to conference at Brussels on industrial property, Dec., 1897; sec. to special commn. to conduct commercial negotiations with China, Sept., 1901; C.M.G., June, 1903; pte. sec. to Visct. Cranborne, M.P., July, 1903, and to Earl Percy, M.P., Oct., 1903; apptd. special asst. sec. in the foreign dept., Calcutta, 15th Sept., 1904.

CODRINGTON, GEORGE HENRY FORBES, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Police Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 4th May, 1857; served on mil. duty till Dec., 1865, and subsequently in the Bombay police dept. as asst. supt. and district supt.; 1st grade supt., April, 1883; reverted to mil. dept., 1892.

COGHILL, NORMAN SINCLAIR, Capt., Indian Army, Barr (1901), Bombay Pol. Dept. (administrator of Radhanpur State).—First commn., 29th Nov., 1890; in mil. employ till May, 1896, when he was apptd. offg. asst. supt., Palanpur; confd. in that appt., Oct., 1897; administrator of Radhanpur State, Oct., 1903.

COGHLAN, ROBERT NESBIT, late Customs Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 23rd April, 1871; from March, 1872, served as gen. manager of the customs bonded warehouses, Bombay; from March, 1874, served as asst. collr. of salt rev.; collr. of sea customs, Karachi, and asst. commr. of salt rev. in Sind, Sept., 1882; collr. of customs and salt rev. in Sind, March, 1891; retired, Feb., 1900.

COGSWELL, MARK JAMES.—Apptd. controller of printing and stationery, Calcutta, 1st July, 1904.

COHEN, SAMUEL, India Audit Office (2nd class clerk).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, 12th March, 1900.

COLDSTREAM, JOHN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad. and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived 19th Nov., 1901, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

COLDSTREAM, WILLIAM, B.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; jud. asst., July, 1876; dep. commr., March, 1877; acted as commr. in various divns. from 1880; ret'd., June, 1894; author of a work on the grasses of the Southern Punjab.

COLE, ALFRED WILLIAM RICHARDSON, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 21st Nov., 1897, and served in the N.W. Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

COLE, CHARLES JOHN, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1879, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., March, 1887; under sec. to govt., railway branch, April, 1899, to Oct., 1901.

COLE, HENRY WALTER GEORGE, Major, Indian Army (dep. commr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Wellington Coll. and R. M. Coll., Sandhurst; first commn., 29th Aug., 1885; joined the Assam commn., July, 1897, as asst. commr.; dep. commr., April, 1901.

COLE, WILLIAM HAMMOND, M.A., late Survey of India Dept.—Served in the educn. dept., N.W. Prov., April, 1862, to March, 1864, as profir. of mathematics at the govt. coll., Agra; from Jan., 1867, served in the great trigon. survey; asst. supt. of surveys, Sept., 1869; dep. supt., July, 1882; apptd. to charge of headquarters office, Dehra Dun, and survey detachment in Kashmir, Jan., 1884; ret'd., April, 1892; author of various contributions to the survey publs., and of translns. relating to geodesy.

COLE, WILLIAM HENRY, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. 1st Oct., 1876, and posted as asst. engr. to state railways; asst. supt. of way and works, N.W. Railway, Jan., 1888; exec. engr., Feb., 1888; ret'd. March, 1898; apptd. sec. to Madras Ry. Co., 1903.

COLEBROOK, HENRY WILLIAM VAUGHAN, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (chief engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll., apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on irrgn. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., May, 1883; transf'd. to Burma, April, 1894, and placed in charge of central irrgn. divn.; supig. engr.,

Dec., 1902; chief engr., April, 1905; ditto, and sec. to govt., Burma, Aug., 1905.

COLERIDGE, FRANCIS ARTHUR, Indian C.S. (head asst. Madras).—Educ. at Malvern Coll., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 5th Dec., 1894, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Dec., 1902.

COLES, FRANK ELLIS, Cent. Prov. Police (dist. supt.).—Apptd. to the dept. as offg. asst. dist. supt. of police, April, 1874; confd., April, 1878; dist. supt., July, 1880.

COLLEN, SIR EDWIN HENRY HAYTER, G.C.I.E., C.B., Lieut.-Gen., late Indian Army.—First commn., royal artillery, July, 1863; served with the Abyssinian expedn., 1868 (medal); commissary of ordnance, India, 1869-70; admitted to the Bengal staff corps, May, 1873; acted as asst. mil. sec. to govt. of India, 1870 and 1873-74; sec. to Indian ordnance commn., 1874-75; 3rd asst. sec. to govt. of India, mil. dept., March, 1875; 2nd asst. sec., April, 1876; 1st asst. sec., Dec., 1876; dep. asst. quartermaster-gen., imperial assemblage, Delhi, 1876-77; on special duty in intelligence branch, London, 1877-79; sec. to Indian army organisation commn., 1879-80; served during the Afghan war as asst. controller-gen. of supply and transport, 1880-81 (medal); dep. sec. to govt. of India, mil. dept., April, 1884; served in Egypt with Suakim field force and as mil. sec. to Sir G. Graham, 1885 (medal with clasp, and Khedive's star); acctnt.-gen., mil. dept., govt. of India, July, 1886; sec. to govt. of India, mil. dept., Nov., 1887; C.I.E., Jan., 1889; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1893; mil. member of govr.-gen.'s council, April, 1896, to April, 1901; G.C.I.E., Jan., 1901; ret'd., Sept., 1905.

COLLETT, ARTHUR PRESTON, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Malvern Coll., and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 28th Nov., 1904, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.

COLLETT, CHARLES MORDEN, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Malvern Coll. and Ariel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh, as asst. commr., and asst. mag. and collr.

COLLIE, MACKINTOSH ALEXANDER THOMAS, M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (presy. surgn., Bombay).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1883, and was placed on general duty; sec. to surgn.-gen., Bombay, March, 1888; resdt. surgn., St. George's Hosp., and profir., Grant Med. Coll., Dec., 1892; from Oct., 1895, served as civil surgn. at various stations; presy. surgn., Bombay, Jan., 1903.

COLLIER, GEORGE HERMAN, India Office (senior clerk, store dept.).—Educ. at Uppingham Sch.; junr. clerk, India office, 17th Feb., 1876, senr. clerk, Aug., 1896.

COLLIN, EDWARD WOODFIELD, B.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Bengal).—Educ. at Eton, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1878, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; private sec. to finl. member of govr.-gen.'s council, Jan., 1880; served under the foreign dept., Jan. to July, 1881, and in the finl. dept., March to Sept., 1885; settlt. officer, Muzaffarpur, Jan., 1886; offd. as dir. of land records, Bengal, April, 1888; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1889; mag. and

collr., Jan., 1894, mag. of 24-pergunnahs, Dec., 1895; sec., rev. board, June, 1900; member legisl. council, Bengal, May, 1902; commr., March, 1904.

COLLINS, SIR ARTHUR JOHN HAMMOND, Kt., K.C., Barr. (1860), late Madras High Court.—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Cambridge, of which he was fellow; called to the bar (Gray's Inn), 1860; Q.C. and bencher of his inn, 1877; recorder of Poole, 1873-79, and of Exeter from 1879, until he was apptd. chief justice of the high court, Madras, 28th Nov., 1885; vice-chancellor, Madras univ., 1880; ret'd., Aug., 1899.

COLLINS, BERNARD ABDY, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Malvern Coll., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 14th Nov., 1904, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

COLLINS, GEORGE GOWER, late Burma Commn.—Held subord. appts. from 25th July, 1870; apptd. to the Burma police dept. as asst. district supt., Oct., 1882; joined the commn. as supernumerary asst. commr., March, 1884; asst. supt., Rangoon jail, Oct., 1884; asst. commr., March, 1886; dep. commr., June, 1898; ret'd., Aug., 1905.

COLLINS, SIDNEY ARTHUR, India Office (2nd class clerk, Store Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, India store dept., 4th Dec., 1885.

COLOMB, RICHARD PASLEY, Major, Indian Army (dep. commr., Berar).—Joined the service, 23rd July, 1879; in mil. employ till July, 1890; asst. pol. resdt., Aden, Aug., 1890, to Nov., 1891; asst. pol. agent, Mahikantha, Jan. to July, 1892; asst. commr., Berar, Sept., 1892; dep. commr., Aug., 1897.

COLQUHOUN, ARCHIBALD ROSS, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Entered the dept. in Oct., 1871, and served as asst. and exec. engr. in Burma; on special duty as personal asst. to Major Street on his mission to Siam, Jan.-May, 1879; posted to special duty in Upper Burma as civil officer and apptd. a dep. commr., Feb., 1886; retransfd. to public works dept., and posted to Baluchistan, Aug., 1889; services lent to British S. Africa Co., 1891-92; ret'd., Nov., 1893; author of 'Across Chryse,' 'Among the Shans,' 'The Truth about Tonquin,' 'The Opening of China,' 'Burma and the Burmans.'

COLQUHOUN, HENRY ARCHIBALD COLEBROOKE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Wellington Coll. and Merton Coll., Oxford; appt. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 18th Dec., 1895, and served in Assam as asst. commr., and asst. to pol. agent, Manipur.

COLSTON, EDWARD JOHN, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Chesterfield Gr. Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1898, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

COLVIN, SIR AUCKLAND, K.C.M.G., K.C.S.I., C.I.E., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Eton; apptd. from Haileybury, 1857; arrived, 17th Jan., 1858, and served in the N. W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlt. officer; offg. under sec. to the govt. of India in the home dept., May, 1864, and in the foreign dept., Jan. to Nov., 1865; settlt. officer, July, 1869; sec., board of rev., N.W. Prov., March, 1870; on special duty for revision of Allahabad settlt.,

June, 1870; offg. sec. to govt., N.W. Prov., April, 1873, and again in 1875; offg. commr. of excise and stamps, and inspr.-gen. of registrn., June, 1873; mag. and collr., Basti, Feb., 1877, and afterwards at Bijaur; offg. commr. of inland customs under the govt. of India, Nov., 1877; transfed. for employment in Egypt, Jan., 1879, and served as controller of Egyptian finance and subsequently as finance minister to the Egyptian govt.; acted as consul-gen. in Sir E. Malet's absence; finl. member of council of the govr.-gen. of India, Oct., 1883; lieut.-govr. of the N.W. Prov., and chief commr. of Oudh, Nov., 1887, to Nov., 1892; ret'd., Dec., 1892.

COLVIN, BAZETT WETENHALL, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Eton; apptd. from Haileybury, 1850; arrived, 29th June, 1850; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. till Jan., 1854, and subsequently in the N.W. Prov.; asst. commr., Kumaon, April, 1855; mag. and collr., Mainpuri, July, 1865; civil and sessions judge, Allahabad, July, 1873; on special duty at Agra during visit of H.R.H. the Prince of Wales, Feb., 1876; offg. sec. to govt., N.W. Prov., May, 1876; commr., Meerut, Jan. 1879; addl. member, govr.-gen.'s council, Jan., 1877, to May, 1881; member, board of rev., N.W. Prov., Jan., 1862; ret'd., April, 1885.

COLVIN, ELLIOT GRAHAM, Indian C.S., Bengal (agent to govr.-genl., Rajputana).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 4th Jan., 1883, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Assam as personal asst. to chief commr., May, 1884; asst. pol. agent, Rajputana, Sept., 1886; private sec. to lieut.-govr. of Bengal, April, 1887; 1st asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent in Baluchistan, April, 1889; served under finl. dept. of govt. of India from June, 1890; offg. postmr.-gen., Bengal, July, 1890; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1891; mag. and collr., March, 1895; pol. agent, Ulwar, Nov., 1896; pol. agent, Eastern Rajputana states, Sept., 1897; rev. and jud. commr., Baluchistan, April, 1900; resident in Kashmir, March, 1903; agent to govr.-genl., Rajputana, and chief commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Oct., 1905.

COLVIN, JOHN RUSSELL COLQUHOUN, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army (pol. agent).—Joined the service, 13th Aug., 1879, and was employed under the mil. dept. to Aug., 1886, including service in the E. Soudan, 1885; apptd. asst. to the agent in Rajputana, Aug., 1886; private sec. to lieut.-govr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Nov., 1887; govr. to the Nawab of Rampur, April, 1889; rest. pol. officer, Rampur state, April, 1894; asst. to rest. in Mysore, April, 1897; pol. agent in Bundelkhand, Aug., 1897; supt. of state council, Cooch Behar, April, 1899, to Nov., 1903; on spel. duty in Central India, Oct., 1905.

COLVIN-SMITH, SIR COLVIN, K.C.B., M.D., Surgn.-Genl., late Madras Med. Service.—Entered the Madras Army, 1851; served in the Burmese war of 1852-53 (medal and clasp) and with the Saugor divn. in the Indian Mutiny, 1857-59 (medal and clasp); held various regimental and civil appointments; apptd. principal medical officer with the Hyderabad subsidiary force and Hyderabad contingent, 1881; prince medl. officer with the Indian contingent in the Egyptian campaign of 1882 (C.B., medal and

clasp, Khedive's star, 3rd Class Order of Osmanieh); retired, Sept., 1884; hony. surgn. to the King, July, 1899; K.C.B., June, 1903.

CONDER, JAMES, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the public works dept., Sept., 1870; exec. engr., Oct., 1873; personal asst. to engr.-in-chief, Punjab N. railway, June, 1875; provisional asst. dir. of state railways, western system, 1877; traffic supt., Indus valley state railway, May, 1878; manager, Holkar, Sindia-Nimach, and Nimach-Nasirabad railways, Feb., 1881; traffic supt., Rajputana-Malwa railways, Sept., 1884; ret'd., 1890.

CONES, JAMES ALFRED, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr. Sept., 1878, and posted to the N.W. Prov. and Oudh (irrigation); exec. engr., Oct., 1892.

CONINGHAM, HENRY EVELYN, Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Police Dept., Madras.—Arrived, 18th March, 1855; served in the dept. from Dec., 1861, as asst. supt. and supt., and acted as dep. inspr.-gen., Sept., 1883, to May, 1884; ret'd., Feb., 1886.

CONNAUGHT AND STRATHEARN (DUKE OF), H.R.H. ARTHUR WILLIAM PATRICK ALBERT, K.G., K.T., K.P., G.C.B., G.C.S.I., G.C.M.G., G.C.I.E., G.C.V.O., P.C., Field-Marshall.—First commn., 19th June, 1868; asst. adjutant-gen., Gibraltar, Oct., 1875; brigadier-gen., Aldershot, Sept., 1880; accompanied the expedy. force to Egypt, Aug., 1882 (medal with clasp, 2nd class Medjidie, and C.B.); commander-in-chief, and 2nd member of council, Bombay, Dec., 1886, to March, 1890; on return to England was apptd. to command of southern divn., Aug., 1890; commanding at Aldershot, Oct., 1893; commanding the forces in Ireland, Jan., 1900; and the third Army Corps, Oct., 1901; presdt. of Council of R. Patriotic Fund Corpn., 1903; inspr.-genl. of the forces, May, 1904.

CONNOLLY, JOHN FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Belvedere Coll., Dublin, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 6th Nov., 1890, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., March, 1894, to May, 1896; dep. commr., Oct., 1900; offg. rev. and finl. sec. to govt., April, 1902, to April, 1904.

CONNOLLY, JOHN JOSEPH, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the accounts estabt., state railways, Jan. 1886, as examiner; ret'd., Oct., 1900.

CONNOLLY, VINCENT, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Belvedere Coll., Dublin, Trinity Coll., Dublin, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived 2nd Dec., 1902, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

CONOLLY, EDWARD ROBERT, Col., I.S.C., late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 9th April, 1858; from Sept., 1866, served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on special duty as asst. to Major Cavagnari, Feb., 1879, and again at Khaibar, Feb., 1880; jud. asst., Sept., 1883; dep. commr., Bannu, Nov., 1884; also district judge; ret'd., Aug., 1890.

CONSTABLE, EDWIN THEOBALD, M.A., late Educ. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the service as mathematical profr., Bareilly coll., 25th Feb., 1862; inspr. of public instrn., 1st

circle, Aug., 1870; inspr. of schools, Dec., 1877; offg. dir. of public instrn., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Feb., 1883, and again, April, 1890; ret'd., Sept., 1891.

CONSTABLE, WILLOUGHBY VERNER, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., 12th Sept., 1872; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1876, and posted to state railways; asst. manager, Rajputana state railway, July, 1882; employed in mil. dept., on Suakim-Berber railway, 1885; dep. consulting engr. for railways, Bombay, Nov., 1886; exec. engr., Feb., 1887; offg. manager, Bhaunagar, Gondal and Morvi railways, April-June, 1887; dep. agent, Bengal-Nagpur railway co., 1887-89; offg. dep. consulting engr. for railways, and under sec. to govt., public works dept., Madras, Feb., 1891; dep. manager, N.W. railway, Nov., 1893; manager, East coast railway, Jan., 1898; manager, Eastern Bengal State Railway, Sept., 1899; inspected rly. working in America, April to July, 1901; ret'd., Nov., 1904.

CONYBEARE, HENRY CRAWFORD ARTHUR, M.A., Barr. (1899), Indian C.S. (commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Westminster Sch., King's Coll., London, and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 3rd Nov., 1873, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Jan., 1885; dep. commr., Aug., 1890; mag. and collr., Aug., 1891; offd. as agent to govr.-gen. in Benares, Oct., 1897; offg. commr., April, 1900; confd., Aug., 1900; author of 'An Account of Pargana Dudhi, in S. Mirzapur,' and of contributions to the provl. gazetteer.

COODE, JOHN MELVILLE, India Tel. Dept. (supt.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. supt., 21st July, 1885; supt., Jan., 1889; has received India medal and Chitral clasp, 1895.

COODE, MONTGOMERY PENROSE, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1876, and posted to Haidarabad; exec. engr., Jan., 1885; transf. to Burma, Oct., 1889; asst. to chief engr. and under sec. to chief commr., Feb., 1895; asst. to chief engr. and under sec. to govt., July, 1898; suptg. engr., March, 1900.

COOK, ARTHUR WILLSTEED, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Portsmouth Gr. Sch., and Pemb. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 9th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

COOK, EDWARD MITCHENER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Uppingham Sch., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 28th Nov., 1904, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.

COOK, HENRY, M.D., F.R.C.P., Surgn.-Gen., late Med. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the Indian med. service, 24th Jan., 1855; arrived, 28th July, 1855, and served under the mil. dept. up to 1859, also holding the civil surgeony of Karachi in 1856; served with the Persian expedy. force in 1857 (medal); apptd. med. officer to pol. agency, Khalat, April, 1859; from April, 1861, served as civil surgn. in Ahmadnagar and Satara, and as supt. of Mahabaleshwar, and asst. collr. and mag. at that station; scientific officer, Abyssinian field force, Dec., 1867 (medal); physician, Jamsetji Jijibhoy hosp., and

prof., Grant med. col., May, 1873; ret'd., Nov., 1886; author of various papers publ'd. in the med. and physical society of Bombay, on the geology, &c., of Baluchistan, and of other reports and papers on scientific and med. subjects.

COOK, JOHN HOME, late Madras Rev. Survey Dept.—Joined the service, 23rd Jan., 1868, as proby. asst. supt.; conf'd., July, 1876; dep. supt., May, 1887; ret'd., May, 1898.

COOKE, ARTHUR GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Bath Coll., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; appt'd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 11th Jan., 1895, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

COOKE, CRAWFORD BOYD, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Burma Commn.—First commn., March, 1864; served under the mil. dept. up to Dec., 1868; and subsequently as asst. commr. in Burma; from Feb., 1874, to May, 1877, served as pol. agent at Bhamo, and from May, 1879, as cantonmt. mag. in Nowgong; from Jan., 1884, served as dep. commr. in Burma, and was appt'd. to special duty in Upper Burma, Dec., 1885; commr., Oct., 1890; acted as finl. commr. in 1894 and 1896-97; author of the 'British Burma Manual.'

COOKE, FREDERICK JAMES, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Bath Coll. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; appt'd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1886, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; transf'd. temporarily to Central Prov. as asst. commr., Nov., 1891; dep. commr., Sept., 1895; retransf'd. to N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Oct., 1898; mag. and collr., Oct., 1899.

COOKE, GORDON HENRY, late Survey of India Dept. (Burma).—Joined the dept., 6th Dec., 1866, and was appt'd. asst. supt., May, 1871; in 1871-72, served with the Lushai expedy. force (medal); served in various districts, and at head-quarters, Calcutta, and was appt'd. dep. supt., Oct., 1885; supt., Jan., 1895; ret'd., Dec., 1896.

COOKE, JOHN EDWARD, late Finl. Dept., Govt. of India.—From Oct., 1863, to April, 1868, served as asst. sec. to the govt. of India in the finl. dept.; dep. acctnt.-gen., Central Prov., Sept., 1870, and in Madras, Jan., 1874; asst. comptr.-gen., May, 1875; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Oct., 1878; dep. acctnt.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, July, 1883; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Oct., 1888; dep. auditor-gen., April, 1891; ret'd., April, 1897.

COOKE, THEODORE, C.I.E., M.A., M.Eng., LL.D., late Educ. Dept., Bombay.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; arrived, Nov., 1860; from June, 1865, served as principal of the civil engrg. coll., Poona; acted as dir. of public instrn. and also as dir. of land records and agric.; ret'd., Oct., 1893, and was appt'd. technical sub-dir., Imperial Institute.

COOMBS, CHARLES STANLEY, India Office (2nd class clerk, Acctnt.-Genl.'s Dept.).—Appt'd. and class clerk, 8th July, 1905.

COOPER, CHARLES PHILIP, late Police Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 28th Jan., 1863; served from June, 1864, as examr. of the insolvent debtors court, Bombay; police mag., Bombay, from Oct., 1871; acting first judge of the small cause court, Sept., 1873; senr. mag. of police and rev. judge, Bombay, June, 1874; chief presidency

mag. and rev. judge, Bombay, June, 1878; ret'd., March, 1895.

COOPER, HARRY ALBERT, B.A., India Office (senr. clerk, India Audit Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., India office, 3rd June, 1880; junr. examr., India audit office, Nov., 1881; B.A., London univ., 1887; designation changed to junr. clerk, Oct., 1892; senr. clerk, July, 1905.

COOPER, JOHN WALTER, India Office (2nd class clerk, India Store Depôt).—Appt'd. 2nd class clerk, India Store Depôt, 9th June, 1898.

COPE, JOHN ALFRED, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr. Burma).—Educ. at St. John's Sch., Leatherhead, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; appt'd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 28th Nov., 1903, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

COPELAND, THEODORE BENFEY, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov.).—Educ. at Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; appt'd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 26th Nov., 1901, and served in the Punjab and N.W. Frontier Prov. as asst. commr.

COPLESTON, FREDERICK SELWYN, late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Marlborough Coll.; appt'd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 23rd Oct., 1873, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; transf'd. to Burma, as asst. commr., Sept., 1874; on forest demarcation duty, Tounghee, Jan., 1880; dep. supt. of census, Sept., 1880; judge under the land acquisition act, March, 1881; commr. of excise and stamps, March, 1885; addl. sessions judge, Tenasserim and Pegu, March, 1886; dep. commr., Jan., 1887; commr., Dec., 1890; offi. as jud. commr., Upper Burma, in 1894-95; conf'd., Oct., 1898; chief judge, chief court, Burma, April, 1900; ret'd., April, 1902.

COPLESTON, MOST REV. REGINALD STEPHEN, D.D., Bengal Ecclesi. Dept. (Bishop of Calcutta).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and Merton Coll., Oxford; Bishop of Colombo, 1875-1902; appt'd. Bishop of Calcutta and Metropolitan of India and Ceylon, 14th May, 1902.

CORBETT, FRANK VINCENT, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Arrived, 13th Dec., 1866; asst. engr., Feb., 1867; exec. engr., March, 1871; employed chiefly on the Betwa canal; suptg. engr., Dec., 1889; chief engr. and sec. to govt. of N.W. Prov. and Oudh, irrign. branch, June, 1894; ret'd., Dec., 1898.

CORDNER, ROBERT ALLEN, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Appt'd. asst. engr., 20th Feb., 1868, and employed on irrign. works in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., March, 1871; on famine relief works in Bengal, 1873-74; member of accts. committee, Nov., 1888; suptg. engr., June, 1894; retired, July, 1898.

CORDUE, WILLIAM GEORGE RANGER, Major, R.E. (mint master, Bombay).—First commn., 25th July, 1882; in mil. employ till 5th May, 1904, when appt'd. offi. mint master, Bombay; conf'd., Jan., 1905.

CORKERY, WILLIAM ALFRED, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Bombay (civil surgn.).—First commn., 2nd April, 1881; in mil. employ till June, 1888, when appt'd. civil surgn.; member of plague committee, 1897-1899.

CORNES, JULIAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 9th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Assam, July, 1899, to Jan., 1905.

CORNISH, ROBERT, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Lancing Coll., and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 22nd Nov. 1870, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on famine relief duty, 1874; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1882; mag. and collr., Dec., 1887; ret'd., Aug., 1895.

CORNISH, WILLIAM HENRY, late Bengal Police Dept.—Joined the service, 21st Jan., 1859; served in the mil. dept., to May, 1863, when he joined the police dept. as asst. supt.; district supt., April, 1870; served in Assam, 1870-74, and 1879-83; ret'd., March, 1896.

CORNWALL, JAMES, Postal Dept., Govt. of India (postmr.-gen., United Prov.).—Inspr.-Gen. of railway mail service; apptd. to the dept., July, 1873; postmr.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, 1898; postmr.-gen., Bombay, 1900; ditto, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, April, 1901.

CORNWALL, WILLIAM WOLFRAU GARDNER, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 17th Nov., 1861, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and asst. settl. officer; mag. and collr., March, 1881; ret'd., Aug., 1887.

COSGRAVE, WILLIAM ALEXANDER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Shrewsbury Sch. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

COSTELLO, CHARLES PETER, F.R.C.S., Surgn.-Col., late Bengal Med. Dept.—Joined the service as asst. surgn., 10th Feb., 1859; served in the Umbeya campaign, 1863-64 (medal and clasp), the Jowaki expdn., 1877-78 (clasp), and the Afghan war, 1878-80 (medal); sanitary commr., Assam, Sept., 1888; offg. dep. surgn.-gen., and sanitary commr., Assam, Sept., 1888; confd. in those grades, Jan., 1889; served as principal med. officer with the Manipur Expedition, 1891 (clasp); awarded good service pension; ret'd., Jan., 1894.

COTGRAVE, THOMAS MANSON, late Salt Dept., Bombay.—Joined the service as acting asst. commr. of customs, salt and opium, 30th Jan., 1866; opium inspr., Bombay, May, 1866; confd. as asst. commr., July, 1867; asst. collr., salt rev., Feb., 1874; dep. collr., April, 1880; asst. collr., 1st class, Sept., 1900; ret'd. Sept., 1901.

COTTERELL, CECIL BERNARD, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at St. Peter's Sch., York, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived 16th Nov., 1899, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and agent to govr., Vizagapatam.

COTTON, CHARLES WILLIAM EGERTON, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Eton and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 8th Dec., 1898, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

COTTON, SIR HENRY JOHN STEDMAN, K.C.S.I., M.P., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Mag-

dalen Coll. Sch., Brighton Coll., and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 20th Oct., 1867; served in the Bengal rev. and jud. dept., and as offg. under sec. to govt. and offg. registrar, high court; asst. sec., govt. of Bengal, June, 1875; junr. sec., April, 1876; mag. and collr., Nov., 1878; offg. commr., Chittagong, 1879, and 1884; sec., board of rev., Jan., 1882; port commr., Calcutta, May, 1887; sec. to govt. of Bengal, finl. and municipal depts., May, 1890; member of Bengal legisl. council, June, 1892; chief sec. to govt., July, 1892; chief commr., Assam, Nov., 1896; addl. member of viceroy's Council, Dec., 1900; K.C.S.I., June, 1902; ret'd., Oct., 1902; M.P. for Nottingham (East), Jan., 1906; author of a 'Memo. on the Rev. History of Chittagong,' 1880, 'Memo. on the Land Tenures of Bengal,' 1884, 'England and India,' 1883, and 'New India, or India in Transition,' 1885.

COTTON, JULIAN JAMES, B.A., Indian C.S. (head asst., Madras).—Educ. at Sherborne Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 24th Dec., 1893, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Sept., 1900; author of a work on monumental inscriptions in Madras.

COTTON, WILLIAM BENSLEY, Barr. (1894), Indian C.S. (asst. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Wellington Sch. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 12th Dec., 1894, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Oct., 1903; asst. commr., Nov., 1904.

COUCHMAN, FRANCIS DUNDAS, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (under-sec., rly. board).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 24th Aug., 1886, and posted to state rlys.; dep. consg. engr. for rlys., Bombay, Aug., 1897; exec. engr., Aug., 1897; dep. consg. engr. for rlys., Calcutta, Nov., 1899; offg. under-sec. to govt., rly. branch, Oct., 1901; confd., Sept., 1902; under sec., rly. board, March, 1905.

COUCHMAN, MALCOLM EDWARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (spcl. settlmt. officer, Madras).—Educ. at Leamington Coll., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 7th Dec., 1893, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. commr. and dist. mag., Feb., 1898; spcl. settlmt. officer, May, 1901.

COUPER, SIR GEORGE E. W., Bart., K.C.S.I., C.B., C.I.E., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Sandhurst, and served in H.M.'s 15th regt. for two years before joining Haileybury; apptd. to the Bengal civil service, 1846; served in Bengal and the Punjab in various grades; under sec., govt. of India, home and fin. dept., 1853; under sec., foreign dept., 1854; sec. to chief commr. of Oudh, 1855; served through the siege of Lucknow, and was present at several engagements during the Mutiny as A.D.C. to Sir H. Lawrence and Sir John Inglis, and afterwards to Sir James Outram; commr. in Oudh, 1858; sec. to govt. of N.W. Prov., 1859; jud. commr. of Oudh., 1863; chief commr. of Oudh, 1874; lieut.-govr. of N.W. Prov., 1877, as well as chief commr. of Oudh; ret'd., 1882.

COUPER, THOMAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Fettes Coll. and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived 1st Dec., 1902, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

COUPLAND, HERBERT, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Ripon Gr. Sch. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 21st Dec., 1895, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. settlmt. officer; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan. 1903.

COURTENAY, ROBERT, B.A., late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 24th Nov., 1874, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; acted as asst. judge at intervals from Dec., 1885; ret'd., Sept., 1890.

COUTTS, WILLIAM STRACHAN, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Dollar and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Dec., 1895, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf'd. to E. Bengal and Assam, as offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct. 1905.

COVENTRY, BERNARD.—Apptd. dir. of the agric. research institute, and principal of the agric. coll. at Pusa, 1st April, 1904.

COVERTON, JAMES GARGRAVE, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (eduen. inspr., Bombay).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. prof'r. of English literature, Elphinstone Coll., 26th May, 1894; and eduen. inspr., Oct., 1898.

COWAN, GEORGE HERBERT, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at King William's Coll., Isle of Man, and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 27th Nov., 1903, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

COWAN, HUGH MONTGOMERIE, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Leys Sch., Cambridge, and St. Andrew's and Edinburgh Univs.; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 6th December, 1904, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

COWAN, SAMUEL HUNTER, Col., I.S.C., late Survey of India.—First commn., lieut. royal artillery, 9th Dec., 1859; arrived, 9th April, 1860; asst. supt., survey dept., Bengal, 19th Jan., 1867; held charge of the Cachar survey, 1878-79; and of the Hugli River survey, 1882-83; dep. supt., April, 1883; ret'd., Oct., 1891.

COWASJI JAMSHEDJI LALKAKA, Khan Bahadur, postal dept., govt. of India (postmr.-genl., Burma).—Entered the service April, 1871; dep. postmr.-gen., 1895; postmr.-genl., Burma, Feb., 1905.

COWIE, CHARLES HENRY, Lieut.-Col., R.E., India Public Works Dept. (dep. manager, N.W. Rly.).—First commn., Feb., 1881; apptd. asst. engr., Nov., 1883, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., Aug., 1893; dep. manager, E. Ben. Rly., Nov., 1897; ditto, N.W. Rly., Dec., 1899; served with Tirah expedition, Sept. to Dec., 1897; and in South Africa, Jan., 1900, to Nov., 1902; again dep. manager, N.W. Rly., Dec., 1902.

COWIE, DONALD WILLIAM GARDEN, M.A., Barr. (1894), Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at the Charterhouse and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 30th Nov., 1886, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst. collr., Jan., 1892; registrar, high court (appellate side), Nov., 1895; sub. collr. and joint mag., Feb., 1898; principal asst. to dist.

mag. and agent to govt., Vizagapatam, Nov., 1900; dist. and sessions judge, Aug., 1902; collr. and mag., Dec., 1903.

COWIE, THOMAS RENN, Col., I.S.C., late Oudh Commn.—Arrived as ensign, 12th Aug., 1860; employed under the mil. dept. till Aug., 1869, from which date he served in Oudh as asst. commr., sub-judge, and dep. commr.; cantonmt. mag., Feb., 1881; dep. commr., Oct. 1888; district and sessions judge, April, 1891; vacated, March, 1898.

COWLEY, FRANCIS WILLIAM RICE, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1863, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; employed in public works dept., March, 1873; dep. commr., wards' estate, Patna, June, 1875; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1878; district and sessions judge, April, 1879; jud. commr., Chota Nagpur, Sept., 1887; ret'd., Oct., 1897.

COX, ALEXANDER RANKEN, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Clifton and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived 31st Dec., 1902, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

COX, ARTHUR FREDERICK, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (comptr. and auditor-gen.).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch. and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 3rd Nov., 1871, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; on special duty in connection with visit of H.R.H. the Prince of Wales, Nov., 1875; from Sept., 1882, served in the finl. dept.; asst. comptr.-gen., July, 1883; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, April, 1885; sub-collr. and mag., Nov., 1887; district and sessions judge, April, 1889; acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Dec., 1889; offg. comptr. and auditor-gen., March, 1894; acctnt.-gen., Bengal, April, 1895; comptr. and auditor-gen., Dec., 1898; K.-i-H. medal, Jan., 1901; C.S.I., Nov., 1901; compiled 'N.' Arcot District Manual.'

COX, GEORGE WILLIAM STIRLING, Bengal Police Dept. (dist. supt. of police).—Joined the service, 19th Oct., 1876, as offg. asst. supt. of police; confd. May, 1880; dist. supt., Nov., 1887.

COX, HENRY WILLIAM HOLMES, Col., I.S.C., late Police Dept., Madras.—Ensign, Madras infantry, Nov., 1857; arrived, 26th Dec., 1857; served in the Madras police from April, 1861; supt., April, 1873; asst. inspr.-gen., Dec., 1881; presidency mag., Oct., 1883; pol. officer in charge of ex-King Thebaw of Burma, Dec., 1885, to April, 1886; dep. inspr.-gen. of police, Aug., 1886; commr. of police, Madras city, Dec., 1891; vacated, Dec., 1895.

COX, HOMERSHAM, M.A., late fellow of Trin. Coll., Cambridge, Indian Educl. Service (prof. of mathematics, Muir Central Coll.).—Apptd. 5th March, 1891.

COX, JOHN HUGH, Indian C.S. (joint sec., board of rev., United Prov.).—Educ. at Clifton Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 21st Nov., 1890, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., April, 1896; asst. commr., Oct., 1899; dep. commr., Dec., 1903; joint sec., board of rev., Oct., 1904.

COX, PERCY ZACHARIAH, C.I.E., Major, Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (offg. resdt., Persian Gulf).—First commn., 6th Feb., 1884; military employ to June, 1893; first

asst. pol. resdt., Aden, Aug., 1893; transf. to Baroda, Nov., 1895; first asst. to resdt., Baroda, Feb., 1897; pol. agent, Maskat, Oct., 1899; C.I.E., June, 1902; offg. resdt., Persian Gulf, April, 1904.

COXE, HENRY REYNELL HOLLED, Indian C.S. (supt. and remembrancer, E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 1st Nov., 1884, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; employed in Burma, 1887-91, as asst. commr. and under sec. to chief commr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1893; dist. and sess. judge, April, 1896; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905, as supt. and remembrancer, and sec. to legisl. council.

COXON, STANLEY WILLIAM (dep. commr., Central Prov.).—Joined the service, 12th Nov., 1888, as asst. dist. supt. of police, Burma; transf. to Central Prov. as asst. commr., Feb., 1893; K.-i.-H. medal, 2nd class, Jan., 1901; dep. commr., March, 1901.

CRADDOCK, REGINALD HENRY, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Reading Gr. Sch., Wellington Coll., and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 1st Dec., 1884, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr., asst. to commr. of settlts. and agric., and settl. officer; commr. of excise, April, 1893; dep. commr., June, 1893; chief sec. to chief commr., April, 1900; offg. commr., April, 1901; commr., March, 1902; C.S.I., Jan., 1903.

CRAIK, HENRY DUFFIELD, B.A., Indian C.S. (regr., chief court, Punjab).—Educ. at Eton Coll., Pembroke Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived 21st Nov., 1899, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; regr. of the chief court, March, 1905.

CRAMPTON, ARTHUR CECIL, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept. — Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1874, and employed in the Central Prov. and the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., May, 1883; transf. to Burma, Sept., 1890; ret'd., June, 1905.

CRANBROOK (EARL OF), RIGHT HON. GATHORNE GATHORNE-HARDY, G.C.S.I., P.C.—Born, 1814; educ. at Shrewsbury Sch., and at Oriel Coll., Oxford; called to the bar at the Inner Temple in 1840, and practised as a barrister for several years; contested Bradford unsuccessfully in 1847; sat in the House of Commons for Leominster from 1856 to 1865, when he was returned for Oxford; under sec. of state for the home dept., 1858; prest. of the poor law board, July, 1866; sec. of state for the home dept., 1867-68; sec. of state for war, Feb., 1874; created Viscount Cranbrook, May, 1878; sec. of state for India, March, 1878, to May, 1880; lord prest. of council, 1885-86, and again, 1886-92; created Earl of Cranbrook and Baron Medway, Sept., 1892.

CRASTER, GEORGE AYTON, Major-Gen., R.E., late Public Works Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—First commn. as lieut., 11th Dec., 1849; from Nov., 1853, served as asst. and exec. engr. in Assam, Oudh, and the Punjab; transf. to the N.W. Prov., March, 1860; offg. suptg. engr., and dep. sec. to govt., N.W. Prov., public works dept., April, 1872; suptg. engr., April, 1872; ret'd., Jan., 1888.

CRÄSTER, SHAFTO LONGFIELD, Major, R.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—First

commn., 22nd Feb., 1882; asst. engr., state rlys., Feb., 1885; mily. works dept., 1887 to 1891; exec. eng., Aug., 1893; field service, N.W. Frontier, 1897, and China, 1901.

CRAWFORD, CLEMENT EDWARD, Indian C.S. (joint mag., United Prov.).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 10th Nov., 1892, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., April, 1897; settmt. offr., Nov., 1900, to May, 1905.

CRAWFORD, DIROM GREY, M.B., Lt.-Col., Ind. Med. Service (civil surgn., Hooghly).—First commn., 1st Oct., 1881; in military employ till April, 1884, since which date has held appointments in Med. Coll. Hosp., Calcutta, and civil surgeries at various places; civil surgn., Hooghly, Sept., 1900; author of 'Notes on the History of the Bengal Medical Service.'

CRAWFORD, FRANCIS COLOMB, Police Dept., Berar (supt. of rly. police, Nizam's dominions).—Joined the service 28th June, 1883, as offg. asst. supt. of police; confd., April, 1886; supt. of rly. police, Nizam's dominions, Nov., 1890; dist. supt. and ditto, April, 1896.

CRAWFORD, FREDERICK JAMES, M.D., Major, Ind. Med. Service (profr., med. coll., Madras).—First commn., 31st March, 1887; military duty till 1889; dist. surg., Ganjam, May, 1890; surgn., genl. hosp., and profr., med. coll., Madras, Aug., 1890; surgn., 3rd dist., Sept., 1900; ditto, and physn., genl. hosp., and profr., med. coll., Madras, July, 1901; profr., med. coll., and 2nd surgn., genl. hosp., with port and marine duties, April, 1905.

CRAWFORD, GEORGE ADAM, Col., late R.A.—First commn., Feb. 1855; served in Afghan War, 1879-80, (twice mentioned in despatches, medal); retired, Dec., 1886; India Office member of Committee for valuation of military equipment, July, 1897, to July, 1905.

CRAWFORD, JAMES ADAIR, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Rugby; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 17th Sept., 1878, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; from Feb., 1881, served in the pol. dept., and acted as asst. to the agent for Rajputana, boundary settl. officer in Mewar, and asst. to the rest. at Hyderabad; attaché, foreign dept., May, 1882; in charge of office of press commr., 1882-83; asst. commr., Ajmer, April, 1884; asst. sec., foreign dept., Jan., 1885; pol. agent, Quetta and Pishin, Nov., 1885; junr. under sec. to govt., foreign dept., March, 1888; acting first asst. and sec. for Berar to rest. at Hyderabad, May, 1890; under sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., April, 1892; on special duty in connection with the revision of Aitchison's 'Treaties,' Oct., 1892; and again with the Archduke of Austria during his tour in India, 1892-93; offg. pol. rest., Persian Gulf, July-Dec., 1893; rest. and rev. commr., Baluchistan, March, 1895; commr., Berar, April, 1899; offg. resdt., Mysore, and chief commr., Coorg, June, 1899, and May, 1901; ret'd. Aug., 1903.

CRAWFORD, RICHMOND IRVINE, C.I.E., Col., I.S.C., late Sind Commn.—First commn., Oct., 1857; arrived, 6th Nov., 1857; served in the pursuit of the Gwalior rebels, &c., in 1858 (medal and clasp for Central India); from Sept., 1864, served in Sind as dep. collr. and mag., and supt. of police; pol. supt. of Thar and Parkar, May, 1875; dep. commr., Thar and Parkar, Aug.,

1881; collr. and mag., Shikarpur, June, 1885; and subsequently at Karachi; has acted for various periods as commr. in Sind; C.I.E., Jan., 1896; returned to Europe, Mar., 1896.

CRAWFORD, WILLIAM MONOD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Roy. Univ., Ireland, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 28th Dec., 1895, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.

CRAWFURD, JAMES, B.A., Barr. (1868), late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at the Coll. Sch. and Univ. of Glasgow, and Dublin Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1868, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. under sec., govt. of India, home dept., Feb., 1875; under sec., govt. of Bengal, July, 1876; first inspr. of registrn. offices, Dec., 1877; registrar, high court, Nov., 1878; district and sessions judge, March, 1885; ret'd., Nov., 1894.

CRAWLEY, CHARLES EDWARD, late 1st lnl. Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the dept., and was attached to the acctnt.-gen.'s office, Bombay, 25th Feb., 1874; asst. commr., paper currency, Bombay, March, 1878; asst. acctnt.-gen., Calcutta, Oct., 1883; Bombay, Aug., 1884; asst. compr., India treasuries, Jan., 1889; asst. acctnt.-gen. and examr. of local accts., Bengal, March, 1889; on foreign service, as compr.-gen. to the Nizam's govt., Aug., 1893; compr., Indian Treasuries, Sept., 1901; offg. acctnt.-gen., Madras, April, 1902; ret'd., June, 1905.

CRAWLEY, GEORGE BURRIDGE, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army, Jud. Dept., Burma (cantonmt. mag., Rangoon).—First commn., 30th Jan., 1878; entered civil employ as cantonmt. mag., July, 1885.

CRAWLEY-BOEVEY, ARTHUR WILLIAM, M.A., B.L., late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Rugby, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 21st Nov., 1868, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and special asst. to pol. agent; from May, 1873, to Dec., 1874, served in Baroda as acting asst. rest; in special charge of Sachin state, Dec., 1874; on special duty at Baroda, Feb., 1875; on famine duty at Kaladgi, Aug., 1877; talukdari settlt. officer, Gujarat, June, 1879; special officer for the settlt. of claims to giras and watans in Baroda territory, 1883 and 1885; acting chief presidency mag. and rev. judge, Bombay, May, 1887; junr. collr. and mag., May, 1889; senr. collr. and mag., Nov., 1891; ret'd., Aug., 1893.

CREAGH, SIR O'MOORE, V.C., K.C.B., Lieut.-Genl., Indian Army.—First commn., Oct., 1866; joined the Indian Staff Corps, Feb., 1870; served in the Afghan campaigns of 1878-80 (medal and V.C.); apptd. brigadier-genl. commanding, Aden district, Feb., 1899; also pol. resdt. and collr. of stamp rev. at Aden; on depn. to China from July, 1900; K.C.B., June, 1903; cmdg. 5th divn. (Western Army Corps), Nov., 1904.

CREGEEN, ARCHIBALD CRELLIN, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept., June, 1859, as asst. engr., Central Prov.; transf. to Punjab, Feb., 1861; exec. engr., April, 1866; railway dept., Central India, Holkar state railway, Oct., 1870; engr.-in-chief, provl. light railway survey extensions, Lucknow, June, 1878; Nimach-Nasirabad survey, Nov., 1878; W. Rajputana state railway, Aug., 1879; Vizagapatam-Raipur railway survey, Oct., 1882;

construction establt., Nagpur-Bengal railway, Oct., 1884; agent and chief engr., Indian Midland railway, Feb., 1886; ret'd., Feb., 1888.

CRERAR, JAMES, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Geo. Watson's Coll. Edinburgh, Edin. Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived 15th Nov., 1901, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

CRESSWELL, CHARLES ARTHUR, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Burma Commn.—First commn., royal artillery, Jan., 1866; arrived, 12th Jan., 1867; joined the Burma commn., Feb., 1871, as offg. asst. commr. and cantonmt. mag. at Tounghoo; personal asst. to chief commr., Dec., 1875; boundary officer, Sandoway, March, 1881; prest., Sandoway town committee, April, 1884; deputy commr., March, 1885; offd. as prest. Rangoon municipality, July, 1895, addl. sess. judge, Irrawadi divn., April, 1897; ret'd., June, 1898.

CRICHTON, RICHMOND TREVOR, Major, Indian Army (supt. of provincial surveys, Bengal).—First commn., 23rd Aug., 1884; joined survey dept. as asst. supt., Dec., 1889; dep. supt., March, 1895; supt. of settlement surveys in Bengal, Oct., 1895; supt. of provincial surveys, Bengal, Dec., 1900.

CRICKMAY, JOSEPH GEORGE, India Office (2nd class clerk, registry and record dept.).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 17th Jan., 1902.

CRIMMIN, JOHN, V.C., C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Bombay Medl. Dept. (health officer to port of Bombay).—First commn., 30th Sept., 1882; on military duty till 1889; civil surgn., Shikarpur, Sept., 1889; ditto, Ratnajiri, Dec., 1892, health officer to the port of Bombay, May, 1900; C.I.E., Jan., 1901.

CROCKETT, WILLIAM GORDON, India Office (second class clerk, store dept.).—Apptd. clerk 2nd class, store dept., 23rd Nov., 1900.

CROFT, SIR ALFRED WOODLEY, K.C.I.E., M.A., late Educl. Dept., Bengal.—Apptd., 8th Dec., 1865; served as profir., presidency coll., principal, Dacca coll., and inspr. of schools; dir. of public instrn., from Aug., 1878; prest. of the commn. to draw up a code for European schools, Oct., 1881; member of the edun. commn., 1882-83; C.I.E., May, 1884; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1887, member of the legisl. council of Bengal, Feb., 1887; on special duty for the preparation of a report on edun. in British India, 1887; fellow-member of the syndicate and prest. of the faculty of arts of Calcutta univ.; vice-chancellor, Dec., 1893; ret'd., Feb., 1897.

CROFTON, CHARLES D'ARCY, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Wellington Coll. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 1st Dec., 1903, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

CROFTON, CHARLES STANHOPE FOSTER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Harrow and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 19th Nov., 1897, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

CROFTON, JAMES, Lieut.-Gen., late R.E.—Second lieut., Bengal engrs., 9th Dec., 1844; joined the public works dept., Nov., 1847; asst. engr., Punjab circle, Dec., 1849; exec. engr., Punjab irrign., 1858; suptg. engr., Jan., 1862; supt., Sutlej canal surveys; chief engr.,

irrign., and under sec. to govt., Punjab, Sept., 1864; inspr.-gen. of irrign. and dep. sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., April, 1874, to Jan., 1882, when he ret'd. from the service.

CROFTS, AYLMER MARTIN, C.I.E., M.D., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (agency surgn., Kotah and Jhalawar).—First commn., 31st March, 1877; on mil. duty till Nov., 1885 (Afghan war, 1878-30; Egyptian campaign, 1882); apptd. residency surgn., Gwalior, Nov. 1886; agency surgn., Kotah and Jhalawar, June, 1905.

CROFTS, JAMES, M.D., Lieut.-Col., late I.M.S.—First commn., Feb., 1877; in mil. employ till Feb., 1883, since when he has held appts. as med. off'r. in the pol. agencies of Jhalawar and Kotah, Ajmer, Jhalrapatan and Kotah, and Gwalior; resdcy. surgn. in W. Rajputana states, Nov., 1900; ret'd., July, 1905.

CROLE, CHARLES STEWART, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Edinburgh High Sch. and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 9th Dec., 1853, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and sub-collr. and joint mag.; district and sessions judge, Kistna, June, 1881; collr. and mag., Nov., 1882; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1886; member of board of rev., Dec., 1890; and of legisl. council, Dec., 1893; C.I.E., Jan., 1898; ret'd., 1898; compiled the 'Chingleput District Manual.'

CROLY, ARTHUR ENGLAND JOHNSON, F.R.C.S., Lieut.-Col., Royal Army Med. Corps.—First commn., 6th Mar., 1880; med. officer to the govr. of Bombay, Sept., 1892 to 1903.

CROMBIE, ALEXANDER, C.B., M.D., Brigade Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Bengal Med. Service.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872; offg. rest. surgn., Calcutta med. coll. hosp., Jan., 1873; prof'r., med. coll., in addn., Oct., 1873; transf'd. to Burma, Feb., 1875, and apptd. junr. civil surgn., Rangoon, and health officer to the port; from April, 1877, served in Bengal as civil surgn.; also at Simla as joint med. officer, 1880-82; civil surgn., Dacca, with charge of the jail, &c., March, 1882; surgn. supt., presidency gen. hosp., Calcutta, June, 1889; ret'd., April, 1898; apptd. acting member, India Office med. board, June, 1900; confd. Aug. 1900; C.B., Aug., 1902; ret'd. from med. board, Aug., 1903.

CROMER (EARL OF), RIGHT HON. EVELYN BARING, G.C.B., G.C.M.G., K.C.S.I., C.I.E.—Lieut., royal artillery, June, 1858; capt. in 1870, and major in 1876; A.D.C. to Sir Henry Storks, in Ionian Islands, in 1861, and sec. to him in 1865, during the enquiry into the outbreak in Jamaica; private sec. to the govr.-gen. of India (the Earl of Northbrook), 1872-76; commr. of the Egyptian public debt, 1877-79; comptr.-gen. in Egypt, Sept., 1879; finl. member of council of govr.-gen. of India, June, 1880; created a Baron of the United Kingdom, 1882; agent and consul-gen. in Egypt, and a minister plenipotentiary in the diplomatic service, Aug., 1883; finl. asst. at the conference in London on Egyptian finance, 1884; G.C.B., Jan., 1895; created a Viscount, Dec., 1898; has 1st class of Medjidie; created a privy councillor, 1899; created an earl, Aug. 1901.

CROMMELIN, CHARLES AINSLIE RUSSELL, India Public Works Dept. (gen. traffic manager, B. B. and C. I. Rly.).—Joined the service, 14th July, 1877, as accont. in the office of the examr. mil. works; asst. traffic supt., Feb., 1881; lent to Bombay, Baroda and C.I. rly. co., Nov., 1885.

CRONIN, JOHN JOSEPH, Major, Indian Army (dep. commr., Burma).—First commn., Aug., 1880; employed in Burma as asst. commr., July, 1886; dep. commr., Dec., 1895.

CROOKE, WILLIAM, B.A., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Erasmus Smith's Gr. Sch., Tipperary, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 2nd Nov., 1871, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; dep. commr., July, 1889; mag. and collr., Feb., 1890; ret'd., Aug., 1896; author of a rural and agricl. glossary for the N.W. Prov. and Oudh, an 'Ethnographical Handbook for the N.W. Prov. and Oudh,' 'Popular Religion and Folklore of N. India,' and 'Gazetteer of Jalesar.'

CROOKE-LAWLESS, WARREN ROLAND, M.D., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col. 1st Coldstream Guards.—Served in the S. African War, 1899-1902; apptd. surgn. to the Viceroy and govr.-genl. of India (Lord Minto), 18th Nov., 1905.

CROSS, GEORGE HAROLD, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Malvern Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1897, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; resigned, March, 1904.

CROSS (VISCOUNT), RIGHT HON. RICHARD ASHETON, P.C., G.C.B., G.C.S.I.—Born, 30th May, 1823; educ. at Rugby Sch., and at Trinity Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar at the Inner Temple, 1849, and for several years went the northern circuit; M.P. for Preston, 1857-62, and S.W. Lancashire, 1868-86; sec. of state for the home dept., 1874-80; bencher of the Inner Temple, 1876; hon. LL.D. of Cambridge, Oct., 1878; G.C.B. in 1880; again sec. of state for the home dept., June, 1885, and was apptd. prest. of the royal commn. on the educn. acts; in Lord Salisbury's second administration, Aug., 1886, was apptd. sec. of state for India, and raised to the peerage under the title of Viscount Cross; G.C.S.I., Aug., 1892; lord privy seal in Lord Salisbury's third administration, July, 1895, to Nov., 1900; author of two legal works.

CROTHWAITE, SIR CHARLES HAUKE TODD, K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1857; arrived, 20th Dec., 1857, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and settlt. officer; from April, 1877, served in the Central Prov. as commr. of the Nagpur and Jabalpur divns., and was apptd. jud. commr., Central Prov. Aug., 1881, and addl. member of the govr.-gen.'s council, Dec., 1881; acting chief commr., British Burma, March, 1883, to April, 1884; chief commr., Central Prov., Jan., 1885, member of the public service commn., Dec., 1886; C.S.I., Feb., 1887; chief commr. of Burma, March, 1887; K.C.S.I., May, 1888; member of council of govr.-gen., Oct., 1890, to Feb., 1891, and again from April, 1892; lieut.-govr., N.W. Prov., and chief commr., Oudh, Nov., 1892, to Jan., 1895; ret'd. from Indian C.S., 1895; member of council of India, March, 1895; vice-prest., 1897-8; ret'd. from council of India, March, 1905; author of 'Notes on the N.W. Prov.' and 'Land Rev. Law.'

CROTHWAITE, HUGH STUART, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United

Prov.)—Educ. at Rugby Sch. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.

CROSTHWAITER, SIR ROBERT JOSEPH, K.C.S.I., B.A., Barr. (1868), late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 30th Oct., 1863, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr. and joint mag. and dep. collr.; asst. supt., sub-judge, and registrar of Dehra Dun, Dec., 1873; small cause court judge, Allahabad, March, 1875; registrar of the high court, N.W. Prov., Jan., 1878; acting jud. commr., British Burma, Dec., 1878; district and sessions judge, Allahabad, May, 1881; addl. sec. to govt. of India, legisl. dept., Jan., 1883; jud. commr. of the Central Prov., Jan., 1885; member of viceroy's legisl. council, June, 1889; offg. chief commr., Central Prov., July-Oct., 1889; rest. and agent to the govr.-gen., Central India, Jan., 1891; C.S.I., May, 1890; agent to the govr.-gen., Rajputana, March, 1895; rest. and chief commr., Ajmere, Oct., 1895; K.C.S.I., June, 1897; ret'd., Jan., 1898; author of 'The N.W. Prov. Rev. Law' (legal part).

CROW, WILLIAM, Store Dept., India Office (clerk for railway stores).—Apptd. clerk for railway stores, 23rd Oct., 1883.

CROWDY, JOHN HENRY, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Mil. Works Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn. as lieut., Dec., 1857; posted to Indian estabt., Sept., 1870; from Dec., 1870, served as exec. engr. in the Punjab; transfld. to mil. works, 1872, and served in the Punjab, the N.W. Prov., and Bengal; suptg. engr., March, 1886; ret'd., March, 1890.

CROWE, WILLIAM HENRY, Barr. (1875), late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Godolphin Gr. Sch., and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 26th Oct., 1867, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. prof. at the Deccan coll., Poona, acting asst. agent for sardars in the Deccan, and asst. judge and sessions judge; acting special judge under the Deccan agriculturists' relief act, March, 1882; and sessions judge, Aug., 1883; also agent for sardars in the Deccan, April, 1892; acted as jud. commr. in Sind, Oct.-Dec., 1894; addl. member of Bombay legisl. council, July, 1893, and July, 1896; judge, high court, June, 1900; ret'd., July, 1904.

CROZIER, ROBERT GEORGE, late Store Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Brighton Coll.; temporary clerk, 30th Nov., 1865; junr. clerk, Jan., 1867; senr. clerk, March, 1887; dep. dir.-gen. of stores, Aug., 1865; ret'd., June, 1900.

CRUICKSHANK, ALEXANDER WALMESLEY, C.S.I., Barr. (1883), Indian C.S. (commr., United Prov.).—Called to the bar at the Middle Temple; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 11th Nov., 1872, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; junr. sec., board of rev., Jan., 1878; under sec. to govt., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Sept., 1886; settlt. officer, Oct., 1886; dep. commr., Nov. 1890; mag. and collr., Nov. 1891; commr., Allahabad, April, 1897; commr., Rohilkhand divn., Feb., 1898; C.S.I., Jan., 1898; member, legisl. council, March, 1901, and March, 1903; addl. member of viceroy's legisl. council, Oct., 1902, and Jan., 1903; author of the Gorakhpur settmt. report.

CRUICKSHANK, JOHN DONALDSON, Col., R.E., late Bombay Public Works Dept.—First commn., 9th Dec., 1859; joined the dept. as proby. asst. engr., July, 1862, and was employed on irrign. works, &c., in Sind; exec. engr., Dec., 1865; suptg. engr., Nov., 1891; chief engr., Feb., 1894; acting sec. to govt. of Bombay Public Works Dept., May-Nov., 1893; ret'd., June, 1896.

CRUMP, HENRY ASHBROOKE, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 10th Nov., 1885, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; commr. of excise, June, 1893; dep. commr., Aug., 1894; second sec. to chief commr., Nov., 1894; chief sec. to ditto, April, 1901, to May, 1902.

CRUMP, LESLIE MAURICE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov.).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., Liverpool, and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 5th Dec. 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. asst. to the rest., Hyderabad, Dec., 1900; asst. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Sept., 1903; offg. asst. sec., foreign dept., Oct., 1904; offg. pol. agent, Wana, May, 1905.

CRUMP, LOUIS CHARLES, Indian C.S. (judge and sess. judge, Bombay).—Educ. under private tuition and at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 10th Dec., 1890, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., Mar., 1897; asst. judge, Feb., 1898; under sec. to govt. pol., judl., and legisl. depts., March, 1899 to Jan., 1900; 1st asst., April, 1902; judge and sess. judge, Sept., 1904.

CUBITT, WILLIAM MARTIN, Major, Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (pol. agent, Baghelkhand).—First commn., Aug., 1884, joined the political dept., Sept., 1888; dep. commr. and pol. agent, Thal Chotiali, May, 1899; pol. agent, Malwa, Oct., 1899; on depts. as supt. of Dholpur State from May to Dec., 1903; pol. agent, Haraoti and Tonk, Jan., 1904; ditto, Baghelkhand, May, 1904.

CULLEN, PETER, M.D., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Med. Dept., Central Prov.—Joined the Bengal med. service, July, 1859; arrived, 24th Jan., 1860, and was employed under the mil. dept., including service in the China war; from Feb., 1862, served in the Central Prov. as civil surgn. and supt. of jail; also supt. of cotton affairs, May, 1865; ret'd., June, 1892.

CULLIS, HENRY THOREAU, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 29th Nov., 1904, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

CUMINE, ALEXANDER, C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Glenalmond; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 28th Oct., 1874, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and railway mag.; 2nd asst. collr., May, 1887; 1st asst. collr., Nov., 1890; junr. collr. and mag., Kaira, and pol. agent, Cambay, May, 1894; senr. collr., March, 1897; acting commr. of customs, &c., April, 1898, and plague commr., Bombay, June, 1898; commr., southern div., April, 1901; addl., member, govr.'s legisl. council, April, 1901; commr. in Sind, April, 1902; ret'd., June, 1904; C.S.I., June, 1904.

CUMING, ARTHUR HERBERT, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ.

at Westminster Sch., London Univ., and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1895, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Assam, Jan., 1899.

CUMING, JOSEPH HENRY, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Prov.).—Educ. at Westminster, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1891, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; joint mag., Jan., 1897; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1904.

CUMMING, ALEXANDER ROBERTSON, Indian C.S. (sec. to commrs. of land rev., Madras).—Educ. at the Aberdeen Gr. Sch., Aberdeen Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 18th Dec., 1892, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; senr. asst., Jan., 1899; sec. to commrs. of land rev., Nov., 1902.

CUMMING, CHARLES LENNOX BRUCE, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 29th Nov., 1867, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; principal asst., Ganjam, Nov., 1883; ret'd., March, 1888.

CUMMING, JOHN ARTHUR, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Aberdeen Gr. Sch., Aberdeen Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 16th Dec., 1887, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; head asst., Dec., 1894; senr. asst., July, 1897; sub-collr. and joint mag., Jan., 1899; collr., dist. mag. and govt. agent, Godavari, Dec., 1903.

CUMMING, JOHN GHEST, M.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at High Sch., Glasgow, Glasgow Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 3rd Nov., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., offg. under sec. to govt., and settl. officer; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1896; mag. and collr., June, 1900.

CUMMING, WILLIAM GORDON, C.I.E., Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., Madras engrs., 1860; arrived, 7th Oct., 1862; joined the public works dept., Feb., 1864, as asst. engr., Port Blair; exec. engr., April, 1866; transf'd. to Madras, Aug., 1868; Haidarabad, May, 1870; from Feb., 1874, served in British Burma; suptg. engr., Jan., 1884; offg. sec. to govt.-gen.'s agent, Rajputana, Oct., 1884; sec. to chief commr., Burma, public works dept., May, 1888; rank of chief engr., May, 1888; C.I.E., Jan., 1893; ret'd., Aug., 1895.

CUNINGHAM, CHARLES ALEXANDER, Major-Gen., late Bombay Pol. Dept.—First commn., 20th June, 1858; served in the Afghan war as asst. adjutant-gen. (medal); dep. asst. adjutant-gen., Bombay, 1877-80, and 1881-88; dep. adjutant-gen., Bombay, 1888-92; asst. adjutant-gen., Bengal, Feb., 1893; sec. to govt. of Bombay, mil., &c., depts., Dec., 1892; pol. rest., collr. of stamp rev., &c., Aden, May, 1895, to Feb., 1899; returned to Europe, 1899; to U.S. list, Jan., 1904.

CUNINGHAM, SIR WILLIAM JOHN, K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad., and Clapham Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 21st Nov., 1870, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; from Aug., 1873, served in Mysore as asst. commr. on special duty at Bangalore and Chitaldrug,

addl. sec. in the famine dept., and sec. to the chief commr., gen. dept.; asst. to the rest. in Mysore, and sec. to the chief commr., Coorg, April, 1881; asst. to the rest., Haidarabad, Dec., 1883; under sec., govt. of India, foreign dept., Sept., 1885; dep. sec., May, 1890; sec., April, 1894; also sec. of orders of Star of India and Indian Empire, May, 1894; C.S.I., Jan., 1894; K.C.S.I., June, 1897; retired, Jan., 1901.

CUNNINGHAM, SIR ALEXANDER FREDERICK DOUGLAS, K.C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Kensington Gr.
