HALLWARD, NORMAN LESLIE, M.A., Indian Educ. Service. (dir. of pub. instrn., E. Bengal and Assam).—Apptd. prof'r., Dacca Coll., 9th Feb., 1890; prof'r., presy. coll., Calcutta, Oct., 1897, and Dec., 1901; offg. in charge of records of govt. of India, June, 1904; dir. of pub. instrn., Assam, May, 1905.

HAM, WILLIAM JAMES, late Postal Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the service, and was attached to the sea post office, Bombay, 6th May, 1868; supernumerary inspr., Oct., 1877; dep. postmr.-gen., Central India, May, 1880, and at Calcutta, attached to the railway mail service, April, 1882; asst. dir.-gen., June, 1887; inspr.-gen., railway mail service, Sept., 1890; has acted as postmr.-gen. at Lucknow, Calcutta, Madras, N.W. Prov. and Oudh; confd. as postmr.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, April, 1897; ret'd., April, 1901.

HAMBLIN, RICHARD EBB, M.A., Indian C.S. (commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., and Christ Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 4th Jan., 1878, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., June, 1887; joint mag., March, 1890; dep. commr., April, 1893; mag. and collr., Sept., 1894; commr., March, 1899.

HAMID MOIZUDIN ABDUL ALI, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Sind).—Educ. at St. Xavier's High Sch., Bombay, Bombay Univ., and Emanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1904, and served in Sind as asst. collr. and mag.

HAMILTON, FRANK SUMNER, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 10th Dec., 1885, and served as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1892; mag. and collr., Oct., 1895; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1897.

HAMILTON, RIGHT HON. LORD GEORGE FRANCIS, G.C.S.I.—Born Dec., 1845; educ. at Harrow; ensign, rifle brigade, 1864; transfld. to the Coldstream guards, 1868; was elected member for Middlesex, Dec., 1868, and represented that county in parliament until the election which followed the reform bill of 1885, when he was returned for the Ealing divn.; under sec. of state for India, Feb., 1874; vice-prest. of the committee of council on educen., April, 1878; first lord of the admiralty in the Marquis of Salisbury's first and second administrations, 1885-86, and 1885-92; chairman, London school board, 1894-95; sec. of state for India in Lord Salisbury's third administration, July, 1895, and fourth administration, Nov., 1900; G.C.S.I., Jan., 1903; resigned, Oct., 1903; chairman of royal commn. on the working of the poor laws, Nov., 1905.

HAMILTON, JOHN MONTGOMERY, India Office (second class clerk, store dept.).—Educ. at Ayr Acad.; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, store dept., 18th Nov., 1901.

HAMILTON, RICHARD ERRIS, late Finl. Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the dept., 1st Aug., 1868; served as asst. acctnt.-gen., N.W. Prov., and British Burma, asst. comptr.-gen., and asst. commr. of paper currency; offg. asst. sec., govt. of India, finl. dept., March, 1882; comptr. and dep. commr. of paper currency, Central Prov., Jan., 1883; comptr., India treasuries, Nov., 1891; acctnt.-gen., Punjab, April, 1894; acctnt.-gen., Bengal, March, 1896; acctnt.-gen., Burma, Nov., 1898; ret'd., Sept., 1904.

HAMILTON, ROBERT CLIFTON, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp. and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1801; arrived, 1st Dec., 1893, and served in Assam as asst. commr. and subdivl. officer; asst. mag. and collr., July, 1899; joint mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1900; asst. collr. on famine duty, Belgaum, April, 1901; author of a grammar of Daffa language.

HAMILTON, THOMAS SHARP, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Old Trafford Sch., Manchester, Chester Coll., and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 6th Nov., 1869, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. judge, Nov., 1887; judge and sessions judge, April, 1890; ret'd., Jan., 1897.

HAMILTON, WILLIAM ROBARTS, Barr. (1890), late Police Dept., Bombay.—Joined the service, 1st Feb., 1869; asst. to the rev. and police commr., Oct., 1869; dep. collr., May, 1874; city mag., Sept., 1874; Huzur dep. commr., July, 1876; presidency mag., Jan., 1888 to 1899; ret'd. 16th Aug., 1899; author of a police manual, and works on local self-govt. and village customs.

HAMILTON, WILLIAM STIRLING, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Winchester, and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1893, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; junr. sec. to finl. commr., Punjab, April, 1897; senr. sec., April, 1898, to Nov., 1900; dep. commr., March, 1904.

HAMMICK, MURRAY, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (sec. to govt., rev. dept., Madras).—Educ. at the Charterhouse; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 18th Dec., 1877, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and in Coorg as asst. commr. and mag.; from Jan., 1883, served in the Madras secretariat as asst. sec., jud. and legisl. depts., and acting under sec. in the rev. dept.; head asst., S. Kanara, April, 1885; acting postmr.-gen., Madras, 1886; sec. to the commrs. of land rev., Oct., 1887; collr. and mag., Malabar, Nov., 1893; inspr.-gen. of Madras police, Dec., 1894; C.I.E., Nov., 1901; sec. to govt., local, munipl., educ. and legisl. depts., Dec., 1901; addl. member of legisl. Council, Madras, Nov., 1901, May, 1902, and March, 1903; acting chief sec. to govt., Oct., 1903; sec. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1903; author of the Madras Municipal Manual.

HAMMICK, STEPHEN, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Marlborough, and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 21st Nov., 1868, and served in Bombay as

asst. collr. and mag., and tutor to the Maharaja of Kolhapur; on famine duty, Kaladgi, Sept., 1877; jud. asst. to the pol. agent, Kathiawar, Aug., 1883; judge and sessions judge, Shikarpur, Feb., 1885; member of Deccan agriculturists' relief act commn., 1891-92; retd., June, 1894.

HAMMOND, ALFRED JOHN, late Punjab Police Dept.—Entered the service as asst. dist. supt. of police, July, 1870; dist. supt., July, 1880; dep. inspr. genl., July, 1901; retd., May, 1903.

HAMMOND, EGBERT LAURIE LUCAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (supl., Cooch Behar State).—Educ. at Newton Coll., S. Devon, and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 27th Nov., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; supt., Cooch Behar State, Nov., 1903.

HAMNETT, FREDERICK HARPER, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras).—Educ. at Clifton Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 17th Nov., 1879, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. acctnt.-gen., Madras, March, 1885; acting sub-collr., Godavari, April, 1886; head asst., Nov., 1887; sub-collr. and joint mag., June, 1891; district and sessions judge, Feb., 1895; coll. and mag., Oct., 1899; dist. and sess. judge, Godavari, April, 1901; ditto, Chingleput, Jan., 1905.

HANCE, JOHN EDWARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Cent. Prov.).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 4th Dec., 1900, and served in the Cent. Prov. as asst. commr. and settlmt. offr.

HANCOCK, GEORGE EDWARD, Col., Indian Army, late Pol. Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 13th Nov., 1859, and served under the mil. dept.; from Nov., 1863, served as asst. supt. of rev. survey and assessment, Gujarat; acting supt. of the Rajkot and Limbdi states, and acting asst. rest. at Aden, and at Baroda; asst. gen. supt. for thagi and dakaiti, Oct., 1871; asst. pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, July, 1872; from Oct., 1873, served as supt. of surveys under rajasthanik court, Kathiawar; acting joint administrator of Gondal state, 1880-81, and was on special duty with the Thakur Saheb in Europe, 1883; on special duty to inquire into administration of Rajpipla state, Nov., 1883; third asst. pol. agent, Kathiawar, Feb., 1885, continuing to be supt. of surveys; prest. of the rajasthanik court, Jan., 1886; pol. supt., Savantvadi, May-Oct., 1891; pol. agent, &c., Kolhapur; offd. in Kathiawar, Jan., 1894; and again from March, 1895; returned to Europe, May, 1896.

HANDCOCK, GEORGE FRASER, Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1881; exec. engr., Feb., 1894.

HANDCOCK, WILLIAM ERSKINE FRASER, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1881, and posted to the Punjab; exec. engr., May, 1894; retd., Dec., 1903.

HANDLEY, ALFRED PERCIVAL, Barr. (1872), late Jud. Dept., Bengal.—Joined the service, 14th March, 1890, as offg. chief presidency mag. and judge of the court for trial of pilots, Calcutta, and served until Oct., 1891; from Feb., 1892, acted as presidency mag., and was apptd. judge of Calcutta small cause court, May, 1892; chief

judge of ditto, Oct., 1893; acting judge, high court, May, 1899, March, 1900, and March, 1903; retd., Aug., 1904.

HANDLEY, FRANCIS FREDERICK, Barr. (1875), late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Sydenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 7th Oct., 1872, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; senr. marriage registrar, Calcutta, Dec., 1880; first inspr. of registrn. offices, Dec., 1881; acting inspr.-gen. of registrn., April, 1885; joint mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1885; district and sessions judge, April, 1889; retd., April, 1900.

HANKEY, HERBERT, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Harrow, and Haileybury; apptd. in 1856; served in the Punjab till 1862, and subsequently in the Lower Prov., and was apptd. mag. and collr., Maldah, 1867, and subsequently at Chittagong; supt. of transport during famine of 1874; inspr.-gen. of police, Lower Prov., 1876; retd., 1878.

HANKIN, EDWARD LEWIS, Lieut.-Gen., I.S.C., late Madras Police Dept.—Ensign, Dec., 1847; arrived, 16th June, 1848; served in the public works dept., Madras, from Aug., 1853, to July, 1859; served subsequently in the police dept. as supt. of police; acting mag. of police for town of Madras, March, 1870; joint mag., Utakamand, Nov., 1870; supt. of family payments and pensions, Oct., 1876; sec. to govt., mil. dept., Madras, Dec., 1882; returned to Europe, 1888.

HANKIN, ERNEST HANBURY, M.A. (chemical exammr., United Prov.).—Joined the service, 26th Oct., 1892, as chemical exammr., analyst, and bacteriologist; author of numerous contributions to the scientific press.

HANNAGAN, JOHN SALISBURY, late Jud. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the service, 18th Nov., 1859, and held various non-gazetted appts. till May, 1880, when he was apptd. sub-judge at Lucknow; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1892; served through the Mutiny (medal); retd., 1896.

HANNAN, JOHN, late Madras Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 31st July, 1866, and served as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; suptg. engr., Dec., 1891; offg. joint sec. to govt., public works dept., April-Nov., 1893; retd., Dec., 1900.

HANNAY, ALEXANDER LIDDERDALE, Indian C.S. (regr., high court, Madras).—Educ. at Uppingham, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 5th Dec., 1891, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; regr. of high court of judicature, Feb., 1898.

HANNYNGTON, FRANK, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Trin. Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 30th Jan., 1899, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

HAPPELL, WILLIAM ALEXANDER, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Crosshill Sch., Maybole, Glasgow Univ., and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 25th Nov., 1864, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and sub-collr. and joint mag.; acting commr. of salt rev., Madras, 1881; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1883; collr. and mag., Nov., 1883; retd., Jan., 1892.

HARBHAMJI RAWAJI, Kumar Shri, Berar Commn. (dep. commr.).—Joined the ser-

vice, 29th Nov., 1886; asst. commr., July, 1890; services lent to Bhartpur State, March, 1895, and to Mewar State, Oct., 1898, to April, 1901; dep. commr., Dec., 1896.

HARCOURT, ALFRED FREDERICK POLLOCK, Col., I.S.C., late Punjab Comm.—Arrived, 15th Oct., 1855; from Sept., 1862, served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; jud. asst., March, 1877; dep. commr., April, 1877; ret'd., March, 1889; author of 'Down by the Drawl,' 'The Royal Umbrella,' and 'Kulu, Lahoul, and Spitti.'

HARCOURT, HENRY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1897, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; transfld. to N.W. Frontier Prov. as district judge, April to Dec., 1902.

HARDIE, ROBERT, late member of the Council of India.—Apptd. to the council of the sec. of state for India on 17th March, 1887, for ten years; vice-prest., 1892-93; was previously engaged in the profession of banking, and held the office of sec. and treasurer of the Bank of Bengal for 15 years; in 1886 was a member of the finance committee apptd. by the govt. of India for the reduction of expenditure.

HARDIMAN, JOHN THOMAS PERCY, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. acctnt.-gen., Burma).—Educ. at Malvern Coll., and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived 10th Dec., 1897, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; transfld. to finl. dept. as asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Aug., 1901; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Feb., 1903; ditto, Burma, Dec., 1903.

HARDING, FRANCIS HENRY, B.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Tonbridge Sch., and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 24th Nov., 1874, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1885; district and sessions judge, May, 1890; ret'd., June, 1900.

HARDING, HERBERT OLIVE DENMAN, Indian C.S. (dist. and sessions judge, Madras).—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 8th Dec., 1886, and served in Madras as asst. to mag. and collr.; head asst. collr., Coimbatore, Feb., 1893; special asst., Malabar, Dec., 1894; acting dist. and sess. judge, July, 1897; sub-collr. and joint mag., March, 1898; dist. and sessions judge, April, 1903.

HARDING, HERBERT WILLIAM, India Office, (asst. to auditor).—Associate of King's Coll., London; writer, India office, 27th April, 1868; junr. examr., India audit office, Nov., 1873; designation changed to junr. clerk, Oct., 1892; senr. clerk, Jan., 1895; asst. to auditor, July, 1905.

HARDY, RICHARD GILLIES, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (member, board of revenue, United Prov.).—Educ. at Witney Gr. Sch., and Victoria Coll., Jersey; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 1st Nov., 1871, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and asst. commr.; mag. and collr., July, 1889; offd. as commr., 1880, 1895, and from April, 1896; commr., Oct., 1896; settl. commr., Lucknow, Oct., 1898; addl. member viceroy's legisl. council, Dec., 1899, and Dec., 1901; C.S.I., Jan., 1900; acting chief sec. to govt., May, 1900, and April,

1901; acting sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept., April, 1902; member, board of revenue, April, 1903; member, legisl. council, June, 1903.

HARE, EDWARD CHRISTIAN, Major, I.M.S. (sany. commr., E. Bengal and Assam).—First commn., 30th Sept., 1886; offd. as civil surgn. in the Central Prov. from June, 1889, to Feb., 1894, when he was transfld. to Assam; confd. as civil surgn., March, 1896; sany. commr., E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

HARE, LANCELOT, C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (member, board of rev., Bengal).—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Hurstpierpoint, and City of London Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 5th Nov., 1873; served in Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; on famine duty, July, 1874; asst. sec. to chief commr. of Assam, March, 1879, and offg. sec., March, 1880; offg. inspr.-gen. of police, Assam, July, 1880; joint mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1884; mag. and collr., June, 1890; offg. commr., April, 1896; C.I.E., Jan., 1900; addl. commr., Mar., 1900; commr., Patna, Nov., 1900; temp. chief sec. to govt., Bengal, Feb., 1903; member, board of rev., Oct., 1904; member, legisl. council, Bengal, Oct., 1904; C.S.I., Jan., 1906.

HARE, ROBERT DOUGLAS, C.I.E., late Hyderabad commn.—Joined the service as extra asst. commr., Chota Nagpur, 14th June, 1869; served on famine duty, May, 1874, and in Berar from Oct., 1875; asst. commr., May, 1878; judge of small cause court, June, 1883; dep. commr., July, 1889; dir. of land records and agric., July, 1890, to Aug., 1891; judl. commr., April, 1903; C.I.E., Jan., 1904; ret'd., July, 1904.

HARE, WILLIAM HARRIS MACVICAR, late Indian Telegraph Dept.—Apptd. 9th Nov., 1870, as asst. supt.; supt., Oct., 1887; ret., Mar., 1898.

HARIGANPATRAO GHARPUREY, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Nagpur High Sch., Calcutta Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1901, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

HARI KISHEN KAUL, Pandit, M.A. (settlt. collr., Punjab).—Probr. under native civil service rules, 17th April, 1890; extra asst. commr., Punjab, April, 1892; junr. sec. to finl. commr., Dec., 1895; settlt. collr., Aug., 1898.

HARINGTON, ARTHUR HENRY, M.A., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 10th Oct., 1862; served in Oudh from Aug., 1863, as asst. commr. and junr. sec. to chief commr.; from Dec., 1866, to Jan., 1868, served under the govt. of India as offg. asst. sec., foreign dept., and offg. under sec., home dept.; offg. supt. of excise and stamps, Oudh, July, 1868; offg. settlt. officer, Hardoi, March, 1870; manager of encumbered estates, Faizabad and Gonda, 1872-75; dep. commr., Aug., 1879; mag. and collr., Saharanpur, Oct., 1883; commr. in Oudh, June, 1889; commr., Allahabad, Nov., 1889; Meerut, May, 1891; ret'd., June, 1896; author of 'Economic Reform in Rural India.'

HARINGTON, HASTINGS NORMAN VICTOR, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (resdcy. surgn. in W. Rajputana States).—First commn., and Oct., 1880; apptd. agency surgn., Alwar, Jan., 1896; civil surgn., Ajmer, May, 1901; offd. as chief med.

offr. in Rajputana, April, 1903, to Oct., 1904, and as admin. med. offr. in Central Prov. from Oct., 1904, to March, 1905; civil surgn., Kurram, March, 1905; resdcy. surgn. in W. Rajputana States, July, 1905.

HARINGTON, HERBERT BARING, M.A., late Oudh Commn.—Arrived, 2nd Feb., 1857, as asst. in telegraph dept.; served during the Mutiny (medal and clasp); from Feb., 1860, served in the Oudh commn. as asst. commr., and asst. settlt. officer; offg. sec. to chief commr., 1869-73; also offg. dir. of public instrn., 1870; dep. commr., July, 1875; district judge, Lucknow, 1870-81; rettd., Nov., 1887.

HARINGTON, HERBERT SEPTIMUS, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (chief engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1875, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., Jan., 1885; engr. in-chief, Mari-Attock railway, April, 1893; suptg. engr., Jan., 1898; chief engr., May, 1904.

HARINGTON, RICHARD, Barr. (1836), (judge, high court, Calcutta).—Apptd. judge, Calcutta high court, July, 1899.

HARMAN, JOHN MACDONALD, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at the Calcutta Civil Engrg. Coll.; apptd., 29th May, 1872, and employed on mil. works in the N.W. Prov.; transf. to state railways, Jan., 1880; exec. engr., May, 1883; offg. engr. in-chief, N.W. railway (frontier section), April, 1895; Burma State railway, April, 1896; suptg. engr., May, 1902; rettd., Sept., 1904.

HARRIDANCE, HENRY, India Office (2nd class clerk, acctnt.-gen.'s. dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 8th March, 1878.

HARRIOTT, GEORGE MOSS, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (supg. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. to the dept., Oct., 1878, and posted to Cent. Provs.; asst. engr., May, 1879; exec. engr., June, 1891; C.I.E., Jan., 1900; 2nd sec. to chief commr., May, 1901, to May, 1902; suptg. engr., Feb., 1905.

HARRIS, EDWARD BRANSON, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Bedford Commercial Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 14th Dec., 1877, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1888; mag. and collr., June, 1892; rettd., April, 1905.

HARRIS, (BARON), RIGHT HON. GEORGE ROBERT CANNING, G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E.—Educ. at Eton, and at Christ Church, Oxford, where he graduated B.A. in 1874; is J. P. and D. L. for Kent, and dep. chairman of the East Kent quarter sessions; under sec. for India in Lord Salisbury's first administration, June, 1885, to Feb., 1886; under sec. of state for war, Aug., 1886; govr. of Bombay, 12th April, 1890, to 18th Feb., 1895; G.C.I.E., May, 1890; G.C.S.I., May, 1895; apptd. a lord-in-waiting to Her late Majesty, July, 1895.

HARRIS, GEORGE FRANCIS ANGELS, Lieut.-Col., Indian Medl. Service (prof., Calcutta Medl. Coll.).—1st commn., 30th Sept., 1878; civil surgeon at various stations, May, 1879—Feb., 1900; prof. of mat. med., Calcutta Medl. Coll., Aug., 1900; also medl. inspr. of emigrants, till Nov., 1902.

HARRIS, GEORGE STEEL TRAVERS, Madras Public Works Dept. (supg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1875, and posted to Burma; exec. engr., Nov., 1881;

lent to Rewa state, Dec., 1883, and to Gwalior, 1886-94; consulting architect, Madras, (suptg. engr.), Oct., 1896.

HARRIS, HENRY NEVILLE, late Bengal Police Dept.—Joined the service, 3rd April, 1866, as asst. supt. of police; employed in Assam in 1870; district supt., Feb., 1874; dep. inspr.-genl., April, 1899; rettd., Aug., 1901.

HARRIS, LEONARD TATHAM, Indian C.S. (commr., Coorg).—Educ. at Falmouth Gr. Sch., Bath Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 10th Dec., 1893, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; dist. mag. and collr., Bangalore, May, 1899; head asst., Sept., 1902; commr., Coorg, Oct., 1905.

HARRIS, ROBERT LISTON, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., London; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 1st Dec., 1877, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; depd. to settlt. dept., 1883-84; dist. judge, Nov., 1889; divl. judge, July, 1894; rettd., April, 1903.

HARRISON, ALFRED GEORGE, India Public Works Dept. (examiner).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1876, and posted to Burma; transf. to superior accts. estabt. as dep. examr., May, 1884; examr., May, 1891.

HARRISON, CHARLES HOLMES, Indian C.S. (postmr.-gen.).—Educ. at Rugby, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 30th Nov., 1893, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dist. judge, Nov., 1901; transf. to postal dept. as postmr.-gen., Bengal, Nov., 1903; offg. dep. dir.-genl., April, 1905.

HARRISON, CHARLES WILLIAM INGLEBY, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., 11th June, 1858; arrived, 9th Sept., 1860; served in the public work dept., irrign. branch, N.W. Prov., from 10th April, 1863, as asst. engr., exec. engr., and asst. to chief engr.; suptg. engr., Dec., 1881; offd. as joint sec. to govt., Bengal, July 1886, and as sec., April-June, 1888; chief engr., Dec., 1889; joint sec. to govt., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Jan., 1890; sec., July, 1891; reverted to mil. dept., June, 1894; rettd., Nov., 1894.

HARRISON, FRANCIS CAPEL, Indian C.S. (acctnt.-genl., Bombay).—Educ. at Rugby, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 27th Nov., 1884, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and in Assam as asst. commr.; offg. asst. acctnt.-gen., Madras, May, 1889; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Sept., 1890; cffg. under sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., May, 1892; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Oct., 1892; acctnt.-gen., Madras, Nov., 1897; acctnt.-gen. N.W. Provs. and Oudh, Oct., 1899; ditto, Bombay, Dec., 1904.

HARRISON, GEORGE McCULLOCH, Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Hill Coll.; entered the service, 1st Oct., 1882, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Dec., 1896.

HARRISON, HENRY ANTHONY, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1855; arrived, 3rd April, 1855; served from May, 1856, to Sept., 1858, in Bengal, as asst. mag. and collr., Cuttack, and subsequently in the N.W. Prov.; joint mag. and dep. collr., 1st grade, April, 1862; asst., settlt. dept., Feb., 1864; mag. and collr., March, 1872; offg.

commr., Jhansi divn., Sept., 1875; civil and sessions judge, March, 1878; retd., June, 1887.

HARRISON, MICHAEL HERMANN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Radley Sch. and Trin. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1899, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

HARRISON, PERCY, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 4th Nov., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., and asst. mag. and collr.; under sec., March, 1894; joint mag. and settlt. officer, May, 1897; dep. commr., Nov., 1901; mag. and collr., March, 1902; author of Bahraich settlt. report.

HARRISON, WILLIAM, India Office (2nd class clerk, India Store Depot).—Clerk, 2nd class, 21st Oct., 1897.

HART, GEORGE HENRY REEVES, late Finl. Dept., Govt. of India.—Probr. in finl. dept., 20th July, 1869; asst. to compr.-gen., Dec., 1870; asst. to private sec. to viceroy, 1874-76; asst. to acctnt.-gen., Bombay, 1877; private sec. to govr. of Bombay, March, 1880; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Aug., 1887; dep. acctnt.-gen., Lahore, Jan., 1889; Bombay, Feb., 1891; acted as compr., India treasuries, Aug., 1893, and as asst. sec., fin. and commerce dept.; confd. as compr. Indian treasuries, April, 1898; retd., April, 1902.

HART, JOHN HENRY EUSTACE, M.I.C.E., late Public Works Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 23rd Nov., 1854; joined the dept., Jan., 1855, and served as civil engr., acting exec. engr. for reclamations, Bombay, architectural exec. engr. and surveyor, Bombay, and acting supt., harbour works, Karachi; acting chief engr. for irrig., Sept., 1877; suptg. engr., March, 1880; joint sec. for irrig., March, 1880; chief engr., Dec., 1885; sec., public works and irrig. dept., Sept., 1887; retd., 1888.

HART, SIDNEY GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at King's Sch., Rochester, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 4th Dec., 1895, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfld. to Assam as asst. commr., June, 1897; sub. divl. offr., May, 1898.

HART-DAVIES, THOMAS, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Marlborough; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 13th Nov., 1869, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; from May, 1876, served in Sind as educt. inspr. and manager of encumbered estates; 1st asst. collr., April, 1887; judge and sessions judge, June, 1891; acted in May, 1895, as jud. commr. in Sind; retd., Feb., 1897.

HARTNOLL, HENRY SULIVAN, Barr. (1898), Indian C.S. (commr., Burma).—Educ. at Exeter Gr. Sch., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1883, and served in Burma as asst. commr. (war medal and clasp); dep. commr., July, 1890; commr., Dec., 1902.

HARTWELL, SYDNEY CHARLES ELPHINSTONE, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. traffic supt., Oudh and Rohilkhand railway, 1st Jan., 1889; offg. manager, 1891-92, and from April, 1895; retd., 1893.

HARVEY, EDWARD, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., 22nd Dec., 1859; served in the dept. from April, 1864; exec. engr., mil. works branch, June, 1871; served in the Lushai expdn., 1871-72 (medal and clasp), in the Jowaki Afridi campaign, 1878-79 (clasp), and in the Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal); supt. engr., May, 1889; retd., Oct., 1892.

HARVEY, JOHN, late Educl. Dept., Punjab.—Served as asst. and head master of various schs. from 1st Nov., 1859; asst. inspr. of schs., April, 1877; tutor to the Raja of Chamba, Nov., 1879; inspr. of schs., Nov., 1883; retd., June, 1902.

HARVEY, WILLIAM LEATHAM, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (sec. to govt. of India, dept. of industry and commerce).—Educ. at the Royal Acadl. Instn., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 25th Dec., 1883, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. officer; under sec. to govt., rev., finl. and gen. depts., Aug., 1891; 1st asst., Aug., 1894; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, July, 1895; municipal commr., Bombay City, Dec., 1898; C.I.E., Nov., 1901; sec. to govt. of India, dept. of industry and commerce, March, 1905.

HARWARD, ALFRED EDGAR, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Rossall Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 12th Dec., 1885, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1892; transfld. to Assam as asst. commr., April, 1894; district and sessions judge, July, 1896; retransfd. to Bengal, Dec., 1898; joint mag. and dep. collr., Sept., 1901; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1903; transfld. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

HASAN KHAN, M. N. (see Muhammad Nizam-ud-din Hasan Khan).

HASHMAT-ULLA, Maulvi, M.A., Statutory C.S. (joint mag., United Prov.).—Apptd. a probr. under the native civil service rules, Mar., 1886; confd., 13th June, 1889, as asst. mag. and collr. in N.W.P. and O.; joint mag., April, 1894; asst. commr., Jan., 1897 to Feb., 1898; author of a translation of Sir A. Colvin's speeches.

HASSAN, A. (see Abul Hassan).

HASTED, JOHN ORD, Col., R.E., late Public Works Dept., Madras.—2nd lieut., Madras engrs., Dec., 1853; served in the dept. from 30th Jan., 1856, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., April, 1865, and suptg. engr., Feb., 1877; chief engr. for irrig., and joint sec. to govt., April, 1884; fellow of Madras univ., 1883; sec., gen. and buildings and road branches, 1886; retd., July, 1890.

HASTINGS, CHARLES GODOLPHIN WILLIAM, C.I.E. (inspr.-genl. of police, Punjab).—Entered the service as asst. dist. supt., Sept., 1873; dist. supt., Dec., 1882; on special duty in Kabul, Feb.-Oct., 1880; with Chitral reief force, June-Sept., 1895; special duty in Kurram from Mar., 1896; C.I.E., June, 1897; dep. inspr.-gen., May, 1901; inspr.-genl. of police, N.W. Frontier Prov., Nov., 1901; ditto, Punjab, and under sec. to govt., Sept., 1904.

HASTINGS, FRANCIS THEOPHILUS CLINTON, India Office (clerk specially attached to pol. and secret dept.).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll.; admiralty writer, 8th Oct., 1868; civil service

copyist, Jan., 1874; clerk specially attached to the pol. and secret dept., India office, 7th March, 1877; rest. clerk, July, 1888; asst. private sec. to the sec. of state, June, 1892; précis writer, Nov., 1896; private sec. to the sec. of state, Nov., 1902; reverted to pol. and secret dept., Oct., 1903.

HASTINGS, HERBERT, Opium Dept., Bengal sub. dep. opium agent.—Joined the service, 16th June, 1870, as offg. asst. sub. dep. opium agent; confd., Aug., 1871; sub. dep. opium agent, Dec., 1885.

HASTINGS, WARREN, Lieut.-Col. Indian Army, Berar Commn. (commr. of Berar).—Educ. at Brighton Coll.; first commn., 28th Feb., 1874; served in Bengal as cantonmt. mag. and small cause court judge from Jan., 1881; special asst. commr., Berar, Sept., 1887; dep. commr., July, 1891; in charge of provl. census operations, 1890-92; insp.-gen. of police, &c., Oct., 1895, to Nov., 1903; commr. of Berar, July, 1904.

HATCH, GEORGE WASHINGTON, Indian C.S. (1st asst., Bombay).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 20th Nov., 1893, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; acting under sec., rev. and finl. depts., Jan., 1899; 2nd asst., Aug., 1900; 1st asst., Oct., 1904.

HATCH, HENRY FREDERICK, late Bombay Survey Dept.—Joined the service, 1st Dec., 1873, and served as asst. supt.; supt. of surveys and asst. to pol. agent, Kathiawar, April, 1896; ret'd., Aug., 1899.

HATCH, WILLIAM KEITH, M.B., C.M., F.R.C.S., Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Service.—Arrived, 10th Nov., 1877; second surgn. of the Jamsetji Jijibhoj hosp., and profr. of anatomy, &c., Grant med. coll., May, 1884; senr. med. officer and senr. surgn. of the hosp., and profr. of surgery at the coll., April, 1893; ditto, and principal of the coll., June 1897; ret'd., Nov., 1903.

HATCHELL, CHARLES, Surgn.-Major, late Med. Dept., N.W. Prov.—Asst. surgn., Bengal med. establ., 23rd July, 1858; served in N.W. Prov. as civil asst. surgn. from Dec., 1859, to Oct., 1864, and subsequently under the mil. dept.; took part in the Bhutan expedn.; served again under govt. of N.W. Prov. from Nov., 1869, as civil asst. surgn., jail supt., and civil surgn.; ret'd., Oct., 1888.

HATCHELL, DAVID GEORGE, Revenue Survey Dept., Madras (dep. director).—Joined the service, 14th Sept., 1885; asst. supt., Feb., 1887; dep. supt., Feb., 1899; supt., Jan., 1901; designation changed to dep. director, June, 1905.

HATHERLY, JOHN RODNEY, late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 23rd July, 1868, as asst. engr., and was employed on various irrig. works; exec. engr., Feb., 1876; ret'd., Nov., 1899.

HATHORNTHWAITE, JAMES THOMAS, M.A., late Educ. Dept., Bombay.—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; arrived, 3rd Oct., 1872, and served from that date as profr. of mathematics at the Elphinstone coll., Bombay; also principal of the coll., April, 1894; ret'd., June, 1900.

HATTEN, JOHN JAMES, B.A., India Public Works Dept. (suprg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 12th Oct., 1875,

and employed on irrig. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., May, 1883; supt. engr., Sept., 1903.

HAUGHTON, WALTER RALEIGH, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (special engr.).—Apptd. from E.B. rly. co., as special engr., 1st July, 1884, and posted to state rlys.; rest. engr., E.B. state rly., July, 1884; supt. of works, Nov., 1898; engr.-in-chief, Murshedabad branch, E.B. state rly., Nov., 1902.

HAUXWELL, THOMAS ADDISON, India Forest Dept. (conservator, Burma).—Entered the service, 31st Dec., 1880, as asst. consr., Burma; dep. consr., May, 1886; consr., Feb., 1902.

HAVELL, ERNEST BINFIELD, Indian Educ. Service.—Apptd. supt. of the Calcutta school of art and art gallery, July, 1866; author of a "Handbook to Agra and the Taj" and "Benares."

HAVELOCK, SIR ARTHUR ELIBANK, G.C.S.I., G.C.M.G., G.C.I.E., late Govr. of Madras.—Entered the army, Jan., 1862; private sec. and A.D.C. to the officer administering the govt. of Mauritius, 1873-74; acting chief civil commr., Seychelles Islands, 1874-75; member of council, Fiji, and for a time colonial sec. and receiver-gen., 1875; prest. of Nevis, 1877; administering the govt. of St. Lucia, 1878-79; chief civil commr., Seychelles, 1879; govr. of W. Africa settls., 1881, and assisted in Anglo-French negotiations at Paris regarding that territory; consul for Liberia, 1881; K.C.M.G., 1884; govr. of Trinidad, 1884; govr. of Natal and special commr. for Zulu affairs, 1885; also govr. of Zululand, 1887; attended Brussels conference on the suppression of the slave trade, 1889; govr. of Ceylon, 1890; G.C.M.G., 1895; govr. of Madras, March, 1896; to Dec., 1900; G.C.I.E., March, 1896; G.C.S.I., April, 1901; govr. of Tasmania 1901 to 1904.

HAWKER, REGINALD JAMES, India Office (2nd class clerk, India store depôt).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, India store depôt, 21st Sept., 1903.

HAWKINS, ROBERT WILLIAM LYONS, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 3rd Oct., 1873, and employed on irrig. works in N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Jan., 1884.

HAY, GEORGE WILLIAM ROBERTSON, M.D., Surg. Gen., late Indian Med. Service.—Joined the service, 1st April, 1868; arrived, 24th Oct., 1868; served in the mil., civil, and jail branches of the service, and was apptd. port surgn. at Aden, June, 1876; sec. to the principal med. officer, Poona, Oct., 1890; principal med. officer, Bombay and Aden dist., Aug., 1897; principal med. officer, Madras, May, 1900; ret'd., Dec., 1904.

HAYDON, WILLIAM HENRY, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 17th Dec., 1862; arrived, 1st March, 1865; exec. engr., Jan., 1870; employed on Bombay reclamation works and defences, Tapti bridge, and with the Kandahar field force; employed under mil. works dept., 1890-91; ret'd., Dec., 1894.

HAYWARD, MAURICE HENRY WESTON, LL.B., Indian C.S. (legal remembrancer, Bombay).—Educ. at Harrow and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 19th Nov., 1889, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; under sec. to govt. of Bombay, pol., &c., depts., Aug. 1895; jud. asst. to pol. agent, Kathiawar, Sept., 1896; acting

dist. judge, Karachi, Nov., 1899; confd., June, 1900; legal remembrancer, Bombay, Oct., 1905.

HAZLETT, HENRY JAMES, M.D., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Madras Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 1st April, 1867; arrived, 8th Nov., 1867, and was employed on mil. and jail duty; analyst to salt commn., Madras, 1876; surgn., 4th district, Jan., 1887, to May, 1888; also profir. in med. coll., Aug., 1887; employed in mil. accts. dept. of govt. of India, 1888 and 1889; surgn., 3rd district, Feb., 1893; retd., July, 1897.

HEAD, EDGAR ALFRED, India Office (second-class clerk, Store Dept.).—Educ. at King Edward's Gr. Sch., Bath; clerk, 2nd class, 30th May, 1895.

HEADLEY, ROBERT HOLLOWELL, LL.B., India Office (second-class clerk, Military Dept.).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., and King's Coll., London; Clerk, 2nd class, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., 9th March, 1896; LL.B. (honours) Lond. Univ., 1901; transfid. to mil. dept., Nov., 1901; called to the bar (Middle Temple), 1903.

HEALD, BENJAMIN HERBERT, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Nottingham High Sch. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 3rd Dec., 1898, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and asst. settlmt. offr.

HEANEY, JOHN SAMUEL, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at the Royal Univ. of Ireland, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 4th Dec., 1895, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; transfid. to Bombay on famine duty, July, 1900; 1st asst., Feb., 1902; re-transf. to Madras, Jan., 1903.

HEATON, BERNARD, Indian Educl. Service (princpl. Sibpur civil engrg. coll.).—Apptd. to public works dept. as asst. engr., 16th March, 1888; since Nov. 1891, has offd. as profir. in the civil engrg. coll. at Sibpur; principal of the coll., July, 1904.

HEATON, JOSEPH JOHN, Indian C.S. (judge and sess. judge, Bombay).—Educ. at Bradford Gr. Sch., and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 24th Dec., 1881, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and acting under sec.; asst. judge and sessions judge, July 1889; private sec. to govr. of Bombay, July, 1895; judge and sess. judge, July, 1898.

HEAVEN, FRANCIS GYDE, India Public Works Dept. (examiner).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept. 1879 and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; transfid. to superior accts. estabt. as dep. examr., Nov. 1885; examr., Jan., 1893.

HEAVISIDE, WILLIAM JAMES, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Survey of India Dept.—Joined Addiscombe, Aug., 1857; lieut., Bengal engrs., June, 1859; served in the public works dept. as asst. engr. from Sept., 1862; asst. field engr. with the Bhutan force, 1865-66 (medal and clasp); joined the great trigonl. survey, Jan. 1866; dep. supt., April, 1872; served in Afghanistan, 1878-79 (medal), and subsequently in Khandesh and Bombay native states; retd., July, 1888.

HEDDON, HAROLD, India Office (2nd class clerk, registry and record dept.).—Apptd. 2nd

class clerk, registry and record dept., 16th Dec., 1902.

HEINIG, JOSEPH, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd., 26th July, 1867, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Oct., 1877; supt. engr., Oct., 1895; retd., June, 1901.

HEMANGA CHANDRA BOSE, late Bengal Judl. Dept.—Joined the service as munsif, 14th April, 1872; sub. judge, July, 1887, to Feb., 1902.

HEM CHANDRA BANERJEE (see Banerjee, H. C.).

HEMING, DEMPSTER, Lieut.-Col., late Police Dept., Madras.—First commn., 16th Nov., 1861; proby. asst. supt. of police, Dec., 1865; dep. commr., Oct., 1874; asst. with the govt. of Madras, 1888, and 1890; supt. of police, Jan., 1892; retd., Feb., 1894.

HEMINGWAY, FREDERICK RICKETTS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Clifton and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 5th Dec., 1898, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

HEMINGWAY, HENRY EDMUND, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Cent. Prov.).—Educ. at Clifton Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in the Cent. Prov. as asst. commr. and settlmt. offr.

HEMINGWAY, PERCY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Oundle, and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 14th Dec., 1896, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr. and settlmt. offr.

HENDERSON, CLAUDE GORDON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Sind).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll. and Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 24th Nov., 1900, and served in Sind as asst. collr. and mag.

HENDERSON, EDWARD PIERCY, BARR. (1871), late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad., and St. Peter's Coll., Radley; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 10th Oct., 1871, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; govt. advocate, Feb., 1878; retd., Sept., 1897.

HENDERSON, GEORGE, M.D., Surgn.-Major, late Med. Dept., Punjab.—Joined the Indian med. dept. in 1859; civil asst. surgn., Shahpur, May, 1862; supt. of Lahore central jail, April, 1868; offg. supt., botanical gardens in Bengal, Dec., 1872; civil surgn., Gujranwala, Dec., 1875, and subsequently at other stations in the Punjab; retd., Nov., 1889.

HENDERSON, GILBERT STUART, BARR. (1876) (judge, High Court, Calcutta).—Apptd. judge, High Court, Calcutta, 13th June, 1902.

HENDERSON, HARRY JOSEPH HUME, late Bombay Police Dept.—Joined the service, May, 1872, as asst. supt. of police; supt., Nov., 1878; dist. supt., Dec., 1898; retd., May, 1903.

HENDERSON, JAMES PERCY, India Public Works Dept. (avec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1879, and posted to Burma; exec. engr., Feb., 1891.

HENDERSON, JOHN JAMES, India Public Works Dept. (special engr.).—Apptd. from S. Punjab and Delhi Rly. Co., as special asst. engr., 1st Jan., 1886, and posted to N.W. Rly.

HENDERSON, PHILIP DURHAM, C.S.I., Maj.-Gen., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Madras cavalry, Feb., 1857; attaché, foreign dept., May, 1871; under sec. to the govt. of India, foreign dept., Nov., 1872; on special duty in Kashmir, 1874; pol. officer on staff of H.R.H. the Prince of Wales, Sept., 1875; C.S.I., March, 1876; gen. supt. of operations for suppression of thagi and dakaiti, March, 1878; rest. in Mysore, and chief commr., Coorg, April, 1892; reverted to mil. dept., Feb., 1895.

HENDERSON, WILLIAM GEORGE HUME, F.R.C.S., Lieut.-Col., Indian M.S. (civil surgn., Poona).—Joined the service, 21st Nov., 1876; in mil. employ till April, 1878, since when has held several civil surgeoncies; was on field service in Upper Burma, 1886-88; presy. surgn., Bombay, April, 1901; civil surgn., Poona, March, 1902.

HENDERSON, WILLIAM SCOTT, India Office, (2nd class clerk, India Store Depot).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, Sept., 1900.

HENDLEY, HAROLD, M.D., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surgn., Punjab).—1st commn., 1st April, 1884; in mil. employ till Jan., 1888, from which time he has held the appt. of civil surgn. at various stations in the Punjab.

HENDLEY, THOMAS HOLBEIN, C.I.E., Col., Indian Med. Service, late Bengal Med. Dept.—First commn., Oct., 1869; apptd. inspr.-gen. of civil hospls., Calcutta, April, 1898; ret'd., April, 1903.

HENNESSY, SEPTIMUS HOPE, late Jud. Dept., Central Prov.—Joined the service as acting extra asst. commr., 13th July, 1864, and was confd., 8th July, 1867; judge, small cause court, Sept., 1889; acted also as cantonmt. mag. from Nov., 1888; ret'd., Jan., 1892.

HENNIKER, FREDERICK CHANDOS, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 26th Nov., 1887, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Nov., 1897; dir. of land records and agriculture, Dec., 1898, to Oct., 1905.

HENRIQUES, QUENTIN QUIXANO, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1897, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

HENRY, EDWARD RICHARD, C.S.I., C.V.O., late Indian C.S. (commr. of metrop. police, London).—Educ. at St. Edmund's Coll., Ware, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 22nd Oct., 1873, and served in Bengal; joint mag. and dep. collr., 1st grade, June, 1882; on famine duty, Tirhut, 1873; private sec. to lieut.-govr. in 1879 and 1880; mag. and collr., May, 1890; offg. sec. to board of rev., 1890-91; inspr.-gen. of police, May, 1892; C.S.I., May, 1893; commr., Mar., 1900; ret'd., March, 1901; asst. commr. of metrop. police, London, 1901; commr. of ditto, March, 1903; C.V.O., June, 1905.

HENSLEY, JOHN WILLIAM, India Telegraph Dept. (chief supt.).—Apptd. asst. supt., Jan., 1879; supt., April, 1888; chief supt., March, 1903; served with Chin-Lushai expdn., 1883-90 (medal and clasp).

HENSLOWE, CECIL WILLIAM EDWARD, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept., June, 1866, and served in the N.W. Prov. and

Oudh, and in the Central Prov.; transf. to railway establ., Jan., 1880; engr.-in-chief and manager, Cawnpore-Farukhabad and Muttra-Hathras railways, Dec., 1880; manager, Cawnpore-Achnera state railway, 1884; transf. to Burma state railway, Dec., 1886; ret'd., 1890.

HENVEY, FREDERICK, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Rugby Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., under sec. to govt., offg. sec. to board of rev., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; on special duty in 1871 for purpose of compiling a narrative of the famine of 1869-70; under sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., July, 1874; offg. rest., Nepal, 1877-78; on special duty, Kashmir, April, 1878; commr., Haidarabad assigned districts, Jan., 1883; rest., E. Rajputana states, Dec., 1885; agent to the govr.-gen. in Central India, Jan., 1889; ret'd., Jan., 1891.

HERBERT, CHARLES, Lt.-Col., Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (resdt., Jaipur).—First commn., June, 1874; served under the mil. dept. to 1882, including Afghan war of 1879-80; extra A.D.C. to viceroy, 1878 and 1882; offd. as asst. to the govr.-gen., Rajputana, June, 1882; mag. of Abu, Nov., 1882; on depn. to Mewar states to settle boundary disputes, 1883; offg. cantonmt. mag., Nasirabad, 1884 and 1885-86; asst. commr., Ajmir, April, 1889; asst. to the agent, Rajputana, and chief commr., Ajmir-Merwara, March, 1891; offg. pol. agent in E. states of Rajputana, 1891-92; 1st asst. to agent, Central India, Oct., 1892; pol. agent, Kotah, March, 1894; ditto, Eastern States of Rajputana, 1900; resdt., Gwalior, Feb., 1901; in charge of ex-Amir, Oct., 1904; resdt., Jaipur, March, 1905.

HERBERT, DAVID WILLIAM, A.M.I.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Oct., 1880; exec. engr. Dec., 1893; under sec. to govt., Jan., 1901; sany. engr., Poona, March, 1902.

HERBERT, DOUGLAS, Major, Indian Army (dep. commr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Tunbridge Sch.; 1st commn., 25th Nov., 1885; joined the Assam commn., March, 1891, as asst. commr.; dep. commr., March, 1899.

HERBERT, HERBERT, F.R.C.S., Major, I.M.S. (prof., Grant Med. Coll., Bombay).—1st commn., 31st March, 1887; on mil. duty till Dec., 1892; civil surgn., Kaira, April, 1893; ophthalmic surgn., J. J. Hospital, and prof., Grant Med. Coll., June, 1897.

HERBERT, PATRICK HERBERT COGHLAN, India Office (asst. sec., Jud. and Public Dept.).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; junr. clerk, accts. branch, 11th March, 1872; jud. and public dept., 1877; rest. clerk, 1885-88; senr. clerk, May, 1891; private sec. to parly. under sec. of state (Mr. G. W. E. Russell, M.P.), 1892-94; editor of India Office List, 1887-91; asst. sec., jud. and public dept., April, 1904; compiled jointly with Mr. Newmarch the India office cypher code.

HERRON, HERBERT GEORGE WHITBY, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Rugby and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 27th Dec., 1887, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Burma as asst. commr., Nov., 1888, and offg. under sec.; returned to Bengal, Nov., 1890; joint mag. and collr., Nov., 1896; ret'd., Jan., 1904.

HERSCHEL, JOHN, F.R.S., Col., R.E., late Survey of India.—Joined Addiscombe, Feb., 1855; first commn., Dec., 1856; arrived at Calcutta, Jan., 1859; served in the great trigonol. survey from Nov., 1859, in the grades of asst. surveyor and dep. supt.; retd., March, 1886.

HERVEY, HAMILTON LAW, Indian C.S. (judge and sessions judge, Bombay).—Educ. at Giggleswick Sch. and Balliol Coll. Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 27th March, 1887, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., May, 1894; 1st asst., April, 1897; judge and sessions judge, July, 1897.

HESELTINE, HARRY NELSON (asst. sec., fin. dept., govt. of India).—Joined the service 19th April, 1881; asst. sec., fin. and comm. dept., govt. of India, Aug., 1899.

HEWAT, RICHARD ALEXANDER, late India Office.—East India House writer, old estabt., 18th Aug., 1858; junr. clerk, mil. dept., Aug., 1855; senr. clerk, Jan., 1875; asst. sec., judl. and public dept., Feb., 1894; retd., March, 1896.

HEWETSON, JOHN, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras).—Educ. at Repton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 13th Dec., 1880, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; served in Burma, from March, 1886, as asst. commr., judge, civil court, Mandalay, and district officer; returned to Madras, Oct., 1888; senr. asst., Ganjam, July, 1889; head asst., Aug., 1893; sub-collr. and joint mag., Dec., 1894; district and sessions judge, May, 1897; collr. and mag., Aug., 1898; again dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1899.

HEWETT, JOHN PRESCOTT, C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (member of govr.-genl.'s council).—Educ. at Winchester, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 17th Dec., 1877, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; in charge of provl. gazetteer, Aug., 1883; acted as asst. acctnt.-genl., Jan., 1884; junr. sec., board of rev., April, 1884; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Oct., 1886; confd., Aug., 1887; offd. as private sec. to the viceroy in 1888 and 1892, and as sec. to govt. of India, home dept., March, 1890; offg. census commr. (1 India, July, 1890; dep. sec. to govt. of Ind. r., home dept., Dec., 1890; C.I.E., May, 1891; dep. commr., March, 1893; sec. of royal commn. on opium, Oct., 1893; mag. and collr., April, 1894; sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Dec., 1895; C.S.I., Dec., 1898; member of plague commn., Nov., 1898; offg. chief commr., Central Provs., Sept., 1902; confd., Nov., 1903; temp. member of govr.-genl.'s council, Aug. 1904; confd., Dec., 1904; author of contributions to provl. gazetteer.

HEWETT, WILLIAM SELWOOD, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army, Jud. Dept., Punjab (cantonmt. mag., Rawalpindi).—First commn., 30th Dec., 1871; arrived, 13th March, 1872, and was employed on mil. duty, including service in the Afghan war, 1878-80 (medal and clasp); cantonmt. mag., Punjab, March, 1887.

HEWISH, JOHN, late India Office.—Junr. clerk, 1st Feb., 1869; senr. clerk, Sept., 1883; dep. acctnt.-gen. and assessor of income tax, May, 1900; retd., April, 1905.

HEWITT, JAMES FRANCIS KATHERINUS, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1853; arrived, 12th Feb., 1859, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and

collr., and settl. officer; dep. commr., wards' estate in Behar, Feb., 1873; offg. sec., board of rev., Sept., 1874; mag. and collr., Oct., 1876; commr., Chota Nagpur, March, 1879; retd., July, 1885; author of 'The Ruling Races of Pre-Historic Times in India,' &c.

HEWITT, JOHN CHARLES, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 29th Jan., 1886, and posted to Bengal; exec. engr., April, 1897; under sec. to govt., Jan., 1904.

HEXT, REAR-ADMIRAL SIR JOHN, A.D.C., K.C.I.E., R.N. (Retd.).—Apptd. Director of the Royal Indian Marine, 26th Feb., 1883; K.C.I.E., June, 1897; retd., Feb., 1893.

HEYCOCK, WILLIAM BISSILL, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Oakham Sch., and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1903.

HEYWOOD, JOHN MAGEE, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined Addiscombe, Aug., 1854; 1st commn., Bengal engrs., June, 1856; served in the Bengal public works dept. from April, 1858, as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; suptg. engr., May, 1881; offg. chief engr., and joint sec. to govt. of Bengal, public works dept., 1883-84; chief engr. and joint sec., irrign. branch, Punjab, 1886; retd., Dec., 1889.

HEZLETT, JAMES, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Coleraine Academy, Queen's Coll., Galway, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived 20th Nov., 1899, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

HICKIE, WILLIAM CORNELIUS, India Public Works Dept. (examiner).—Apptd. to the dept. as acct., 14th Sept., 1878; asst. examr., Oct., 1882; dep. examr., May, 1886; examr., Aug., 1894; offd. as manager of Bhavnagar-Gondal rly. in 1900 and 1903-04.

HIGGINS, ALFRED WILLIAM BUCKLE, late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Haileybury; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 6th Dec., 1876, and served as asst. collr. and mag.; on famine duty, 1877-78; sec. to the draft forest bill committee, 1882; dep. conservator of forests, Cuddapah, Dec., 1882; also forest settlt. officer, July, 1886; sub-collr. and joint mag., June, 1890; principal asst. to collr., Jan., 1891; collr. and mag., Jan., 1892; member, rev. board, and dir. of land records and agric., Dec., 1893; addl. member of legisl. council, Madras, Aug., 1901; member, rev. board, and commr. of rev. settlt., April, 1902; retd., May, 1903.

HIGGINS, ANDREW FRANK, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (supg. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Oct., 1873, and posted to Hyderabad; exec. engr., May, 1881; K.-i.-H. medal, 2nd class, May, 1900; transfld. to Bengal as suptg. engr., Feb., 1901.

HIGGINS, JOHN JAMES, late Police Dept., Central Prov.—Apptd. acting adjutant, mil. police, Narsinghpur, for services rendered during the Mutiny under Sir Henryavelock, Dec., 1858; from March, 1861, served in the police dept. of the Central Prov., and was promoted to district supt., 1st class, June, 1879; retd., March, 1894.

HIGHAM, SIR THOMAS, K.C.I.E., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. proby. asst. engr., July, 1867; arrived, Oct. 1867, and served in the irrig. dept., Punjab; exec. engr., Sept., 1873; asst. sec. to govt. irrig. branch, Nov., 1882; supt. of works, Derajat, Sept., 1883; suptg. engr., Aug., 1886; offd. as chief engr. and joint sec. to govt., Punjab, July-Oct., 1891; chief engr. and sec., irrig. branch, Oct., 1894; on special famine relief duty, May to July, 1897; insp.-gen. of irrigation and joint sec. to govt. of India, Nov., 1897; author of 'Hydraulic Tables', C.I.E., May, 1896; K.C.I.E., June, 1902; ret'd., April, 1903.

HIGHT, ARTHUR EDWARD, A.M.I.C.E., late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Trained at the Royal Indian Engrg. Coll.; entered the service as asst. engr., Nov., 1878; exec. engr., March, 1890; ret'd., Feb., 1900.

HIGNELL, SIDNEY ROBERT, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Malvern and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 8th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., judl. and pol. depts., Nov., 1903, to July, 1904.

HILDEBRAND, ARTHUR HEDDING, C.I.E., late Burma Commn.—From Feb., 1868, served in the Burma police dept. as asst. supt.; from March, 1872, served in the Burma commn. as asst. commr., and was sent on special mission to Chiengmai, 1875, and in charge of expdn. to W. Karenni, 1876; dep. commr., Jan., 1885; supt., S. Shan states, May, 1888; C.I.E., May, 1888; supt. and pol. officer, S. Shan states, Aug., 1891; ret'd., March, 1902.

HILDEBRAND, GODFREY, Col., R.E., late Mil. Works Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Oct., 1864; arrived, Jan., 1868; joined the public works dept., March, 1869, and served in Bengal and the N.W. Prov.; transf. to mil. works, May, 1872; sec. to defence committee, 1881; suptg. engr., March, 1886; dep. dir.-gen., mil. works, 1890-91; asst. insp.-gen. of fortifications, War Office, July, 1894; dep. inspr.-gen., of fortifications, Nov., 1897; ret'd., June, 1902.

HILL, ARTHUR, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., F.C.H., Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Oct., 1880; exec. engr., June, 1892; C.I.E., Jan., 1903.

HILL, CLAUDE HAMILTON ARCHER, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (pol. agent, foreign dept., govt. of India).—Educ. at St. Mark's Sch., Windsor, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1887, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; under sec., pol., &c., depts., and sec. to legisl. council, Nov., 1892; under sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Aug., 1895; 1st asst., Nov., 1897; acting pol. agent, Hyderabad, Nov., 1897; private sec. to govt. of Bombay, Feb., 1900; dep. sec., foreign dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1903; C.I.E., Jan., 1904; offg. sec., pol. dept., Bombay, from June, 1904, to April, 1905.

HILL, ERNEST GEORGE, B.A., Indian Educ. Service (prof., Muir Coll., Allahabad).—Educ. at Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. prof'r. of nat. sci., Muir Coll., Allahabad, Dec., 1895; ditto, and meteorological reporter, United Prov., Nov., 1902; author of two works on Chemistry.

HILL, FREDERICK GEORGE, India Office (2nd class clerk, Funds Dept.).—Educ. at City of London Sch.; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, 6th July, 1882.

HILL, FREDERICK JUSTLEY ARNOLD, late Salt Dept., Bombay.—Educ. at King's Sch., Sherborne; joined the service, 20th June, 1881, as superny. asst. collr.; asst. collr. of salt rev., June, 1883; ret'd., June, 1905.

HILL, HENRY, late India Office.—Educ. at the Edinburgh Acad. and Edinburgh Univ.; India office writer, old establ., 1859; junr. clerk, finl. dept., 1860; asst. private sec. and précis writer to the sec. of state, 1867-74; semr. clerk, Jan., 1872; rev., statistics and commerce dept., 1874; asst. sec. in that dept., Sept., 1877; asst. in public works dept., and asst. govt. dir. of guaranteed Indian railway cos., June, 1892; reverted to rev. and statistics dept. as asst. sec., July, 1898; ret'd., April, 1900.

HILL, JOHN, Col., R.E., late Survey of India Dept.—Lieut., royal engrs., June, 1862; arrived, 10th Dec., 1865; apptd. to the trigon. survey as asst. surveyor, Dec., 1866, and employed in Kumaon and Garhwal, Bombay, &c.; dep. supt., July, 1882; attached to Afghan boundary commn., Sept., 1884; supt. (new scale), Aug., 1892; in charge of tidal and levelling operations, Poona, Nov., 1890; reverted to mil. dept., Dec., 1895; ret'd., March, 1896.

HILL, ROWLAND, late Stationery Dept., Madras.—Dep. supt., govt. press, Oct., 1874; supt., Sept., 1886; ret'd., Oct., 1904; K-i-H medal, Jan., 1905.

HILL, SAMUEL CHARLES, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Educ. Service (director of public instrn., Central Prov.).—Apptd. Profr., Dacca Coll., June, 1881; principal, Krishnagar Coll., Mar., 1888; prof'r., Hooghly Coll., Nov., 1891; prof'r., Presy. Coll., Mar., 1897; insp. of schools, Behar circle, April, 1897; ditto, Presy. circle, Feb., 1899; in charge of records of Govt. of India, and asst. sec., Home dept., Nov., 1900; dir. of public instrn., Central Prov., July, 1904; author of 'Three Frenchmen in Bengal' and 'Bengal in 1756-57'.

HILL, THOMAS COUTTS, M.I.E.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Apptd., 1st Aug., 1857; supt., British Burma, Feb., 1879; employed in connection with occupation of Upper Burma, 1886; chief supt., Sept., 1892; ret'd., March, 1894.

HILTON, JOHN EDWARD, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 8th Oct., 1868, and posted to the Punjab; transf. to mil. works dept., May, 1872; exec. engr., Aug., 1877; transf. to Punjab provl. establ., Nov., 1880; under sec. to govt., Sept., 1892; suptg. engr., May, 1893; ret'd., July, 1899.

HINDMARSH, THOMAS ANDREW, India Public Works Dept. (loco. supt.).—Apptd. to loco. dept., state rly's, 1st Nov., 1884; asst. loco. supt., Dec., 1885; dep. loco. supt., April, 1903; loco. supt., Jan., 1905.

HIRTZEL, FREDERIC ARTHUR, M.A., India Office (private sec. to sec. of state).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Trinity Coll., Oxford (Craven scholar); fellow of Brasenose, 1895; apptd. junr. clerk, 11th June, 1894; rest. clerk, Mar. 1897; private sec. to parly. under sec. of state, July, 1898; ditto to permanent under sec. of state, Mar., 1901; senior clerk, mil. dept., July, 1902; private sec. to sec. of state, Oct., 1903.

HITCHCOCK, JOHN (inspr. of machinery, India Store Dept.).—Apptd. asst. examr. of stores, July, 1882; examr., Nov., 1896;

supervisor, 2nd grade, Dec., 1898; inspr. of machinery, Sept., 1903.

HITTU RAM, C.I.E., Rai Bahadur, late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the service, 1st April, 1859, and held non-gazetted appts. till Feb., 1877, when he was apptd. native asst. to govt.-gen.'s agent in Baluchistan; on special duty with Afghan boundary commn., 1884; extra asst. commr., Jan., 1890; on special duty under Kalat state, May, 1893; author of a Baluchi manual, and a history of Dera Ghazi Khan.

HLA OUNG, MAUNG, Fin. Dept., Govt. of India (comptr., India Treasuries).—Entered the service, 1872, and served in the finance dept.; asst. comptr., Nov., 1880; asst. acct.-gen. for Burma, April, 1898; asst. comp.-gen., Calcutta, Nov., 1898; dep. acct.-gen., Burma, Dec., 1899; offg. dep. auditor.-gen., April, 1902; offg. comptr., India Treasuries, Sept., 1902, and Dec., 1903; confd., Aug., 1905; has published a revision of 'Gordon's Burma manual.'

HOARE, HERBERT JAMES, Indian C.S. (settlmnt. offr., Indore State).—Educ. at private schs. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 4th Nov., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., April, 1895; settlt. officer, Nov., 1896; dep. commr., Dec., 1901; settlmnt. offr., Indore State, Oct., 1904.

HOBART, ROBERT THOMPSON, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in the N.W. Prov., as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; dep. inspr.-gen. of police, Dec., 1873; inspr.-gen. of prisons, Oct., 1882; inspr.-gen. of police, May, 1886; rettd., Dec., 1889.

HOBART-HAMPDEN, AWDRY GEORGE, India Forest Dept. (conservator, United Prov.).—Joined the service as asst. consr., 2nd Jan., 1882; dep. consr., Feb., 1891; consr., Dec., 1902; director, forest school, Dehra Dun, April, 1903.

HOBDAV, JAMES RAMSAY, Col., Indian Army, late Survey of India Dept.—First commn., 13th Jan., 1869; proby. asst. supt., survey dept., Sept., 1874; confd., July, 1875; served in the Afghan war, 1879, and with Upper Burma field force, 1886; dep. supt., Jan., 1887; supt., Dec., 1895; dep. surveyor-genl., Sept., 1898; rettd. from survey dept., July, 1905; accompanied special expdn. in Upper Burma along the Chinese frontier, 1890-91.

HOBHOUSE, SIR CHARLES PARRY, Bart., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Rugby; apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 22nd July, 1844, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and in the finl. dept., govt. of India, as asst. to sub-treasurer, and asst. to acctnt.-gen.; collr. of Bardwan, 1857-59; mag. and collr., 1850; on special duty in 1860 to adjudicate on suits arising out of the indigo disturbances, Nuddea and Jessor; supt. and remembrancer of legal affairs, and govt. advocate, 1862; also member of Bengal legisl. council, 1863-64; civil and sessions judge, 1864-66; member of govt.-gen.'s legisl. council, 1866; chairman of the Orissa famine fund, 1867; judge of the high court, 1868-71; rettd., 1872.

HODGE, WILLIAM GEORGE, India Office (2nd class clerk, India Store Depôt).—Educ. at Christ's Coll., Brecon; apptd. 2nd class clerk, 26th Aug., 1904.

HODGSON, GODFREY BECKETT, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army, Survey of India (dep. surveyor-genl.).—First commn., 1st May, 1878; served in Afghan war, 1880; apptd. proby. asst. supt., 13th Sept., 1883; confd., July, 1884; dep. supt., May, 1889; supt., June, 1897; dep. surveyor-genl., Aug., 1905.

HODSON, CHARLES WILLIAM, C.S.I., M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1873, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., April, 1883; supt., way and works, N.W. railway, Nov., 1891; engr.-in-chief, Mushkaf-Bolan railway, July, 1894; services lent to Ceylon Govt., June, 1896; suptg. engr., Aug., 1896; dir. of railway construction and dep. sec. to govt. of India, railway branch, Jan., 1898; offg. sec., Jan., 1904, to Jan., 1905; C.S.I., Jan., 1905.

HODSON, RICHARD GEORGE, late Educ. Dept., Burma.—Joined the dept. as dep. inspr. of schs., Mysore, April, 1861; inspr., April, 1867; acting dir. of public instrn., 1879-80; transfed. to Burma as acting principal, high sch., Rangoon, April, 1880; from Oct., 1881, served as inspr. of schs. in Burma, and offd. as dir. of public instrn.; rettd., Feb., 1893; has transld. Munshi Srinivasaya's 'Dialogues in Kanarese.'

HOERNLE, AUGUSTUS FREDERICK RUDOLF, C.I.E., M.A., Ph.D., late Educ. Dept., Bengal. —Apptd. principal, Calcutta Madrassa, Feb., 1884; member of board of examrs., Nov., 1896; C.I.E., June, 1897; rettd., Mar., 1899; has written a grammar of the Gandian languages; dictionary of the Bihari language, and other philological works.

HOEY, WILLIAM, M.A., D.Lit., late Indian C.S., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Educ. at the Wesleyan Connexional Sch., and Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 18th Oct., 1872, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., asst. mag., and joint mag.; employed on archaeological explorations in Gonda in 1884; dep. commr., Feb., 1890; mag. and collr., Aug., 1890; offg. commr., Oct., 1897; author of 'Trades and Manufactures in N. India,' 'A History of Asaf-ud-Daulah, Nawab Wazir of Oudh,' 'Buddha; His Life, His Doctrine, and His Order,' 'Memoirs of Delhi and Fyzabad,' &c., &c.; rettd. July, 1900.

HOGAN, JOHN LEMON POICTEVIN, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (supg. engr.).—Trained at Thcmason Coll.; apptd., 1st Nov., 1873, and posted to mil. works; transfed. to Mysore, Sept., 1877; to Central Prov., Sept., 1878; and to state railways, Jan., 1880; services lent to Bombay, Baroda and Central India railway co., Jan., 1885; exec. engr., Feb., 1885; suptg. engr., May, 1899; junr. consg. engr. to govt. of India, for rlys., Calcutta, May to Dec., 1901.

HÖGG, SIR ADAM GEORGE FORBES, K.C.B., Lieut.-Gen., Indian Army, late Pol. Dept., Bombay.—First commn., Bombay infantry, Jan., 1854; served with the Persian expdn., 1857 (medal and clasp), in the Mutiny, the China war, 1860, the Abyssinian expdn., 1867-68 (medal), and the Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal and clasp); apptd. pol. rest. and collr. of stamp rev., with charge of the vice-admiralty court, Aden, May, 1885; reverted to mil. duty, 1890; K.C.B., June, 1904.

HOGG, SIR FREDERIC RUSSELL, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1856; arrived, 12th Feb., 1857, and

served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; postmr.-gen., Punjab and Sind, July, 1863; postmr.-gen., Bengal, Nov., 1868; representative of the govt. of India at the postal congress, Paris, 1878; dir.-gen. of post office of India, March, 1882; C.S.I., Feb., 1887; K.C.I.E., May, 1888; ret'd. April, 1889.

HOGG, STAPLETON COTTON, late Finl. Dept., India Office.—Temporary clerk, 25th July, 1861; junr. clerk, accts. dept., 1862; transfid. to finl. dept., 1863; senr. clerk, Dec., 1875; asst. sec., Sept., 1895; ret'd., Oct., 1901.

HOGG, SIR STUART S., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1853; arrived, Jan., 1853; served in the Punjab as asst. commr. till 1859, and afterwards in Oudh as dep. commr.; mag. and collr., Bardwan, 1861; chairman, justices of the peace, Calcutta, 1866; commr. of police, Calcutta, and member of Bengal legisl. council, 1874; ret'd., 1878.

HOGG, THEODORE WILLIAM, Col., Indian Army, late Central Prov. Comm.—First commn., 4th Dec., 1857; arrived, 28th Jan., 1858; and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr. from Aug., 1867; small cause court judge, March, 1883; dep. commr., Aug., 1889; on U.S. list, July, 1898.

HOGHTON, HENRY ALEXANDER, India Forest Dept. (conservator, United Prov.).—Joined the service as asst. consr., Burma, 5th Jan., 1885; dep. consr., Sept., 1887; transfid. to Central Prov., Sept., 1892; to Punjab, Nov., 1895; to United Prov., Aug., 1904; consr., Aug., 1904.

HOLDERNESS, THOMAS WILLIAM, C.S.I., B.A., India Office (sec., rev. and statistics dept.).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 17th Feb., 1873, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., asst. sec. to govt., and junr. sec., board of rev.; under sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agricl. dept., Sept., 1881; joint mag., N.W. Prov., April, 1885; under sec. to govt., N.W. Prov., Nov., 1886; dir. of land records and agric., Nov., 1888; sec. to govt., finl. dept., March, 1892, at the same time acting as sec. in the jud. dept.; member of legisl. council, Aug., 1892; mag. and collr., Aug., 1893; sec. to govt., judl. dept., July, 1894; famine duty, home dept., govt. of India, Jan., 1897, and sec. to govt., rev. and agric. dept., July, 1898; C.S.I., Jan., 1898; K.-i-H. medal, Jan., 1901; retired from Indian C.S., May, 1901; apptd. sec., rev. and statistics dept., India Office, April, 1901.

HOLDICH, SIR THOMAS HUNGERFORD, K.C.M.G., K.C.I.E., C.B., Col., R.E., late Survey of India.—Lieut. royal engrs., Dec., 1862; arrived, 3rd March, 1865, and served in the Bhutan campaign, 1865 (medal and clasp); joined the topogr. survey, July, 1866; on duty with the Abyssinian expedn., 1867-68 (medal); joined the Afghanistan field force in 1879, and was in charge of survey operations in N. Afghanistan during second period of the campaign, and asst. field engr. during defence of Sherpur (medal and clasp); in charge of Kohat topogr. survey, Feb., 1881; dep. supt., April, 1883; in charge of Baluchistan topogr. survey, Oct., 1883; attached to the Afghan boundary commn., Sept., 1884; survey officer with the Zohb field force in 1890; supt. (new scale), Aug., 1892; C.B. and C.I.E., May, 1894; on special duty with Khaibar-Asmar delimita-

tion commn., 1894-95; in charge, Pamir detachment, June, 1895; temporary appt. to Ceylon, Sept., 1896; K.C.I.E., June, 1897; chief survey officer with Tirah expedi. force, Aug., 1897; ret'd., Feb., 1900; member of Chile-Argentina Boundary Tribunal, 1902; K.C.M.G., Dec., 1902; author of articles on the geography of Afghanistan and the Indian frontier, and on mil. instrn., topography, &c., and 'The Countries of the King's Award.'

HOLIDAY, FREDERICK CHARLES (auditor, India Audit Office).—Temporary clerk, 17th June, 1861; junr. asst., June, 1863; senr. examr., Dec., 1868; designation changed to senr. clerk, Oct., 1892; asst. auditor, Jan., 1895; auditor, Feb., 1903; India Office member of the following deptl. committees: on the Eastern mail service, May, 1903; on the Indian army transport service, July, 1904; on the adjustment of charges for Indian native regts. serving in the Colonies, Sept., 1904.

HOLLAND, GERALD EDWARD, C.I.E., D.S.O. Commander Royal Indian Marine.—Joined the Indian Marine, July, 1880; promoted commander, April, 1893; marine transport officer to the South African Force, Sept., 1899; port officer, Rangoon, May, 1901 to Sept., 1903; C.I.E., July, 1901; ret'd., Aug., 1905.

HOLLAND, ROBERT ERSKINE, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr. of salt, &c., Madras).—Educ. at Winchester and Oriel Coll., Oxford; appt. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 10th Dec., 1896, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; transfid. to Bombay, July, 1900; 1st asst., March, 1901; retransf. to Madras, Nov., 1902; dep. commr. of salt, &c., July, 1903; offg. asst. to the agent in Central India, June, 1904; offg. asst. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Sept., 1904.

HOLLAND, THOMAS HENRY, F.G.S., Geol. survey of India (director).—Joined the service 10th July, 1890, as asst. supt. and curator, geol. museum and laboratory; dep. supt., July, 1894; dir., Feb., 1903; has contributed to the survey publications.

HOLME, ALAN THOMAS, Indian C.S. (asst. to resdt. in Mewar).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., Clifton Coll., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 15th Nov., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.; asst. settl. offr., Nov., 1898; settl. offr., Nov., 1901; asst. to resdt. in Mewar, and settl. offr. for Banswara, &c., Jan., 1904.

HOLME, CHARLES HENRY, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll., apptd. 1st Oct., 1876, and posted as asst. engr. to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., March, 1891; ret'd., Oct., 1899.

HOLME, HENRY EDWARD, Barr. (1892), Indian C.S. (dep. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 27th Sept., 1890, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., May, 1894; joint mag., June, 1896; dep. commr., Nov., 1902.

HOLME, HUGH BASIL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. sec. to govt., Burma).—Educ. at Rugby Sch., Durham Coll. of Science, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived,

9th Dec., 1902, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to govt., Aug., 1905.

HOLMES, ARTHUR CLEMENT, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived 20th Nov., 1899, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

HOLMES, JOHN LLEWELLYN.—Apptd. resident engr., Port of Rangoon, 29th June, 1903.

HOLMES, ROBERT ANDREW KING, M.A., M.D., Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Service.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872, as asst. surgn., Bengal med. establ.; jail supt., Meerut, April, 1876; transfed. in similar capacity to Lucknow, Feb., 1885; in 1892 acted as inspr.-gen. of prisons; ret'd., Mar., 1899.

HOLMES, WILLIAM, late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Windermere Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 21st Nov., 1868, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; in charge of Amir Yakub Khan, March, 1880; joint mag., April, 1881; private sec. to lieut.-govr., N.W. Prov., March, 1883; asst. commr., Oudh, Dec., 1883; joint mag., June, 1884; dep. commr., Aug., 1887; mag. and collr., Dec., 1888; acted as inspr.-gen. of police in 1893; ret'd., Sept., 1894.

HOLMES, WILLIAM CUTHBERT, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 7th Nov., 1877, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr., and in Bombay as offg. asst. acctnt.-gen.; head asst., Coimbatore, Dec., 1884; sub-collr. and joint mag., Jan., 1890; district and sessions judge S. Kanara, Feb., 1893; collr. district mag. and agent to govt., Vizagapatam, Dec., 1896; sub. collr. and joint mag., N. Arcot, Dec., 1897; dist. and sess. judge, Kistna, Feb., 1899; ret'd., Aug., 1902.

HOLMS, JOHN MITCHELL, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (chief sec. to govt., United Prov.).—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 26th Nov., 1883, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., April, 1889; junr. sec. to board of rev., May, 1889, and Feb., 1891; asst. commr., Oudh, March, 1892; joint mag., Sept., 1892; acted as registrar, high court, in 1894; dep. commr., Nov., 1895; mag. and collr. and joint sec. to rev. board, Aug., 1896; sec. to rev. board, Nov. 1898; inspr.-gen. of registrn. and commr. of excise, Mar., 1899; mag. and collr., Nov., 1901; second sec. to govt., April, 1903; member, legisl. council, June, 1903; chief sec. to govt., July, 1905; C.S.I., Jan., 1906.

HOLMS, WILLIAM FREDERICK, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1888, and posted to the Punjab; exec. engr., Feb., 1899.

HOLMWOOD, HERBERT, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Uppingham; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 16th Dec., 1879, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1890; inspr.-gen. of registrn., Nov., 1890; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1894.

HOLROYD, WILLIAM RICE MORLAND, Col., Indian Army, late Educ. Dept., Punjab.—First commn. as ensign, Dec., 1853; served from 11th Aug., 1858, as inspr. of schools, and was apptd. dir. of public instrn., Punjab, Feb., 1868; under sec. to govt., educ. dept., Oct., 1884; reverted to mil. duty, Oct., 1890.

HOMAN, DOUGLAS KNOX, Punjab Police Dept. (dist. supt.).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1875; apptd. asst. dist. supt. of police, Nov., 1878; dist. supt., March, 1888.

HOME, ALEXANDER LEFEVRE, late Indian Forest Dept.—From 1st March, 1864, served in the dept. in the Punjab, Central Prov. and Bengal, and was apptd. dep. conservator, Oct., 1871; asst. comptr.-gen. of forests, April, 1877; conservator of forests, Bengal, April, 1883; Upper Burma, Oct., 1892; Assam, Dec., 1894; N.W. Prov., April, 1899; ret'd., Jan., 1901.

HOME, FREDERICK JERVIS, C.S.I., Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., 11th June, 1858; arrived, 9th Sept., 1860; served in the public works dept., N.W. Prov., irrign. branch, from Feb., 1861, as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; suptg. engr., Jan., 1883; offg. chief engr., irrign. branch, and joint sec. to govt., N.W. Prov., 1885; on special duty in the Bhattpur state, Oct., 1886; chief engr. and joint sec., Punjab, Dec., 1889; inspr.-gen. of irrign. and dep. sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., Nov., 1890; C.S.I., May, 1892; reverted to mil. dept., Oct., 1894; ret'd., May, 1895.

HOME, WALTER, C.I.E., India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. 1st Oct., 1877, and posted as asst. engr. to State Railways; exec. engr., Dec., 1891; K.-i.-H. medal, May, 1900; suptg. engr., Feb., 1904; C.I.E., June, 1904.

HOOPER, CLEMENT WILLIAM ROBERT, Cmdr., R.I.M. (principal port offr., Burma).—Joined the service, 30th Sept., 1878; cmdr., Sept., 1891; dep. consr. of the port of Madras, June, 1902; principal port offr., etc., Burma, Sept., 1903.

HOOPER, EDWARD DAW MASHTER, India Forest Dept., (conservator, Madras).—Entered the dept. as asst. consr., Dec., 1874; dep. consr., Sept., 1882; consr., March, 1900; is the author of reports on the forests of British Honduras and several West Indian islands.

HOOPER, JOHN, C.S.I., M.A., Indian C.S. (member, board of revenue, United Prov.).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., and Univ. of London; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 7th Dec., 1871, and served in Oudh as asst. commr.; settlt. officer, Basti, Nov., 1884; mag. and collr., April, 1890; sec., board of rev., Aug., 1890; addl. commr., Fyzabad, Oct., 1896; commr., July, 1899; member, board of revenue, Oct., 1903; C.S.I., Jan., 1904; member, legisl. council, June, 1902, and June, 1904; author of Basti settlt. report.

HOOPER, SIR WILLIAM ROE, K.C.S.I., Surgn.-Gen.—Asst. surgn., Bengalmed. establ., Feb., 1859; arrived, Aug., 1859, and served in Bengal, holding various mil. med. charges; from July, 1861, served in the N.W.
