MUL RAJ, LALA, M.A., Punjab Provl. C.S. (judge, small cause court, Lahore).—Joined the service, Jan., 1880, as offg. extra asst. commr.; confd. Dec., 1883; extra judl. asst. commr., March, 1887; judge, small cause court, Lahore, Nov., 1904; author of a sanitary primer and a text-book in Hindi.

MULRONEY, THOMAS RICHARD, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., Punjab).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1880; in mil. employ till Aug., 1883, since when he has held several civil surgeoncies in the Punjab.

MUMFORD, CHARLES ALLAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Oundle Sch., and Peterhouse, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 8th Dec., 1896, and served in the N.W.P. and Oudh as asst. commr., asst. mag. and collr., and asst. supt.

MUMFORD, GERALD BLOMFIELD, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Felsted Sch. and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 1st Dec., 1902, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

MUNRO, JAMES HUME, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras).—Educ. at New-castle-on-Tyne Gr. Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 21st Nov., 1886, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., March, 1893; sub-collr. and joint mag., Dec., 1898; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1903.

MURDOCH, DAVID DEIG, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras).—Educ. at Univ. of Aberdeen, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 7th Dec., 1882, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., April, 1890; dep. commr. of salt, &c., rev. (S. divn.), and sec. to the commr., July, 1894; sub-collr. and joint mag., Oct., 1896; collr. and mag., Dec., 1897; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1905.

MURPHY, MATTHEW JOSEPH, Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and joint mag., Madras).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., Blackrock, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 21st Dec., 1891, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Dec., 1897; senr. asst. dist. mag. and agent to govr., Vizagapatam, May, 1898; sub-collr. and joint mag., Oct., 1904.

MURPHY, PATRICK WILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. settlmnt. offr., Bengal).—Educ. at

St. Peter's Coll., Wexford, and Univ. Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlmt. offr.

MURPHY, STEPHEN JAMES, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Framlingham Coll. and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 24th Nov. 1899, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

MURRAY, ALAN, Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Oudh Commn.—Arrived, 4th Feb., 1857, and served during the Mutiny (medal and two clasps); served in the Oudh police dept. as asst. supt. in 1866-67, and subsequently as asst. commr., and asst. settl. officer; junr. sec. to chief commr., 1874-78; attaché in foreign office, 1876-77; district judge, April, 1886; dep. commr., Kheri, April, 1886; ret'd., March, 1887.

MURRAY, FREDERICK CHARLES, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Calcutta Civil Engrg. Coll.; apptd., 24th May, 1872, and employed in the Punjab in mil. works branch; transfld. to that prov., Nov., 1879; exec. engr., Oct., 1881; suptg. engr., July, 1900; ret'd., Sept., 1904.

MURRAY, GEORGE RAMSAY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Cheltenham and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived 30th Nov., 1903, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.

MURRAY, JOHN, M.A., Indian Educl. Service, (inspr. of schools, United Prov.).—Educ. at Aberdeen Grammar Sch., Christ Coll., Cambridge, and Aberdeen Univ.; apptd. prof. of physical science, Muir Central Coll., 12th Oct., 1891; ditto, and meteorological reporter to the govt. of N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Nov., 1891; offg. meteorological reporter to govt. of India, May, 1902; inspr. of schs., United Prov., Dec., 1902; ditto, and of European schs., Dec., 1904; author of a manual of practical physics.

MURRAY, JOHN PETER, India Office (second class clerk, Registry and Record Dept.).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 5th Feb., 1901.

MURRAY, ROBERT DAVIDSON, M.B., Col., Indian Med. Service (inspr.-genl. of civil hospitals, United Prov.).—First commn. 31st March, 1875; served as civil surgn. at various stations from April, 1876; 1st rest. surgn., Calcutta Gen. Hosp., March, 1886; civil surgn., Howrah, Dec., 1895; profir. Calcutta med. coll., Aug., 1898; inspr.-genl. of civil hospitals, United Prov., March, 1905; author of numerous contributions to the med. press.

MURRAY, STEWART BINNY, A.M.I.C.E., Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1884; exec. engr., Sept., 1895; dep. chief engr. and under sec. to govt., March, 1900.

MUSPRATT - WILLIAMS, C. A., Major, R.A. (chief inspr. of explosives).—First commn., 26th July, 1881; asst. supt., Kirkee Factory, May, 1887; ditto, Ishapore, Nov., 1890; chief inspector of explosives, Bengal, Nov., 1894; ditto with the govt. of India, Sept., 1898.

MYLNE, RIGHT REV. LOUIS GEORGE, M.A., D.D., late Ecclesi. Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 4th July, 1876; installed as Bishop of Bombay, 6th

July, 1876; ret'd., Jan., 1898, and apptd. vicar of St. Mary, Marlborough, Wilts.

NAILER, HENRY AUGUSTUS FITZROY, M.B., C.M., Lieut.-Col., late Indian Medl. Service.—First commn., 1st April, 1877; on mil. duty till July, 1882, when he was apptd. civil surgn., Chingleput; dist. medl. and sanitary officer, Tanjore, Nov., 1899; ret'd., Oct., 1903.

NANAVUTTY, E. M. (see Erachshah Maneckji Nanavutty).

NANNEY, LEWIS CHARLES, L.F.P.S., L.S.A., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Madras Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., 31st March, 1886; served under the mil. dept., also acting as civil surgn. at various stations, and as supt. of jail; supt., lunatic asylum, Madras, Oct., 1875; also profir., med. coll., July, 1876; med. officer, Coonoor and Kotagiri, 1879-81, and at Utakamand, 1881-83; special sanitary officer, Utakamand, Aug., 1881; civil surgn., Cannanore, July, 1883, Trichinopoli, June, 1884, and Karnul, May, 1890; ret'd., May, 1892.

NAPIER, ALAN BERTRAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Rossall Sch., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 24th Sept., 1880, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; registrar of the jud. commr.'s court, 1890-91; under sec. to chief commr., April to July, 1892; settl. officer, Sept., 1894; dep. commr., April, 1897; commr. of excise and inspr.-gen. of registration, April, 1902 to Feb., 1903; K-i-H., medal, 2nd class, Jan., 1904.

NAPIER, HON. EDWARD HERBERT SCOTT, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1883, and posted to State rlys.; exec. engr., Aug., 1894; dep. consg. engr., Bombay, Sept., 1900; ditto, Lucknow, Nov., 1901 to Dec., 1903.

NAPIER, HON. GEORGE CAMPBELL, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 14th Dec., 1861; from July, 1867, served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on special duty in Persia, 1874, and at Kandahar, 1881; private sec. and A.D.C. to lieut.-govr. of N.W. Prov., April, 1882, to Sept., 1883; jud. asst., Feb., 1883; dep. commr., Nov., 1884; supt., Kapurthala state, April, 1885; ret'd., April, 1887.

NARAIN DAS, Rai Bahadur, late Oudh Jud. Dept.—Served in the postal dept. from June, 1855; temporary extra asst. commr., March, 1867; sub-judge, Oct., 1881; judge of the small cause court, June, 1882; ret'd., Sept., 1892.

NARAYANA AIYAR, RAJAPPAYA, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Madras University and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 189; arrived 25th Dec., 1900, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

NARAYANA AIYAR SUBRAMANIYAM DIWAN BAHADUR, B.A., Barr. (1876) (admr.-gen., Madras).—Joined the service, 15th May, 1891, and offd. as admr.-gen.; small cause court judge, April, 1892; admr.-gen. and offl. trustee, Oct., 1897.

NARAYAN GANESH CHANDAVARKAR, B.A., LL.B.—Judge of the High Court, Bombay, Jan., 1901.

NARENDRA NATH, Diwan, M.A. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Apptd. probir. under native civil service rules, 3rd Feb., 1888; asst. commr.,

Punjab, Feb., 1890; dep. commr., July, 1897; has transld. Mill's 'Liberty' into Urdu.

NARSINGRAO BHOLANATH DIVATIA, B.A., Statutory C.S. (1st asst. Bombay).—Apptd. prob. under the native civil service rules, Jan., 1844; confd. as asst. collr. and mag., Feb., 1886; 1st asst., May, 1895.

NATH, N. (see Narendra Nath).

NATH, P. (see Prem Nath).

NATH, S. (see Sham Nath).

NATHAN, ROBERT, C.I.E., B.A., Barr. (1888), Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at St Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 20th Dec., 1888, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and on settl. work in Orissa; joint mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1894; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, fin. and commerce dept., March, 1895; special plague duty, April, 1897; under sec., home dept., Nov., 1897; mag. and collr., March, 1900; C.I.E., Jan., 1903; dep. sec., home dept., April, 1904; private sec. to the viceroy, Nov., 1904, to Nov., 1905; author of quinquennial review of education, 1897-8 to 1901-2.

NATHAN, WILLIAM, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1885, and posted to state rlys.; exec. engr., Sept., 1895; dep. consg. engr. for rlys., Burma, Dec., 1900 to Nov., 1901.

NAVROJI DORABJI KHANDALAVALA, B.A., LL.B. (special judge, Bombay).—Joined the service, 6th Nov., 1874, as subordinate judge; judge of small cause court, Poona, Feb., 1891; special judge under Deccan Agriculturists Relief Act, Jan., 1897.

NAWAZ KHAN, H. M. (see Hafiz Muhammad Nawaz Khan).

NAYLOR, JAMES RICHARD, C.S.I., late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 28th Oct., 1862, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. judge; remembrancer of legal affairs, July, 1876; acting chief sec. to govt., jud. &c. depts., 1879-80, and 1887; member of legisl. council, Bombay, Aug., 1884; temporary member of council, Bombay, in 1887 and 1888; C.S.I., May, 1890; ret'd., June, 1892; legal adviser to the Gaekwar of Baroda, Oct., 1892.

NAYUDU, D. R. (see Dharma Rao Nayudu).

NEALE, WALTER ERSKINE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 10th Oct., 1862, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr. and settl. officer; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1881; mag. and collr., Dec., 1881; commr. in Oudh, Feb., 1890; commr., Agra, Nov., 1893; acted as member of rev. board in 1894, and from Jan., 1895; commr., Meerut, May, 1896; ret'd., April, 1897.

NEATHAM, ALBERT HENRY JOHN (staff clerk, India Audit Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 22nd Feb., 1879; staff clerk, May, 1901.

NEAVE, EVERARD REGINALD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 21st Nov., 1900, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh., as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.

NEDHAM, EDWARD MONTGOMERIE, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army (cantt., mag., Neemuch).—

First commn., 9th Aug., 1873; cantt. mag., Neemuch, Dec., 1900.

NEDHAM, WILLIAM ALFRED, late Central Provs. Commn.—Joined the service as asst. commr., 12th July, 1864; served as asst. district supt. and district supt. of police, supt. of sch. of industry, and in 1874 in Bengal on famine duty; in 1882 was dep. supt. of census; judge of small cause court, Jabalpur, Sept., 1889; dep. commr., Dec., 1889; acting commr., April, 1897; confd., Jan., 1900; ret'd., Nov., 1901.

NEEDHAM, FRANCIS JACK, C.I.E., Bengal Police Dept. (dist. supt. of police and asst. pol. offr., Sadiya).—Entered the service, Dec., 1867, as dep. mag. and dep. collr.; asst. supt. of police, Jan., 1876; asst. pol. offr., Sadiya, Nov., 1882; dist. supt., and ditto, April, 1885; C.I.E., Dec., 1898; is author of a grammar of the Khampti language.

NEEL, EDMUND, C.I.E., late Public Works Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Victoria Coll., Jersey; India office writer, old establ., 3rd March, 1859; jun. clerk, mil. dept., 1860; asst. précis writer to the sec. of state, 1868-71; mil. dept., 1871; senr. clerk, Jan., 1872; funds dept., 1874; asst. sec. in the mil. dept., Aug., 1881; asst. sec. in the pol. and secret dept., 1885; offd. as sec., Jan. to May, 1887, Sept., 1889 to May, 1890, and June, 1890, to Jan., 1891; C.I.E., May, 1890; sec., public works dept., Aug., 1897; ret'd., Oct., 1901.

NEILL, JOHN WILLIAM, late Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at King's Coll., London, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 25th Nov., 1864, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr., registrar, jud. commr.'s court, and asst. sec. to chief commr.; sec., March, 1877; commr., June, 1880; fellow of the univ. of Calcutta, 1883; offd. as chief commr., Feb., 1887, and Nov., 1890; judicial commr., Jan., 1891; prest. of the commn. on the working of the Deccan agriculturists relief act, Dec., 1891; ret'd., April, 1894; apptd. lecturer on Indian law, Univ. Coll., London, July, 1895.

NEILL, LINDSAY, late Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at foreign schs., King's Coll., London, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 31st Oct., 1867, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt. of India, home dept., April, 1877; inspr.-gen. of registrn., supt. of stamps, and commr. of excise, Central Prov., Dec., 1877; dep. commr., June, 1880; addl. commr., Jan., 1883; sec. to the chief commr., March, 1883; commr., April, 1890; ret'd., Oct., 1894.

NELSON, ARTHUR EDWARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Cent. Prov.).—Educ. at Newcastle High Sch., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, Nov., 1899; served in the Cent. Prov. as asst. commr. and registrar, judl. commr.'s court.

NELSON, HENRY, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomson Coll.; apptd. to the dept., Oct., 1875, and posted to the N.W. Prov. and Oudh (irrigation); asst. engr., July, 1876; exec. engr., April, 1890.

NELSON, JOHN CLEMENT, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. settlmt. offr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and Trin. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 2nd Dec. 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., asst. commr., and asst. settlmt. offr.

NELSON, REGINALD ARBOUIN, M.A., LL.M., Barr. (1886), (principal, Law Coll., Madras).—Educ. at Clare Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar (Inner Temple), July, 1886; joined the service, 2nd May, 1891, as principal, law coll., Madras; also acting law reporter, April, 1894.

NELSON, WILLIAM HENRY, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Watson's Coll. and the Univ., Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1905, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

NESFIELD, JOHN COLLINSON, M.A., late Educ. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Educ. at Merton Coll., Oxford; from 22nd Jan., 1867, served in Bengal as profrr., Krishnagar coll. and at the presidency coll.; from May, 1872, served in Lower Burma as dir. of public instrn., and inspr. of schs.; transfed. to Oudh, March, 1874, as dir. of public instrn.; principal, Benares coll., May, 1878; inspr. of schs., Nov., 1879; fellow of the Allahabad univ., 1887; dir. of public instrn., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Aug., 1892; retd., Oct., 1894; author of 'An Essay on the Kanjar Tribe,' 1882, 'On the Results of Primary Educn. in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh,' 1883, and 'On the Tharu and Bagsha Tribes,' 1884; 'Brief View of the Caste System of the N.W. Prov. and Oudh,' 1885; 'Two Essays on the Mushera Tribe,' 1888, &c., &c.

NETHERSOLE, MICHAEL, India Public Works Dept. (suprg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1880, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Nov., 1892; lent to Kashmir state, 1893-1900; suprg. engr., Nov., 1903.

NEVILL, HENRY RIVERS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at the Charterhouse, and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 21st Nov., 1899, and served in the N.W. Provs. and Oudh, as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; engaged in revision of district gazeteers from Aug., 1901.

NEW, ROWLAND HILL, Indo-European Telegraph Dept. (engr. and electrician).—Joined the service, 1st June, 1867; asst. supt., April, 1891; supt., May, 1895; engr. and electrician, Dec., 1899.

NEWBERY, HENRY JAMES, B.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861; served in the Bengal rev. and jud. dept., and was apptd. mag. and collr., 1st grade, Dec., 1885; retd., April, 1887.

NEWBOULD, BABINGTON BENNETT, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Bedford Sch. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 28th Nov., 1888, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Assam as asst. commr., April, 1894; dep. commr., Oct., 1895; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1900.

NEWCOMBE, ALFRED CORNELIUS, B.A., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab and the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; served in the superior accts. establt., 1878-81 and 1886-90, and in Assam, 1881-86; services lent to Nizam's govt., 1891-95; transfed. to railway branch, Dec., 1896; transfed. to Hyderabad admn., Oct., 1900;

retd., Nov., 1902; author of 'Village, Town, and Jungle Life in India.'

NEWILL, JOSEPH HENRY, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army, late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 4th July, 1865; arrived, 25th Dec., 1865; served under the foreign dept. in the thagi and dakaiti branch, from May, 1872; cantonmt. mag., Jan., 1882; 2nd asst. to rest. at Hyderabad, March, 1884; from March, 1887, served as pol. agent at Bhopawar, and afterwards in S.E. Baluchistan; pol. officer in charge of Sardar Ayub Khan, Nov., 1889; acting pol. agent, Jhalawar, Nov., 1892; cantonmt. mag., Nasirabad, Feb., 1895; offg. rest., W. states of Rajputana, March, 1895; rest. at Gwalior, March, 1898; transfed. to U.S. list, Sept., 1899.

NEWMAN, JOHN HENRY, M.D., Col., late Indian Med. Service—First commn., 1867; served in the Abyssinian war, 1868, and Afghan war, 1878-79; civil surgn., Ajmir, April, 1879; offd. as agency surgn., &c., in Rajputana in 1884 and 1889; residency surgn. and chief med. officer in Rajputana, Aug., 1892; offg. inspr.-gen. of civil hospss., Bengal, July-Nov., 1893; administrative med. officer and sanitary commr., Central Prov., March, 1895; inspr.-gen. of civil hospss., Bengal, Nov., 1897; retd., July, 1900.

NEWMARCH, ALEXANDER, Finl. Dept., Govt. of India (dep.acctnt.-genl., United Prov.).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll.; entered the dept., 17th April, 1889; asst. comptr., India treasuries; July, 1891; asst. comptr., Burma, June, 1894; comptr., Assam, Sept., 1899; asst. acctnt.-gen. United Prov., Aug. 1902; offg. comptr., Hyderabad, Dec., 1902, to June, 1903; dep. acctnt.-genl., United Prov., Nov., 1904.

NEWMARCH, FRANCIS WELLES, B.A., India Office (asst. sec., finl. dept.).—Matriculated at Balliol Coll., Oxford, Jan., 1873; scholar of Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford, 1873-78; junr. clerk, mil. dept., India office, 18th May, 1877; senr. clerk, March, 1892; rest. clerk, 1885-94; sec. to Lord Onslow's departmental commn., 1896-97; asst. sec., finl. dept., July, 1902; compiled, jointly with Mr. Herbert, the India office cypher code.

NEWMARCH, LINDSAY SHERWOOD, Lieut.-Col. Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (pol. resdt. in Turkish Arabia).—Educ. at Haileybury and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; first commn., 13th Aug., 1879; from Feb., 1885, served as asst. agent at Indore, Ajmir, and Quetta; asst. rest., Mysore, Oct., 1889; first asst. to agent, Rajputana, Oct., 1892; and in same capacity, Central India, Aug., 1893; pol. agent, Bhopal, May, 1897; ditto, Sehore, Nov., 1899; pol. offr. with Sardar Ayub Khan, Oct., 1901; offg. pol. resdt. in Turkish Arabia, March, 1902; confd., March, 1903.

NEWMARCH, SIR OLIVER RICHARDSON, K.C.S.I., Major-Gen., late I.S.C.—Ensign, Bengal infantry, March, 1855; admitted to the Bengal staff corps, Feb., 1861; 3rd asst. sec. to govt. of India, mil. dept., 1871; dep. acctnt.-gen., mil. dept., 1875; acctnt.-gen. and ex-officio dep. sec. to the govt. of India, April, 1878; sec. to govt. of India, mil. dept., July, 1886; retd., Nov., 1887; C.S.I., Jan., 1888; sec., mil. dept., India office, May, 1889 to May, 1890; K.C.S.I., May, 1894.

NEWNHAM, ARTHUR TRISTRAM HERRERT, Major, Indian Army (cantonmt. mag., United

Prov.)—First commn., 22nd Jan., 1881; apptd. cantonmt. mag., Nasirabad, April, 1899; transfed. to United Prov., May, 1901.

NEWNHAM-SMITH, EDWARD DEEDES, Barr. (1887), Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Bombay Jud. Dept.—First commn., 27th Feb., 1869; served through the Afghan war, 1878–80 (medal and clasp); cantonmt. mag., Disa, Oct., 1887; and at Poona and Kirkee, Oct., 1888; ret'd., March, 1901.

NEWTON, JOHN, India Office (staff clerk, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 9th Oct., 1879; staff clerk, March, 1903.

NEWTON, WILLIAM GYLICH, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1876, and employed on state railways and in the Central Prov.; on famine duty in Madras, 1877; exec. engr., Jan., 1885; transfed. to Burma, Aug., 1887; suptg. engr., Aug., 1900.

NICHOLETTIS, HENRY SAMUEL, late Hyderabad Administration.—Arrived, 20th Jan., 1858, and served in the telegraph and police depts.; district supt. of police, Berar, 1868; asst. commr., May, 1872; judge, small cause court, Sept., 1882; dep. commr., Nov., 1888; ret'd., April, 1896.

NICHOLL, BERNARD VINCENT, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Beaumont Coll., Windsor, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 9th Nov., 1889, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Nov., 1897; mag. and collr., Nov., 1901; transfed. to Bengal, Dec., 1901; dist. and sess. judge, March, 1902.

NICHOLLS, GEORGE JASPER, LL.B., Barr. (1877), late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Exeter Gr. Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 29th Nov., 1864, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr. and in various acting appts.; transfed. temporarily to Bengal on famine duty, 1874–75; small cause court judge, Jabalpur, Sept., 1880; dep. commr., March, 1883; from July, 1883, served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as manager of the Awa state, and district and sessions judge; ret'd., June, 1895; author of 'The Law of the Central Prov.,' 'Digest of the Circulars and Standing Orders of the Chief Commissioner, Central Prov., 1862–80,' 'An Economic Gazetteer of the Central Prov.,' &c.

NICHOLSON, EUSTACE FERRERS (solicitor to govt., and public prosecutor, Bombay).—Offd. as solicitor to govt. and public prosecutor, May–Nov., 1897; confd., April, 1898.

NICHOLSON, SIR FREDERICK AUGUSTUS, K.C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at the Royal Med. Coll., Epsom, and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 4th Dec., 1869, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., acting sub-sec. and sec., board of rev., and principal asst.; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1889; collr. and mag., Oct., 1889; member of viceroy's legisl. council, Oct., 1897; acting resdt., Travancore and Cochin, Aug., 1898; C.I.E., June, 1899; member of rev. board, commr. of rev. settl. and dir. of land records and agric., Nov., 1899; ditto, commr. of land rev., and dir. of land records and agric., April, 1902; addl. member of viceroy's legisl. council, Oct., 1900; addl. member of legisl. council, Madras, April, 1902;

K.C.I.E., Jan., 1903; ret'd., March, 1904; compiled district manual of Coimbatore.

NICHOLSON, GEORGE FREDERICK, M.D., F.R.C.S.I., Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Service, Punjab.—Entered the service, March, 1878; in mil. employ till March, 1881, since when he has held various civil surgeoncies; civil surgn., Mooltan, Nov., 1897; ret'd. Feb. 1899.

NICHOLSON, SIR WILLIAM GUSTAVUS, K.C.B., Lieut.-Gen., R.E.—First commn., March, 1865; arrived, Dec., 1871; joined the public works dept., Feb., 1872, and served as asst. engr., Hyderabad, and in the Punjab irrig. branch; transfed. to mil. works branch, 1873; exec. engr., March, 1876; served in the Afghan war, 1878–80 (medal and 3 clasps, bronze star); sec. to defence committee, April, 1882; served in Egyptian expedi., 1882 (medal and clasp, bronze star, 4th class Osmanieh); ex-officio asst. adjutant-gen., 1885; suptg. engr., March, 1886; served in Burma expedi., 1886–87; mil. sec. to commander-in-chief, July, 1890, to April, 1893, when he reverted to the mil. works dept.; chief engr., April, 1894; dep. adjutant-gen., Punjab, April, 1895; adjt.-gen. in India, Nov., 1898; mil. sec. to field-marshal comdg. in South Africa, Dec., 1899; dir. of transport, S. Africa, Feb., 1900; dir.-gen. of mobilization and mil. intelligence, May, 1901; attached to Japanese Army in Manchuria, Feb., 1904, to March, 1905; quarter-mr.-genl., and third mil. member of Army Council, Dec., 1905.

NIGHTINGALE, WALTER HAWKINS, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd., 23rd July, 1868, and posted to Bengal; exec. engr., March, 1872; divl. supt., district works, 1882 and 1887; inspr. of local works in various divns., 1887–91; suptg. engr., March, 1892; sec. to chief commr. of Assam, public works dept., June, 1895, and again May, 1899; chief engr., Jan., 1899; sec. to govt. United Prov. public works dept., July, 1901; ret'd., Sept., 1903.

NILAMBARA MUKERJI, M.A., B.L.—Apptd. vice-chairman of the Calcutta corporation, Jan., 1896.

NISBET, JOHN, late Indian Forest Dept.—Apptd. to the dept. as asst. conservator, Dec., 1875, and served in Burma; dep. conservator, May, 1882; conservator, Feb., 1899; ret'd., Aug., 1900.

NISBET, ROBERT PARRY, C.I.E., Barr. (1872), Col., I.S.C., late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 20th Nov., 1859; from Aug., 1862, served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and asst. settl. officer; jud. asst., July, 1876; dep. commr., April, 1877; commr., Rawal Pindi, Nov., 1886; rest. in Kashmir, Nov., 1888; ret'd., May, 1892; author of Gujranwala settl. report.

NOBLE, CHARLES SIMEON, Col., I.S.C., late Oudh Commn.—Arrived, as ensign, Bengal native infantry, 14th Nov., 1858; from April, 1862, served in the police dept., Oudh, as asst. district supt., and district supt., and from June, 1866, in the commn. as asst. commr., and dep. commr.; ret'd., Nov., 1890.

NOEL-PATON, FREDERICK WALTER FERRIER, Dept. of Comm. and Industry.—Having held the appt. of sec. of the Bombay chamber of comm., was apptd. director-genl. of commercial intelligence, 27th Feb., 1905.

NOETLING, FRITZ, Ph.D., F.G.S.—Served as palaeontologist to the geological survey of India, Feb., 1887 to Oct., 1903.

NORFOR, CECIL HENRY THOMAS, late Madras Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 30th Aug., 1870; exec. engr., May, 1883; ret'd., Feb., 1900.

NORMAN, THOMAS, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Royal Sch., Enniskillen, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 21st Jan., 1863; served in Bengal, and was apptd. mag. and collr., 1st grade, Oct., 1886; ret'd., Dec., 1887.

NORTHCOATE (BARON), RIGHT HON. HENRY STAFFORD, G.C.M.G., G.C.I.E., C.B. (govr. gen. of Australia).—Educ. at Eton and Merton Coll., Oxford; priv. sec. to Lord Salisbury, 1876-77, and to chancellor of the exchequer, 1877-80; M.P. for Exeter, 1880-99; finl. sec. to War Office, 1885-86; surveyor-gen. of ordnance, 1886-87; created a baronet, 1887; apptd. govr. of Bombay, Feb., 1900, and created a Baron of the United Kingdom and G.C.I.E.; govr. gen. of Australia, Dec., 1903; G.C.M.G., 1904.

NORTHCOTT, JOHN PERCY CYRIL SALT-MARSH, India Office (2nd class clerk, political dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., 17th Dec., 1894; transf'd. to pol. dept., Nov., 1897.

NORTON, DAVID, C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Kingstown Sch., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 24th Nov., 1874, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; transf'd. for service in Burma as dep. commr., Jan., 1837; prest. of the Rangoon municipality, May, 1890; commr., April, 1891; C.S.I., Jan., 1898; member of legisl. council, Burma, Jan., 1900; ret'd., Sept., 1903.

NOYCE, FRANK, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Salisbury Sch. and St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived 1st Dec., 1902, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

NUGENT, JOHN, late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Stonyhurst Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 6th Nov., 1872, and served in Bengal, and the N.W. Prov. and Oudh, as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; senr. asst. commr., Kumaon, Jan., 1887; supt., Dehra Dun, May, 1888; dep. commr., July, 1891; mag. and collr., July, 1893; ret'd., Sept., 1899.

NUNN, ALBERT JAMES, India Office (2nd class clerk, Registry and Record Dept.).—Apptd. and class clerk, 20th May, 1905.

NUNN, HAROLD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Ripon Gr. Sch., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 6th Dec., 1897, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.

NURUL HUDA, SYUD, B.A., LL.M., Barr. (1880) Statutory Civilian (dist. and sess. judge, E. Bengal and Assam).—Joined the service 4th August, 1886, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1893; mag. and collr., Oct., 1897; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1897; transf'd. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

NYAYARATNA, M. C. (see Mahesh Chandra Nyayaratna).

OAKDEN, RALPH, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag., United Prov.).—Educ. at Cranbrook Gr. Sch., and at Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 16th Dec., 1895; and served in N.W.P. and Oudh as asst. mag.

and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., March, 1904.

OATES, EUGENE WILLIAM, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 26th July, 1867, and posted to Burma; exec. engr., Feb., 1877; suptg. engr., Dec., 1891; ret'd., March, 1899.

OBARD, OWEN JAMES, Major, Indian Army. Burma commn. (dep. commr.).—First commn., 20th Aug., 1885; joined the Burma commn., May, 1890, as asst. commr.; dep. commr., March, 1902.

OBARD, ROBERT, B.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 27th Dec., 1873, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; on famine duty in Madras, 1877-78; special asst. commr., Berar, March, 1887; dep. commr., July, 1890; civil and sessions judge, April, 1891; jud. commr., Oct., 1895; ret'd., Feb., 1902.

O'BRIEN, EDMUND CORNELIUS, Postal Dept., Govt. of India (inspr.-gen., railway mail service in India).—Entered the service, Oct., 1878; apptd. asst. dir.-gen. of Post Office, April, 1888; inspr.-gen., railway mail service in India, Jan., 1899.

O'BRIEN, JOSEPH, M.D., Lieut.-Col., late India Med. Service.—Joined the service, 1st April, 1870, and was in mil. employ until May, 1881; from that date served as civil surgn. and offg. profr., Calcutta med. coll.; apptd. profr. at the coll., and surgn. at the coll. hosp., April, 1892; ret'd., Aug., 1893.

O'BRIEN, PETER HENRY, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Malvern Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 6th Nov., 1882, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1891; transf'd. to Assam as dep. commr., Jan., 1892; mag. and collr., March, 1898.

O'CALLAGHAN, SIR FRANCIS LANGFORD, K.C.M.G., C.S.I., C.I.E., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept., June, 1862, as asst. engr., Central Prov.; exec. engr., April, 1866; transf'd. to the railway branch, 1870, and served as engr.-in-chief on various railway surveys; suptg. engr., Jan., 1880; C.I.E., May, 1883; C.S.I., Jan., 1888; chief engr., April, 1889; consulting engr. to govt. of India for state railways, May, 1889; sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., Aug., 1892; ret'd., July, 1894; K.C.M.G., 1902.

O'CALLAGHAN, IGNATIUS, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept., Feb., 1873; traffic supt., Rajputana state railway, Dec., 1874; gen. traffic manager, Rajputana-Malwa railway system, April, 1885; ret'd., 1896.

O'CONNELL, HUGH HAMLYN, Madras Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; entered the dept., Sept., 1878, and served as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; suptg. engr., March, 1904.

O'CONOR, JAMES EDWARD, C.I.E., M.I.S.I., late Finl. Dept., Govt. of India.—Registrar, dept. of agric. and commerce, June, 1873; asst. sec. in statistical branch of dept. of rev., agric., and commerce, Feb., 1875; asst. sec., dept. of fin. and commerce, Dec., 1879; on special duty in connection with the Melbourne exhibn., 1880-81; C.I.E., Jan., 1891; dir.-gen. of statistics, retaining his post as

asst. sec., May, 1895; retd., Oct., 1903; author of reports on the production of tobacco, vanilla, and lac, and on the cultivation of the ground nut, the carob tree, and the eucalyptus in India.

ODDIE, HENRY JAMES, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept. — Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. 1st Oct., 1876, and posted as asst. engr. to state rlys.; exec. engr., May, 1888; retd., May, 1903.

ODLING, CHARLES WILLIAM, C.S.I., M.E., M.I.C.E., fellow of Calcutta Univ., late India Public Works Dept. — Joined the service, 20th Oct., 1865; exec. engr., Sept., 1870; under sec. to govt. of Bengal, Feb., 1883; supt. engr., Nov., 1889; chief engr. for irrig. and joint sec. to govt. of Bengal, March, 1892; sec., irrig. branch, Jan., 1893; transfed. to N.W. Prov. and Oudh as chief engr. and sec. in buildings and roads branch, Dec., 1895; C.S.I., Jan., 1898; member of legisl. council of N.W. Prov. and Oudh., Feb., 1896, to Nov., 1900; retd., May, 1902.

O'DONNELL, CHARLES JAMES, M.P., M.A., late Indian C.S. — Educ. at St. Ignatius' Coll., and Queen's Coll., Galway; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 14th Oct., 1872, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on famine duty, June, 1874; asst. to dir.-gen. of statistics, Feb., 1875; joint mag., July, 1884; mag. and collr., Aug., 1889; provl. supt. of census, Bengal, 1890-91; commr., Dec., 1898; retd., April, 1900; M.P. for Walworth, Jan., 1906.

O'DONNELL, GEORGE BOODRIC, B.A., LL.B., Lieut.-Col., Indian Army, Political Dept., Bombay (pol. agent). — First commn., 18th Jan., 1880; on mily. duty till Feb., 1887, when he offd. as asst. pol. agent, Mahi Kantha; confd., March, 1889; manager of Malia State, Kathiawar, Sept., 1892; asst. pol. agent, Kolhapur, June, 1896; ditto, South Mahratta country, Feb., 1900; administrator of Radhanpur, July, 1900; ditto, Idar, Dec., 1901; asst. pol. agent, Kathiawar, April, 1902; pol. agent, Savantvadi, May, 1903, to Aug., 1904.

O'DONNELL, SAMUEL PERRY, Indian C.S. (asst. supt., Dehra Dun). — Educ. at Repton, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1897, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, Dec., 1903.

O'DONOGHUE, WILLIAM FREEMAN, India Public Works Dept. (exmr. of accounts). — Apptd. acctnt. in subord. accts. estab., pub. wks. dept., 4th April, 1873; asst. exmr., Sept., 1876; dep. exmr., July, 1880; exmr., May, 1886.

O'DWYER, MICHAEL FRANCIS, B.A., Indian C.S. (rev. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov.). — Educ. at St. Stanislaus Coll., Tullamore, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after open exam. of 1882; arrived, 19th Nov., 1885, and was apptd. asst. commr. in the Punjab; settlt. collr., 1889-94; dir. of dept. of land records and agric., Punjab, Dec., 1896; settlt. officer for Ulwar and Bhurtpore, July, 1897; special duty in connection with the scheme of the new frontier, April, 1901; dep. commr., July, 1901; rev. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Nov., 1901.

OERTEL, FREDRICK OSCAR, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.). — Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. apprentice engr.,

10th May, 1883, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh.; asst. engr., Nov., 1883; exec. engr., Sept., 1896.

O'FARRELL, HORACE HANDLEY, late Indian C.S. (Madras). — Educ. at Westminster; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1875, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and joint mag., Dec., 1888; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1891; collr. and mag., Jan., 1894; offg. judge, high court, April, 1899; retd., March, 1902.

OGDEN, WILLIAM, India Public Works Dept. (examiner). — Apptd. to Indian Tel. Dept., July, 1868; transfed. to acents. estabt., public works dept. as accent., 6th Oct., 1873; asst. exmr., Sept., 1881; dep. exmr., Oct., 1882; exmr., Aug., 1889.

OGILVIE, GEORGE MACARTNEY, late Indian C.S. (Punjab). — Educ. at Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1863, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and jud. asst.; dep. commr., Oct., 1881; commr. and supt., Nov., 1888; in 1893-94 and 1894-95 acted as finl. commr.; retd., Jan., 1896.

O'HARA, WILLIAM, Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (inspr.-gen. of prisons, Madras). — Joined the service, 30th Sept., 1874; arrived, 18th April, 1875, and served in Madras under the mil. dept., and as civil-surgn. and jail supt. at various stations; district med. and sanitary officer, Trichinopoli, May, 1895; inspr.-gen. of prisons, Nov., 1899.

O'KELLY, HILARY MARTIN, B.A., M.I.E.E., late India Telegraph Dept. — Joined the service as asst. supt., 21st June, 1866; arrived, 14th Sept., 1866; supt., Jan., 1886; offg. dir., traffic branch, July-Oct., 1894; retd., May, 1898.

O'KINEALY, PETER, Barr. (1876) (advocate genl., Bengal). — Called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, 1876; standing counsel, Calcutta high court, 19th April, 1895 to 1st April, 1903; offg. advocate-genl., Bengal, April, 1904; confd. July, 1904.

OLDFIELD, FRANCIS DU PRÉ, Indian C.S. (sub collr. and joint mag., Madras). — Educ. at Marlborough and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 11th Nov., 1890, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Nov., 1897; sub. collr. and joint mag., June, 1904.

OLDFIELD, SIR RICHARD CHARLES, Kt., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh). — Apptd. from Haileybury, 1848; arrived, 9th Nov., 1848; served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; asst. sec. to govt., and asst. acctnt., N.W. Prov., March, 1856; mag. and collr., Farakhabad, March, 1864; from May, 1869, served in the jud. dept., and was apptd. civil and sessions judge, Jan., 1872; addl. judge, high court, N.W. Prov., Dec., 1874; puisne judge, high court, Nov., 1882; retd., March, 1887.

OLDHAM, ABEL JOHN, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept. — Apptd., 1st March, 1873, and posted to Bengal; employed in Madras on famine relief works, 1877-78; and in N.W. Prov. and Oudh on public works accts., 1881-82; exec. engr., Aug., 1881; transfed. to state railways, Sept., 1886; offg. dep. consulting engr. for railways, Calcutta, 1890; and at Lucknow, 1892-94; public works under sec. to govt. of Bengal, 1891-92; retd., Oct., 1895.

OLDHAM, CHARLES EVELYN ARBUTHNOT WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Galway Gr. Sch., and, Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 12th Dec., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec., jud., &c., depts., March, 1895; joint mag. and dep. collr., July, 1898; mag. and collr., Sept., 1900; K.-i-H. medal, June, 1902.

OLDHAM, FRANCIS GEORGE, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., Dec., 1858; joined the dept., Feb., 1873; comptr. of accts., Hyderabad, April, 1875; examr. of guaranteed railway accts., March, 1878; examr. of telegraph accts., 1883; examr. of accts., Allahabad, Aug., 1885; examr. of accts., mil. works, Jan., 1887; ret'd., Jan., 1892.

OLDHAM, RICHARD DIXON, A.R.S.M., F.G.S. late Geological Survey of India.—Joined the survey, 6th Nov., 1879, as asst. supt.; dep. supt., Sept., 1885; on special duty in connection with Tibet mission, 1886; supt., Oct., 1891; ret'd., May, 1904; has revised the 'Manual of the Geology of India,' and contributed largely to the survey publns.; author of a report on the Assam earthquake of June 12, 1897.

OLDHAM, WILLIAM BENJAMIN, C.I.E., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Kingstown Gr. Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1865, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1874; dep. commr., Nov., 1879; on famine relief duty, 1866 and 1874, and at Bellary, 1877-79; mag. and collr., Aug., 1888; commr. of Chittagong, Oct., 1891; C.I.E., Jan., 1893; member of rev. board, Dec., 1898; ret'd., Nov., 1900.

OLIPHANT, JOHN S., late Funds Dept., India Office.—East India house writer, old establ., 22nd June, 1853; junr. clerk, minuting branch, 1855; private sec. to chairman and dep. chairman, 1857-58; India office, secretariat dept., 1859; asst. private sec. and précis writer to the sec. of state, 1863-65; senr. clerk, funds dept., April, 1866; asst. sec., mil. dept., 1872, but continued doing duty in funds dept.; also agent to administrators-gen. of India, 1877; acted as jud. and public sec. in 1879; dir. of funds, July, 1879; ret'd., Dec., 1893.

OLIVER, ARTHUR KNAPP (master and registrar in equity, Bombay high court).—Joined the service, Jan., 1876, as deputy registrar; asst. regr., May, 1877; asst. commr., Sept., 1893; officiated as master and registrar in equity, April, 1897; confid., Jan., 1896.

OLIVER, GILBERT THOMAS INGLIS, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as apprentice engineer, Oct., 1878, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; asst. engr., Feb., 1879; transf. to Burma, June, 1892; exec. engr. Sept., 1892.

OLIVER, JAMES WILLIAM, late India Forest dept.—Arrived, 4th Dec., 1874, and was apptd. asst. conservator; dep. conservator, July, 1880; conservator, May, 1891; dir. of forest school, Dehra Dun, Aug., 1896; ret'd., Dec., 1904.

OLIVIER, HENRY DACRES, Col., R.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Entered the service, 15th Dec., 1871; joined the dept., 1st June, 1875; employed in Baluchistan, 1878-80; exec. engr., and dep. consulting engr. for railways, Bombay, March,

1884; employed on Soudan railway, 1885; under sec. to govt., public works dept., 1889-91; agent to the Bombay, Baroda, and Central India railway co., Aug., 1894; suptg. engr., Sept., 1900; ret'd., April, 1904.

OLLIVANT, SIR EDWARD CHARLES KAYLL, K.C.I.E., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Marlborough; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 21st Nov., 1868, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; on special duty at Baroda in connection with estate of Mir Zulfikar Ali, Jan., 1879; asst. commr., branch inspr.-gen. of registrn. in Sind, and Sind translr. to govt., Sept., 1880; also dep. supt. of census in Sind, Jan., 1881; municipal commr. for Bombay, May, 1881; on special duty in England in this connection, 1883; C.I.E., May, 1888; pol. agent and collr. of stamp rev., Kathiawar, April, 1889; K.C.I.E., May, 1892; offd. as commr. in Sind, April, 1895; to March, 1896; offd. as chief sec. to govt. of Bombay, March to July, 1895; member of council, Bombay, April, 1897; ret'd., April, 1902.

OLPHERT, HUGH STEWART, India Telegraph Dept. (superintendent).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. supt., 26th Sept., 1880; supt., Sept., 1896.

O'MALLEY, LEWIS SIDNEY STEWARD, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at K. Edward's Sch., Norwich and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 7th Dec., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., gen. and rev. depts., July, 1903; supt. of gaz. revision, Feb., 1905.

OMMANNEY, EDWARD LACON, C.S.I., Col., Indian Army, late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 12th June, 1855; asst. to commr. in charge of ex-king of Delhi and state prisoners, Sept., 1857; took ex-king of Delhi and family to Rangoon, and served in Pegu commn., 1858-59; served subsequently in the Punjab as asst. commr., personal asst. and dep. commr.; commr. and supt., Derajat, Dec., 1883; on special duty with Hazara field force, Nov., 1888; C.S.I., May, 1889; reverted to mil. duty, 1891.

OMMANNEY, HENRY TRAVERS, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 12th Nov., 1870, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., railway mag., cantonmt. mag., and second asst.; acting under sec. to govt., rev., &c., depts., April, 1886; and acting inspr.-gen. of police, May, 1888; junr. collr. and mag., Nov., 1891; senr., Feb., 1895; member of hemp drugs commn., 1893-94; ret'd., Oct., 1896.

ONSLLOW (EARL OF), RIGHT HON. WILLIAM HILLIER ONSLLOW, G.C.M.G.—Born in 1853; educ. at Eton and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. parly. under sec. of state for the colonies, Feb., 1887, and again, Nov., 1900; acted as vice-prest. of the colonial conference, 1887, and as a delegate to the conference on sugar bounties; K.C.M.G., 1887; parly. sec., board of trade, Feb., 1888; govr. and commr.-in-chief, New Zealand, 1889-92; G.C.M.G., 1889; parly. under sec. of state for India, July, 1895 to Nov., 1900; minister of agric., May, 1903 to Feb., 1905; chairman of committees in House of Lords, 1905.

OPPENHEIM, ERNEST FERDINAND, B.A., Barr. (1899), Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Rugby Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of

1898; arrived, 5th Dec., 1899, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

OPPERT, GUSTAV, Ph.D., late Educ. Dept., Madras.—Profr. of Sanscrit, presidency coll., and curator of govt. oriental manuscript library, 14th Nov., 1872; fellow of Madras univ.; 1873; Telugu translr. to govt., Sept., 1878; retd., April, 1893.

ORANGE, HUGH WILLIAM, C.I.E. (Director-gen. of Education).—Educ. at Winchester Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; junior examr., Board of Education, London, 1893-1902; apptd. director-gen. of education in India, 6th March, 1902; C.I.E., Jan., 1906.

ORMOND, ERNEST W., Barr. (1887) (judge, Calcutta small cause court).—After serving temporarily in 1891-92 as asst. sec. to the govt. of Bengal, legisl. dept., was apptd. to act as 2nd judge, Calcutta small cause court, March, 1893; confd., Dec., 1894.

ORMROD, JOHN BAXTER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 16th Nov., 1899, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.

ORR, ADOLPHE ERNEST, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1883, and posted to the Punjab; exec. engr., March, 1897; and sec. to govt., buildings and roads, May, 1904.

ORR, JAMES PETER, Indian C.S. (junr. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Hurstpierpoint, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 12th Oct., 1889, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and forest settl. officer; and asst., March, 1896; 1st asst., Sept., 1897; acting under sec., rev. and finl. depts., Nov., 1897; acting collr. and mag. Apr., 1898; K-I-H. medal, May, 1900; Junr. collr. and mag., March, 1905.

ORR, SUTHERLAND, Judl. Dept., Madras (govt. solicitor).—Offd. as govt. solicitor, April to Dec., 1903; confd. in that appt., 1 Feb., 1904.

ORR, THOMAS CHALMERS, Bengal Police Dept. (dist. supt. of police).—Apptd. offg. asst. supt. of police, 15th Feb., 1876; confd., April 1883; served in the transport dept., 1880-1882; dist. supt. of police, Nov., 1889.

OSBORN, WILLIAM, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Bombay Public Works Dept.—First commn., 18th Dec., 1861; asst. engr., Nov., 1864; served in the Abyssinian campaign, 1867 (medal); exec. engr., Jan., 1869; suptg. engr., Jan., 1893; retd., May, 1894.

O'SULLIVAN, DANIEL, India Office (second class clerk, registry and record dept.).—Apptd. clerk, and class, registry and record dept., 18th Nov., 1901.

OTTEWILL, WILLIAM THOMAS, India Office (second-class clerk, registry and record dept.).—Clerk, and class, 12th Sept., 1898.

OTTLEY, SIR JOHN WALTER, K.C.I.E., Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept. (presdt., Royal Indian Engrg. Coll.).—First commn., Jan., 1864; arrived, Nov., 1868; joined the dept., Feb., 1870; asst. to chief engr. for irrign., Punjab, April, 1870; exec. engr., Sept., 1872; Punjab to chief engr. for irrign., Bengal, Feb., 1874; re-transfd. to Punjab, irrign. branch, June, 1875; asst. sec., irrign. branch, Punjab, July,

1881; supt. of works, Bari Doab circle, April, 1882; suptg. engr., Dec., 1884; chief engr. and joint sec. to govt. of Punjab, public works dept., Nov., 1890; C.I.E., May, 1892; sec., irrign. branch, Sept., 1892; inspr.-gen. of irrign. and dep. sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., Oct., 1894; retd. from the dept., 1896; served in the campaign on the N.W. frontier in 1897-98 (medal and two clasps); retd. from the army, July, 1898; apptd. presdt. Royal Indian Engrg. Coll., Cooper's Hill, Oct., 1899; K.C.I.E., June, 1904.

OUNG, H. (see Hla Oung).

OWEN, EDWARD, India Office (staff clerk, acctn.-gen.'s dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 17th Feb., 1872; staff clerk, July, 1896; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, Jan., 1900.

OWEN, RICHARD, Lieut.-Col., late 21st Lancers (mil. sec. to govr. of Bombay).—First commn., 12th Nov., 1873; A.D.C. to commander-in-chief in India, Feb., 1882, to April, 1886; A.D.C. to govr. of Jamaica, March, 1887, to Dec., 1888; mil. sec. to govr. of Bombay from Feb. 1905.

OWEN, WILLIAM, M.D., F.R.C.S., Lieut.-Col., Indian Medical Service, late Bengal Opium Dept.—Joined the service, 31st March, 1877, and was employed under the mil. dept. and as second med. officer, Port Blair; from 24th May, 1882, served in Bengal as supt. of vaccn., def. sanitary commr. and civil surgn.; principal asst. to opium agent, Behar, April, 1889; factory supt., Behar, May, 1891; retd., March, 1903.

OWENS, JOHN, Postal Dept. (presy. postmr., Calcutta).—Entered the service, June, 1868; inspr. of post offices, April, 1873; supt., Sept., 1881; presy. postmr., Bombay, Feb., 1892; ditto Calcutta, April, 1893.

OZZARD, HUDSON HENRY, Major, I.S.C. First commn., 13th June, 1874; arrived, 1st Nov., 1874, and was employed on mil. duty, including service in Perak expdn. (medal and clasp) and Afghan war, 1878-79 (medal); acting cantonmt. mag., Feb., 1888; confd., April, 1890; retd., April, 1899.

PADDISON, GEORGE FREDERICK, B.A., Indian C.S. (spcl. settlmnt. offr., Madras).—Educ. at Richmond, Yorks., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 6th Dec., 1897, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; spcl. settl. offr., Sept., 1902.

PAGE, HORACE, M.A., Indian C.S. (1st asst., Bombay).—Educ. at St. Olave's G. Sch., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 19th Dec., 1893, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; and asst., April, 1900; ditto, and joint judge and sessions judge, Sept., 1901; 1st asst., Sept., 1904.

PAGE, WILLIAM HUMPHREY, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Nottingham Gr. Sch., and Winchester Coll., apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 10th Dec., 1868, and served in Bengal; joint mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1879; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1885; retd., April, 1891.

PAGET, HENRY EDWARD CLARENCE, late Bengal Police Dept.—Joined the service, 10th July, 1878, as offg. asst. supt. of police; confd., May, 1882; dist. supt., Oct. 1890 to Nov. 1903.

PAINTER, HAROLD LISCOMBE, B.A., Indian C.S. (and asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Clifton Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after

exam. of 1894; arrived, 9th Dec., 1895, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., March, 1904.

PAL, J. N. (see Jogindra Nath Pal).

PAL, S. N. (see Sri Nath Pal).

PALIT, L. N. (see Lokendra Nath Palit).

PALLISER, HARRY GEORGE, M.I.C.E., late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Educ. at Chiswick Sch.; joined the dept., 28th Oct., 1867; exec. engr., Nov., 1872; suptg. engr., Feb., 1894; chief engr., June, 1899; ret'd., Dec., 1903.

PALMER, ARTHUR LAURENCE HAMILTON, M.I.E.E., India Telegraph Dept. (dep. director, traffic branch).—Trained at R. I. E. Coll.; apptd. asst. supt., Sept., 1878; supt., Feb., 1894; chief supt., March, 1903; dep. director, traffic branch, July, 1904; has received India medal, with 'Burma, 1885-87' clasp.

PALMER, CHARLES EDGAR, Indian C.S. (2nd asst., Bombay).—Educ. at the Mathematical Sch., Rochester, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 5th Dec., 1896, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., asst. judge and addl. sess. judge; 2nd asst., Dec., 1904.

PALMER, CHARLES GEORGE, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 7th Oct., 1868, and employed on irrign. works in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., May, 1876; suptg. engr., Jan., 1898; C.I.E., Jan., 1898; chief engr., and sec. to govt., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, public works dept., Aug., 1900; ret'd., Oct., 1902.

PALMER, SIR ELWIN MITFORD, K.C.B., K.C.M.G., late Finl. Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the finl. dept., Nov., 1871; asst. to acctnt.-gen., British Burma, Dec., 1873; transid. to Bombay, April, 1876; asst. to compt. -gen., Oct., 1878; served under the Egyptian govt., 1878-79; from Jan., 1880, served in Madras as offg. asst. and dep. acctnt.-gen.; compt. -Hyderabad, Dec., 1882; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Nov., 1884; asst. compt. -gen., Dec., 1884; transid. for service under Egyptian govt., Aug., 1885; K.C.B., Jan., 1897; died, Jan., 1906.

PANDE, R. A. (see Ram Autar Pande).

PANDURANG RAO, VOMBATKERE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Govt. Coll., Mangalore, Madras Univ., and St. John's College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 19th Dec., 1904, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

PANIOTY, CONSTANTINE DEMETRIUS, Barr. (1884), (judge, small cause court, Calcutta).—Joined the service 22nd March, 1888, as offg. reporter, high court, Calcutta; confd., Nov., 1889; offg. regr., small cause court, Calcutta, Oct., 1892; confd., Oct., 1895; offg. judge, small cause court, Calcutta, Jan., 1896; confd., April, 1902.

PANK, PHILIP DURRELLI, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (resdy. surgn., Jaipur).—First commn., 31st March, 1880; civil surgn., Bikanir, Jan., 1886; resdy. surgn., Meywar, March, 1893; ditto, Jaipur, June, 1898.

PANSE, G. D. (see Ganpatrao Damodhar Panse).

PANTIN, ROBERT GERALD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr. Central Prov.).—Educ. at Balfield Coll., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 21st Dec., 1904, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr. and acting under sec.

PANTON, EDWARD BROOKS HENDERSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Dulwich, and at Christ's Coll., Cambridge, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

PARAKH, D. N. (see Dhanjisha Navroj Parakh).

PARASHRAM KRISHNA CHITALE, L.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Joined the service 7th March, 1885, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., July, 1897.

PARGITER, EDWIN HERMANN, India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., May, 1883; suptg. engr., Dec., 1902.

PARGITER, FREDERICK EDEN, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high court, Calcutta).—Educ. at Taunton Coll. Sch., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 6th Nov., 1875; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., settlt. officer, commr. of the Sundarbans, and offg. under sec.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1887; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1893; offg. judge, high court, Calcutta, April, 1903, and March, 1904; confd., June, 1904; author of 'The Revenue History of the Sundarbans from 1765 to 1870,' and of a transln. of the Markandeya Purana.

PARKER, AUBREY HASTINGS, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., Melbourne Univ., and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 13th Dec., 1897, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

PARKER, EDWIN WOODALL, late Registrar of Chief Court, Punjab.—From 20th Oct., 1863, served in the Punjab as extra asst. commr., and small cause court judge; district judge, Simla, May, 1887, to Jan., 1888; registrar of chief court, Oct., 1888; author of 'Outlines of the Constitutional Law of India,' and 'Outlines of Jurisprudence'; ret'd., May, 1900.

PARKER, JOSEPH, C.S.I., late Dir.-Gen. of Stores, India Office.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; East India house writer, old establ., 9th July, 1851; junr. clerk, marine dept., Aug., 1858; store dept., 1860; senr. clerk, Nov., 1866; asst. to dir.-gen. of stores, Jan., 1890; dir.-gen. of stores, June, 1891; ret'd., Aug., 1896.

PARKER, WILLIAM JACKSON, Col., Indian Army, late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 16th Dec., 1858; from July, 1867, served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and jud. asst.; district judge, Nov., 1884; dep. commr., Sept., 1887; divl. judge, Oct., 1891; reverted to mil. dept., Nov., 1894.

PARKES, BENJAMIN, M.I.C.E., M.E., F.C.H., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1874, and posted to state railways; transfld. to Punjab provl. establ., Jan., 1879; exec. engr., Oct., 1881; transfld. to Bengal as suptg. engr., July, 1899; offg. chief engr., and sec. to chief commr., Central Prov., from Dec., 1903; chief engr., April, 1905; ret'd., Sept., 1905.

PARKIN, HENRY, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army (dep. inspr.-gen. of mil. police, Burma).—First commn., 11th May, 1878; served in the Zhoob expdn., 1884, and in Burma, 1886-87 and 1887-89 (medal); asst. dir. of police supply and clothing, Burma, 15th Aug., 1888; dep. inspr.-gen. of police for supply and clothing, June,

1890; dep. inspr.-gen. of mil. police, Dec., 1896; offg. inspr.-gen., April, 1901, to April, 1902.

PARLETT, LEONARD MONTAGUE, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Bradford Coll., and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 21st Dec., 1891, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and settl. officer; dep. commr., Jan., 1905.

PARRATT, CHARLES, India Office (clerk, Indo-European telegraph, Public Works dept.).—Apptd. to the London office of the Indo-European telegraph dept. as clerk, 26th March, 1881; transf. to India office, 1st Jan., 1889.

PARROTT, BENJAMIN ALEXANDER NAPIER, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Burma Commn.—First commn., July, 1868; arrived, Nov., 1868; served in the Lushai expdn., 1871; from April, 1872, served in the Burma commn. as asst. commr., asst. town mag., and settl. and boundary officer; dep. commr., Feb., 1886; commr., Oct., 1894; ret'd., May, 1898.

PARRY, R., late Educl. Service, Bengal.—Joined the service, 14th Jan., 1865; served as head-master, profr., and offg. principal at various colls., and as inspr. of schools (temporary); ret'd., 1890.

PARRY, ROBERT, Indian C.S. (dist. judge, Burma).—Educ. at Bedford Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 15th Nov., 1892, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dist. judge, March, 1905.

PARSONS, CLEMENT GEORGE, Major, Indian Army (dep. commr., Punjab).—Arrived 28th April, 1880; apptd. to Punjab commn. as asst. commr., Sept., 1885; dep. commr., April, 1896; inspr.-gen. of registration, commr. of excise, and supt. of stamps, Oct., 1898, to Nov., 1903.

PARSONS, EDWARD REID, Police Dept., United Prov. (dep. inspr.-gen.).—Entered the service, 17th July, 1875; offg. asst. dist. supt., June, 1878; confd., Feb., 1881; dist. supt., July, 1885; dep. inspr.-gen., March, 1901.

PARSONS, FRANCIS COTTON, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Amer-sham Hall Sch., Reading, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 30th Jan., 1880, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; special settl. officer, July, 1894; head asst. coll. and mag., Aug., 1897; special settl. officer, Malabar, Jan., 1898; head asst. collr., S. Canara, 29th May, 1901; sub.-collr. and joint mag., Aug., 1902; collr. and mag., Oct., 1904.

PARSONS, HENRY JAMES, Barr. (1877), late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Eton, and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 21st Nov., 1867, and served in Bombay as asst. mag. and collr., asst. judge, and judge and sessions judge; acted as jud. commr. and judge of the sadar court in Sind, in 1881-82, and again in 1887; judge of high court, Nov., 1887; acted as chief justice for several periods since June, 1898; ret'd., 1900.

PARSONS, OCTAVIUS SYDNEY, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 28th Nov., 1891, and served in Burma as asst. commr., cantonmt. mag., small cause judge, and settl. officer; dep. commr., April, 1903.

PARTHASARATHI AIYANGAR, M.O., M.A., M.L. (judge, small cause court, Madras).—Apptd. judge, court of small causes, Madras, April, 1896.

PARTRIDGE, JOHN GEORGE DENMAN, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Sherborne, and at Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 15th Jan., 1887, and served in Madras as asst. to mag. and collr.; senr. asst., Ganjam, Aug., 1893; sub.-collr. and joint mag., May, 1898; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1902; collr., dist. mag. and govt. agent, Ganjam, Dec., 1903.

PARTRIDGE, WILLIAM REGINALD, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, Sherborne Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1883, and served in Oudh as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., May, 1888; senr. asst. commr., Garhwal, Nov., 1890; dep. commr., Oct., 1891; also settl. officer, Nov., 1893; joint mag., Nov., 1894; dep. commr., Nov., 1895; mag. and collr., April, 1896; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1901; mag. and collr., Oct., 1902.

PASSMORE, JOHN REGINALD JUTSUM, India Office (and class clerk, acct.-general's dep.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 10th June, 1899.

PATAK, P. H. (see Pestonji Hormasji Patak).

PATCH, JAMES, late Bengal Police Dept.—Joined the service, 17th May, 1863, as asst. supt. of police; transf. to Assam, Jan., 1869; district supt., Sept., 1870; served with Lushai expdn., 1871-72; returned to Bengal, July, 1884; ret'd., April, 1894.

PATE, HENRY REGINALD, Indian C.S. (asst., collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 30th Nov., 1904, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

PATEL, R. M. (see Rustamji Merwanji Patel).

PATERSON, GEORGE ALFRED, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Blair-lodge Sch., Univ. Coll., London, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 30th Oct., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; dep. supt. of family domains of Maharaja of Benares, Sept., 1899; asst. sess. judge, March, 1903; asst. commr., Jan., 1905.

PATTABHIRAMA RAO, NEMALI, B.A. (diwan, Cochin State).—Entered the Madras rev. settlmt. dept., 15th April, 1882; asst. commr., March, 1895; diwan, Cochin State, Aug., 1902.

PATTERSON, ALEXANDER BLEAKLEY, C.I.E., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Belfast Royal Acad. Inst., and Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 20th Dec., 1863, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., settl. officer, and joint mag.; mag. and collr., Dec., 1883; acting commr. of Jhansi, July-Oct., 1890; commr. of N. India salt rev., April, 1891; C.I.E., May, 1895; ret'd., Dec., 1898; author of Fatehpur and Karwi settl. reports.

PATTERSON, DAVID CLARKE, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Oundle Sch. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 10th Dec., 1903, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

PATTERSON, WILLIAM HENRY, late Survey of India Dept.—Joined the service, 1st Dec., 1851;

served as a volunteer during the Mutiny; asst. supt., Jan., 1883; dep. supt., Feb., 1889; ret'd., Sept., 1893.

PATTLE, ARTHUR JOHN, India Office (junior clerk, acct.-gen.'s dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 17th Feb., 1872; staff clerk, July, 1896; junior clerk, April, 1902.

PATUCK, PESTONJEE SORABJEE, B.A., Barr. (1898), Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Cent. Prov.).—Educ. at Elphinstone Coll., Bombay, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 12th Dec., 1898, and served in the Cent. Prov. as asst. commr.

PAUL, ALFRED WALLIS, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Clifton Coll. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1870, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and acting inspr.-gen. of registrn.; on special duty in connection with the Thibet mission, April, 1886; dep. commr., Aug., 1887; C.I.E., Jan., 1889; on special duty on Thibetan frontier, April, 1890; British commr. for Anglo-Chinese convention regarding Sikkim and Thibet, Feb., 1891; mag. and collr., May, 1891; on special duty at Darjiling, March, 1892; ret'd., Dec., 1895.

PAUL, JOSIAH EDWARD, late Madras Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1875; asst. to chief engr., and under sec. to govt., irrign. branch, in 1880; exec. engr., June, 1886; ret'd., Dec., 1900.

PAYNE, CHARLES FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (dep. chairman, Calcutta Corp'n.).—Educ. at Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 6th Dec., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; dep. chairman, Calcutta corp'n., Dec., 1904.

PEACOCK, EDWARD BOVILL, late Punjab Judicial Dept.—Joined the service, 23rd April, 1862; apptd. asst. commr. 25th Feb., 1867; offd. as judge of small cause court in 1874 and 1875; district judge, April, 1889; ret'd., March, 1899.

PEACOCK, HENRY BARNES, Major, Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (pol. agent, Malwa).—First commn., 13th Oct., 1886; asst. commr., Merwara, May, 1895; asst. to govr.-genl.'s agent in Rajputana, June, 1898; asst. pol. agent, Banswara and Pertabgarh, July, 1898; asst. resdt. in Meywar, Aug., 1898; pol. agent and asst. commr., Quetta-Pishin, Dec., 1901; asst. commr., Ajmer, March, 1902; pol. agent, Malwa, Feb., 1905.

PEACOCK, HENRY PHIPSON, Major-Gen., late Pol. Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Bengal cavalry, Sept., 1856; served during the Mutiny (medal), and subsequently with the govr.-gen. s body guard; acted as agent to the govr.-gen. with King of Oudh, and supt. of pol. pensions, on several occasions; pol. agent, Murshidabad, 1878; pol. agent, Alwar, 1882; acting resd., W. Rajputana, March, 1886; offg. rest. at Jaipur and offg. agent in Central India, May, 1888; pol. rest. in Turkish Arabia, Nov., 1891; rest., Jaipur, May, 1892; reverted to mil. dept., March, 1894.

PEARS, STEUART DURAND, Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1880; exec. engr., Jan., 1894; presdt., Madras municip. commn., Dec., 1902.

PEARS, STEUART EDMUND, Indian C.S. (pol. agent, N.W. Frontier Prov.).—Educ. at Edin-

burgh Univ. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 7th Dec., 1898, and served in the Punjab and N.W. Frontier Prov. as asst. commr.; pol. agent, Tochi, March, 1905.

PEARS, THOMAS CALDWELL, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (resident, Kashmir).—First commn., 29th May, 1872; served in the Afghan war, 1878-79, and with the Thal Chotiali field force (medal); on special pol. duty in Afghanistan in 1880; apptd. asst. commr., Merwara, Nov., 1882; served in the thagi dept. in Rajputana, and on boundary duty on the Mewar and Tonk border in 1884; pol. asst., March, 1886; settlt. officer under the Tonk durbar, Nov., 1886; pol. agent, Nov., 1891; settlt. officer, Tonk, Dec., 1891; pol. agent, Alwar, March, 1893, Johdpur, March, 1898; offg. resdt., Gwalior, May, 1899; resdt., Nepal, June, 1901; ditto, Jaipur, April, 1903; offg. resdt. in Kashmir, Mar., 1905; confd., Oct., 1905.

PEARSE, HERBERT GEORGE, Barr. (1878), late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Rugby, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 7th Nov., 1871, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and asst. commr.; judge, small cause court, Allahabad, Dec., 1887; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1890; ret'd., May, 1898.

PEARSON, CHARLES, M.A., late Educl. Dept., Punjab.—Served in the dept. from March, 1861, as prof. and principal, Agra coll.; inspr. of schs., Nov., 1865; offg. dir. of public instrn. in 1873, 1876, and 1883; ret'd., March, 1886.

PEARSON, JAMES RAE, Indian C.S. (joint mag., United Prov.).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 10th Nov., 1892, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., May, 1898; dep. commr., Mar., 1900.

PEARSON, TINDAL ARTHUR, B.A., Barr. (1872) late Bengal Admn.—Joined the service, 3rd April, 1878, as offg. reporter, Indian law reports; confd., Jan., 1880; acted subsequently as dep. supt. and rememberer of legal affairs, registrar of the diocese, and receiver of the high court, Calcutta; apptd. chief presidency mag., Calcutta; and judge of the court for trial of pilots, May, 1894; ret'd., Oct., 1903.

PEART, DENIS GEORGE, Major, Indian Army (cantonmt. mag., United Prov.).—First commn., 25th Aug., 1886; apptd. cantonmt. mag., Lucknow, April, 1898, since when he has served in that capacity at various stations in the United Prov.

PEASE, HENRY THOMAS, Major, Indian Civ. Vety. Dept. (principal, Veterinary Coll., Lahore).—Joined the (home) service 27th Aug., 1884; principal, Veterinary Coll., Lahore, Oct., 1896; author of numerous contributions on veterinary subjects.

PECK, FRANCIS SAMUEL, Lieut.-Col., Indian Medl. Service (prof., Calcutta Medl. Coll.).—1st commn., 2nd April, 1880; in mily. employ till April, 1883; resdt. surg., Calcutta Medl. Coll., Oct., 1883; civil surg. at various stations, Oct., 1887, to Mar., 1899; prof. of midwifery, Calcutta Medl. Coll., and obstetric physn. and surgn., Eden hosp., Mar., 1900; is author of a work on ovariotomy.

PEDLER, SIR ALEXANDER, C.I.E., F.R.S. (dir. of public instrn., Bengal).—Apptd. to the educn. dept., 22nd Jan., 1873; arrived, 8th May, 1873, and served as prof. of agricl. chemistry, presidency coll.; on special duty, eclipse expdn., March, 1875; meteorological reporter to govt. of Bengal, May, 1889, in addn. to his duties as prof.; principal, Presidency Coll., Nov., 1896; dir. of public instrn., Jan., 1899; C.I.E., Nov., 1901; addl. member of viceroy's legisl. council, Nov., 1903; vice-chancellor, Calcutta Univ., 1904; Kt., Jan., 1906.

PEET, ARTHUR WILLIAM, late Forest Dept., Madras.—Joined the service, 18th Oct., 1871, as asst. conservator; dep. conservator, April, 1872; conservator, Oct., 1891; ret'd., July, 1901.

PEILE, SIR JAMES BRAINTWAITE, K.C.S.I., M.A., late Bombay C.S., and late member of the Council of India.—Educ. at Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. to the Bombay civil service after exam. of 1855; arrived 8th Oct., 1856, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; on special duty for inquiry into claims of thakurs of Bhaunagar on account of land and sea customs, May, 1859; specially apptd. to inquire into condition of talukdars, Ahmadabad, Jan., 1860; alienation settl. officer, Gujarat, April, 1861; asst. to rev. commr., Dec., 1861; under sec. to govt. of Bombay, rev. dept., April, 1862; on special duty for carrying out provisions of bill for relief of the talukdars, Ahmadabad, Dec., 1862; commr. for revising subord. civil establs., April, 1866; registrar-gen. of assurances, July, 1866; dir. of public instrn., April, 1869; acting municipal commr. Bombay, May—Oct., 1872; acting pol. agent, Kathiawar, Jan., 1873; on duty at Delhi in connection with imperial assemblage, Dec., 1876; acting commr. in Sind, June, 1878; member of famine commn., 1878-79; acting sec. to govt., pol., &c., depts., March, 1879; from April, 1879, served as sec. in the rev., finl., and gen. depts., and acted as chief sec.; on special duty on famine commn. in England, 1879-80; on special duty in India, 1880 and 1881; member of council, Bombay, Dec., 1882; acted as govr. and prest. in council in March, 1885; temporary member of govr.-gen.'s council, Oct., 1886; ret'd., Nov., 1887; member of council of India, 12th Nov., 1887; vice-prest., 1894-95; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1888; member of royal commn. on Indian expenditure, May, 1895; ret'd. from Council of India, Nov., 1902.

PEILE, SOLOMON CHARLES FREDERICK, C.I.E., Col., Indian Army, Burma Police (inspr.-gen. of police).—First commn., 9th Aug., 1874; served in the Afghan war of 1878-79 (medal), and in the Burmese expdn. of 1886-87 (medal and clasp); apptd. dir. of police supply and clothing, Upper Burma, March, 1887, the office being subsequently extended to the whole of Burma; dep. inspr.-gen., mil. police, June, 1890; inspr.-gen. of police, Feb., 1892; C.I.E., Jan., 1901.

PELLEREAU, HENRY ETIENNE, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1883, and posted to Bengal; transf. to Punjab, Feb., 1892; exec. engr., Jan., 1897.

PEMBERTON, ROBERT, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (dist. med. offr., Madras).—First commn., 30th March, 1878; in mil. employ till 7th Oct., 1887, when apptd. civil surgn, Guntur; dist. med. offr., April, 1890.

PEMBERTON, ROBERT CHARLES BOILEAU,

C.S.I., Major-Gen., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Lieut., Bengal engrs., 1853; joined the dept., May, 1857; served during the Mutiny (medal and two clasps), and subsequently in the road dept., Oudh, as exec. engr.; asst. sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., March, 1865; offg. suptg. engr., railway branch, Haidarabad, Sept., 1870; dir. of stores, railway branch, May, 1877; consulting engr. for guaranteed railway's, Lucknow, 1880; chief engr., 1st class, March, 1886; dir.-gen. of railways and dep. sec., govt. of India, railway branch, Feb., 1887; sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., April, 1887; reverted to mil. dept., 1890; offg. member of viceroy's council, Feb., 1891, to April, 1892; ret'd. from the army, Nov., 1892; C.S.I., May, 1894.

PENNEL, CHARLES STUART, M.A., Indian C.S. (private sec. to lieut.-govr., Burma).—Educ. at Edinburgh univ. and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 10th Dec., 1897, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., Feb., 1902; private sec. to lieut.-govr., Aug., 1905.

PENNINGTON, HENRY FRANCIS DUMARESQ, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Westminster; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 21st Oct., 1875, and served in Oudh as asst. commr. and sub-judge; joint mag., April, 1887; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1890; also addl. judge, Moradabad, 1893-94; ret'd., April, 1901.

PENNINGTON, JAMES BURN, B.L., late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Sedbergh Gr. Sch., and St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1861; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., sub-collr., sub-sec. to the board of rev., and acting collr. of sea customs at Madras; collr. and mag., July, 1875; ret'd., Sept., 1888.

PENNY, EDMUND, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1874, and posted to state railways; transf. to Central Prov., Aug., 1875; exec. engr., May, 1883; planned and carried out scheme for Nagpur water-works, 1890; acted as asst. sec. to chief commr. in 1891; suptg. engr. and sec. to chief commr., Jan., 1899; C.I.E., May, 1900; ret'd., Feb., 1902.

PENNYCUICK, JOHN, C.S.I., Col., R.E., late Public Works Dept., Madras.—Lieut., Madras engrs., Dec., 1858; arrived, 11th Nov., 1860; served in the dept. from April, 1862; exec. engr., Dec., 1866; served in the Abyssinian expdn., 1867-68 (medal); suptg. engr., Oct., 1881; dep. chief engr., and under sec. to govt., Jan., 1883; supt. of works, tank maintenance scheme, April, 1884; on special duty to England to select machinery for Periyar works project, July, 1888; chief engr. and sec. in the public works dept., July, 1890; member of legisl. council, Nov., 1893; C.S.I., Oct., 1895; ret'd., Jan., 1896; presdt. of Royal Indian Engrg. Coll., Cooper's Hill, Sept., 1896, to Sept., 1899.

PERCIVAL, PHILIP EDWARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (under sec. to govt., pol., judl., and legisl. depts., Bombay).—Educ. at the Charterhouse and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 25th Nov., 1895, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and joint addl. sess. judge; 2nd asst., Sept., 1903; under sec. to govt., pol., judl., and legisl. depts., Oct., 1903.

PERCY (Earl), HENRY ALGERNON GEORGE, M.P., D.L.—Educ. at Eton and Christ Church,

Oxford; apptd. parly. under sec. of state for India, 18th Aug., 1902; is a trustee of the National Portrait Gallery, and has represented Kensington (S.) in the House of Commons since 1895; under sec. of state for foreign affairs, Oct., 1903, to Dec., 1905; author of 'Notes from a Diary in Asiatic Turkey' and 'The Highlands of Asiatic Turkey.'

PERKINS, NORMAN CHICHESTER, Major, I.S.C. late Burma commn.—First commn., 13th Aug., 1879; served in Afghan campaign of 1880-81, and in Burma, 1886-89 (medal and clasp); joined the commn. as asst. commr., Aug., 1886; dep. commr., Oct., 1896; ret'd., April, 1900.

PERRAM, GEORGE JAMES, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 21st Dec., 1868, and posted to Mysore; exec. engr., Jan., 1880; transf'd. to Central Provs., March, 1881; to Coorg, Jan., 1887; and to Burma, Feb., 1893; suptg. engr., Jan., 1896; transf'd. to Central Provs. as sec. to chief commr., Feb., 1900; chief engr., March, 1901; chief engr. and sec. to chief commr. of Assam, May, 1902; ret'd., Nov., 1903; C.I.E., Jan., 1904.

PERRIN, CHARLES, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd., 10th Oct., 1874, and employed on irrig. works in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., May, 1883; under sec. to govt., Feb., 1892; sanitary engr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, with rank of suptg. engr., July, 1893; supt. of practical training of passed students at R.I.E. Coll., 1896-7; ret'd., April, 1899.

PERROTT, FINCH, India Office (senr. clerk, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.).—Junr. clerk, 24th Dec., 1880; senr. clerk, May, 1900.

PERROTT, HENRY RONALD TAYLOR SIMPSON, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived 13th Dec., 1900, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

PERRY, FRANCIS FREDERIC, F.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service, (principal, Lahore Medical Coll.).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1879; from March, 1882, served in the Punjab as civil surgn., prof'r., Lahore med. coll., and chemical examr. to govt.; prof'r. of surgery, and surgn. to Mayo hosp., Lahore, Dec., 1888; acted as prof'r., Calcutta med. coll., in 1894; hon. surgn. to Viceroy, Nov., 1901; principal and prof'r. of surgery, Lahore med. coll., Oct., 1904.

PERT, FREDERICK JAMES, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 15th Nov., 1888, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Feb., 1893; settlt. officer, Nov., 1894; dep. commr., July, 1898; joint sec. to rev. board, March, 1899 to March, 1901; mag. and collr., Nov. 1902.

PESTANJI JEHANGIR, C.I.E., Khan Bahadur, late Bombay Uncov. Service.—Joined the service as asst. master, Elphinstone instn., Bombay, 10th Oct., 1853; served in the settlt. dept. from April, 1857; 1st grade dep. collr., Aug., 1869; C.I.E., Jan., 1882; talukdari settlt. officer, May, 1889; ret'd., 1889.

PESTANJI, S. P. (see Sorabji Padamji Pestanji).

PESTONJEE SORABJEE PATUCK (see Patuck, P. S.).
