Prov. and Oudh, March, 1890; in 1892 acted as surgn.-gen. and sanitary commr. with the govt. of India; ret'd., May, 1894; was a member of the Venice Sanitary Conference, 1897; a member of the Indian Famine Commission, 1897; and has been a member of the Army Sanitary Commn. since July, 1896; K.H.P., Sept., 1903.

RICHARDSON, THOMAS WILLIAM, Indian C.S. Barr. (1905) (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Brighton Coll., Cheltenham Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 8th Dec., 1886, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec., gen., rev., and statistical depts., March, 1893; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1895; registrar, high

court, appellate side, Dec., 1895; dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1899.

RICHARDSON, WILLIAM PELHAM, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 8th Oct., 1868, and employed on irrigation works in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Sept., 1872; ret'd., May, 1896.

RICKETTS, GEORGE H. M., C.B., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Winchester, and Haileybury; apptd., 1847; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and as asst. to the govr.-gen.'s agent, S.W. frontier; from 1853, served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and dept. commr., and from 1858 in the N.W. Prov. as mag. and collr., and civil and sessions judge; C.B. for service in the Punjab in 1857, and medal for service with the troops; commr., Allahabad divn., 1873; junr. member, board of rev., 1878; ret'd., 1879.

RICKETTS, MONTAGUE POYNTZ, Col., I.S.C., late Central Prov. Commn.—Arrived 22nd March, 1855; served in the Central Prov. from Sept., 1859, as asst. commr.; small cause court judge, Jabalpur, Aug., 1862; dep. commr., May, 1867; addl. commr., Nov., 1883; ret'd., April, 1890.

RIDDELL, ROBERT VANITTART, Col., R.E.—Joined Addiscombe, Feb., 1858; lieut., royal engrs., 10th June, 1859; arrived, March, 1861; from Aug., 1862, served in the topogr. survey of India as mil. asst.; dep. supt., Oct., 1867; asst. surveyor-gen. Oct., 1876; apptd. mint master, Calcutta, Jan., 1884; mint master and mint engr., Bombay, Aug., 1889; ret'd., March, 1895.

RIDGEWAY, THE RIGHT HON. SIR JOSEPH WEST, G.C.M.G., K.C.B., K.C.S.I., P.C., Col.—First commn., Bengal infantry, Dec., 1860; in civil employ from May, 1868, in the Central India and Rajputana agencies; attaché, foreign dept., 1873; asst. agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, 1875; pol. agent, E. states, Rajputana, Aug., 1876; on special pol. duty with the field force in Afghanistan, under Gen. Sir F. Roberts, 1880 (medal with clasp, bronze star); from Sept., 1880, served in the secretariat of the foreign dept., and was apptd. asst. sec., Nov., 1880, and junr. under sec., Dec., 1881; from Aug., 1884, served as asst. with the commn. for the delimitation of the Afghan frontier, and was apptd. H.M.'s commr. for this purpose, Sept., 1885; K.C.S.I., July, 1885; subsequently was H.M.'s commr. for same purpose at St. Petersburg; returned to Europe, 1887, and was apptd. under sec. for Ireland; ret'd. from the army, Aug., 1889; K.C.B., Jan., 1891; envoy to Morocco, Jan., 1893; lieut.-govr. of the Isle of Man, Nov., 1893; govr. of Ceylon, 1895 to 1903; G.C.M.G., Jan., 1900.

RIEKIE, JOHN, M.I.M.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the dept., Mar., 1877; asst. locomotive supt., Feb., 1880; dist. locom. supt., Nov., 1887; dep. locom. supt., Oct., 1898; locom. supt., April, 1900; ret'd., March, 1903.

RIEU, JEAN LOUIS, Indian C.S. (1st asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., London, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 20th Nov., 1863, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., June, 1900; ditto, and manager, encumbered estates in Sind, Feb., 1903; 1st asst., Dec., 1904.

RIGG, ARTHUR EDMUND, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Kingswood Sch., Bath, and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 15th Dec., 1894, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

RIGG, HENRY, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 13th June, 1862, and arrived, 7th Dec., 1862; asst. engr., Feb., 1864; served in various divns. in Madras, and was apptd. exec. engr., March, 1868; on special duty, Upper Burma, 1885-86 (medal and clasps); manager and engr.-in-chief, Burma state railway, June, 1889; consulting engr. to the govt. of India for railways, central divn., May, 1892; chief engr., Aug., 1892; consulting engr. of railways, Lucknow, Jan., 1895; ret'd., Feb., 1898.

RIGHY, VANS, M.I.C.E., late Public Works Dept., Bengal.—Apptd. proby. asst. engr., Feb., 1856; exec. engr., March, 1871; from Jan., 1875, was employed on Tirhut state railway, and was apptd. manager and supt. of way and works, Tirhut and Patna Gya state railways, March, 1880; transf'd. to superior rev. establ., Oct., 1882; supt. of way and works, N.W. railways, 1886; ret'd., Dec., 1891.

RILEY, HENRY JAMES, India Office (second class clerk, acctnt.-gen.'s Dept.).—Clerk, and class, 3rd April, 1882.

RINGE, RICHARD HEAD, Major, late Indian Civ. Vety. Dept.—Joined the (home) service, 5th Oct., 1878; supt., civil veterinary dept., N.W.P. and O., March, 1897; ret'd., March, 1904.

RIPON (MARQUESS OF), THE RIGHT HON. GEORGE FREDERICK SAMUEL ROBINSON, K.G., G.C.S.I., C.I.E., P.C., D.C.L.—Born 24th Oct., 1827; attaché to a special mission to Brussels, 1849; sat in the house of commons (as Viscount Goderich) as member for Hull, 1852-53, for Huddersfield, 1853-57, and for the W. Riding of Yorkshire, 1857-59, in which year he succeeded to the upper house; under sec. for war, June, 1859; prest. of the royal geographical society, 1860; under sec. for India, Jan.-July, 1861; sworn a privy councillor, April, 1863; lord high steward of Hull, Oct., 1863; sec. for war, April, 1863, to Feb., 1866; sec. of state for India, Feb.-July, 1866; lord prest. of the council, Dec., 1868, to Aug., 1873; K.G., 1869; hon. D.C.L., Oxford, 1870; chairman of the joint high commn. which arranged the treaty of Washington, 1871; created Marquess of Ripon, 1871; viceroy and govr.-gen. of India, June, 1880, to Dec., 1884; was a royal commr. for the London exhibn. of 1886; first lord of the admiralty, Feb.-Aug., 1886; sec. of state for the colonies, Aug., 1892, to July, 1895; mayor of Ripon, 1895-96; Lord Privy Seal, Dec., 1905.

RISLEY, HERBERT HOPE, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (sec. to govt. of India, home dept.).—Educ. at Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 24th Oct., 1873, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. to dir.-gen. of statistics; asst. sec., govt. of Bengal, Oct., 1877; under sec., rev. and gen. depts., Jan., 1878; offg. under sec., govt. of India, home dept., Feb., 1879; asst. commr., Jan., 1880; supt. of survey of Ghatwali and other service tenures in Manbhum, Feb., 1884; employed on the special duty of compiling statistics relating to the castes and occupations of the people of Bengal, Jan., 1885; dep. commr., April, 1886; sec., police commn., collr. and mag., May, 1890; 1891; sec. to govt. of Bengal, finl. and municipal depts., July, 1892; member of provl. legisl. council, 1892-93, and from March, 1895; C.I.E.,

Jan., 1892; census commr., Oct., 1899; offg. sec., home dept., govt. of India, Sept., 1902; confd. Nov., 1903; C.S.I., June, 1904; author of 'Sikkim and Tibet,' 'Tribes and Castes of Bengal,' an 'Ethnographic Glossary,' and other works on ethnology.

RITCHIE, JOHN GERALD, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Winchester, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 28th Oct., 1875, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1887; dep. commr., May, 1891; mag. and collr., Dec., 1891; chairman of Calcutta commrs., April, 1895; mag. and collr., Feb., 1897; commr., April, 1902; ret'd., Dec., 1902.

RITCHIE, RICHMOND THACKERAY WILLOUGHBY, C.B., India Office (sec., pol. and secret dept.).—Educ. at Eton, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; junr. clerk, finl. dept., 18th May, 1877; rev., statistics and commerce dept., 1881; private sec. to parly. under sec. of state, 1883-92; senr. clerk, Oct., 1892; private sec. to permanent under sec., Oct., 1892, to Feb., 1894; sec. to royal commn. on Indian expenditure, May, 1895, but resigned on appt. as private sec. to sec. of state (Lord George Hamilton), July, 1895; C.B., May, 1898; sec., pol. and secret dept., Nov., 1902.

RIVAZ, SIR CHARLES MONTGOMERY, K.C.S.I., Indian C.S. (lieut.-gov. of the Punjab).—Educ. at Blackheath Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 28th Nov., 1864, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and offg. under sec. and sec. to govt.; supt., Kapurthala state, April, 1877; dep. commr., April, 1885; sec. to Punjab govt., May, 1887; commr. and supt., Nov., 1888; and finl. commr., Feb., 1893; C.S.I., May, 1895; addl. member of legislative council of govr.-gen., May, 1896-Oct., 1896; temp. ord. member of govr.-gen.'s Council, Oct., 1897; confd., April, 1898; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1901; lieut.-gov. of the Punjab, March, 1902.

RIVAZ, GEORGE WILMOT, late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Blackheath Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 29th Oct., 1870, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; registrar, chief court, Punjab, 1877-84; district judge, Aug., 1886; divl. judge, March, 1891; ret'd., Jan., 1897; edited the 2nd edn. of the circular orders of the chief court.

RIVETT-CARNAC, CHARLES JAMES, late Finl. Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the dept., 21st May, 1872, and served in the N.W. Prov. and the Punjab; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Oct., 1882; asst. comptr.-gen. (forest branch), Oct., 1884; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, March, 1885; comptr., Central Prov., Nov., 1891; transf'd. to Burma, Nov., 1895; dep. auditor-gen., April, 1898; on deptn. as finl. adviser to the King of Siam from Nov., 1898; ret'd., March, 1903.

RIVETT-CARNAC, JOHN HENRY, C.I.E., A.D.C. to the King, V.D., F.S.A.; late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 12th Nov., 1858, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. under sec., govt. of India, home dept., Sept., 1861; asst. sec. to the chief commr., Central Prov., June, 1862; settlt. officer, Chanda and Wardha, Aug., 1864; cotton commr., Central Prov., Sept., 1866, and Berars, April, 1867; commr. of cotton and commerce with the govt. of India, Dec., 1871; member and hon. sec. of central com-

mittee of international exhibits; on duty in England, 1872-73; special commr. of transport, Bengal famine; opium agent, Benares, March, 1875; ret'd., Feb., 1894; author of reports on Indian cotton cultivation and trade, and on the interchange of traffic on Indian railways, &c.

RIVETT-CARNAC, JOHN THURLOW, Police Dept., E. Bengal and Assam (dep. inspr.-genl. of police).—Joined the service 27th Nov., 1874, as offg. dep. mag. and dep. collr.; offg. asst. supt. of police, June, 1877; confd., Nov., 1880; dist. supt., June, 1888; transf'd. to E. Bengal and Assam as dep. inspr.-genl. of police, Oct., 1905.

RIX, HARRY SIDNEY, B.A., Indian C.S., (joint mag., United Prov.).—Educ. at Sherborne Sch., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 28th Dec., 1894, and served in N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; judge, small cause court, Aug., 1898; asst. commr., Dec., 1893; joint mag., Dec., 1901.

ROBARTS, CHARLES JAMES, Lieut.-Col., late Indian Army.—First commn., 12th Feb., 1873; in mil. employ till April, 1890, when he was apptd. cantonmt. mag., Bengal; transf'd. to the Punjab, March, 1896; ret'd., May, 1905.

ROBB, JOHN, M.D., C.M., Surgn.-Major, late Med. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the Indian med. service, 1st Oct., 1868; from Nov., 1873, served as civil surgn., Zanzibar, and acted as asst. pol. agent in 1874-75 and 1881; from July, 1882, served under the govt. of Bombay, as civil surgn., jail supt., &c., at Aden, Surat, and Ahmadabad; ret'd., Nov., 1889.

ROBERTS, ALFRED ERNEST, M.B., Major I.M.S. (statistical offr. to govt. of India in sany. and med. depts.).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1887; in mil. employ till Dec., 1891, when he was apptd. civil surgn. in N.W. Prov.; spel. health offr., Simla, May, 1899; statistical offr. to govt. of India in sany. and med. depts., April, 1904.

ROBERTS, ALLAN SCOTT, Lieut.-Col., Bengal Infantry, late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 2nd April, 1862; from June, 1866, served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and jud. asst.; dep. commr., Nov., 1884; ret'd., Dec., 1894.

ROBERTS, CHARLES HAMILTON (dist. and sess. judge, Oudh).—Joined the service, 25th Feb., 1874, and served as extra asst. commr. and cantonmt. mag.; dep. collr., Jan., 1887; on special duty in Garhwal, April, 1890; supt., Tarai, Sept., 1890; dep. commr., Naini Tal, Oct., 1891; dist. and sess. judge, Rae Bareli, Jan., 1893; ditto, Fyzabad, Feb. 1901.

ROBERTS (EARL), RIGHT HON. FREDERICK SLEIGH, V.C., K.G., K.P., G.C.B., O.M., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., Field-Marshal, R.A.—First commn., Bengal artillery, 1851; during the Mutiny served at the siege and capture of Delhi, &c. (medal and three clasps, V.C.); dep. asst. quartermaster-gen., Bengal, April, 1858; served in the N.W. frontier expedn., 1863 (medal and clasp); asst. quarter-master-gen., Bengal, Feb., 1863; served with the Abyssinian expedn., 1868 (medal), and with the Lushai expedn., 1871-72; dep. quarter-master-gen., Bengal, Jan., 1872; quarter-master-gen., Bengal, Jan., 1875; during the operations in Afghanistan in 1878-9-80, commanded the Kuram valley field force, and sub-

sequently the Kabul field force at the occupation of Kabul and battle of Charasiah, and in the march to the relief of Kandahar (medal with four clasps, bronze star, thanks of both houses of parliament, created a bart., K.C.B., G.C.B.); commander-in-chief, Madras, and member of govr.'s council, Nov., 1881; commander-in-chief, in India, and member of the govr.-gen.'s council, Nov., 1885, to April, 1893; created a peer, Jan., 1892; G.C.S.I., June, 1893; field-marshal, May, 1895; commander-in-chief in Ireland, Oct., 1895; commander of the forces in S. Africa, Dec., 1899, to Nov., 1900; commander-in-chief, Army Head-quarters, Nov., 1900, to Feb., 1904; created an Earl of the United Kingdom and K.G., Jan., 1901; apptd. to the Order of Merit, June, 1902; author of 'Forty-one years in India.'

ROBERTS, JAMES REID, Major, I.M.S. (admin. medl. offr., Central India).—First commn., 31st March, 1888; agency surg., Gilgit, April, 1893; resdcy. surgn., Gwalior, Oct., 1900; ditto, Indore, May, 1901; admn. medl. offr., Central India, June, 1904.

ROBERTS, REUBEN WILLIAM, B.A., LL.B., A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Oct., 1876, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., Jan., 1888; lent to Burma rly. co., 1895-1899.

ROBERTS, SYDNEY GORDON, Indian C.S. (head asst., Madras).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 27th Nov., 1893, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Jan., 1902.

ROBERTS, THOMAS, M.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Cheltenham Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, Nov., 1863, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., jud. asst., and dep. commr.; addl. commr., Jalandhar, Aug., 1884; divl. judge, Nov., 1884; ret'd., April, 1891.

ROBERTS, WILLIAM HOWLAND, M.D., Surgn.-Col., late Madras Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., 23rd July, 1858; arrived, 24th Sept., 1858, and served in the Central Prov. till Sept., 1864, and subsequently in Madras as civil surgn., supt. of jail, &c.; was also in charge of meteorological observatories at Coimbatore and Salem; reverted to mil. duty, Sept., 1885; ret'd., July, 1893.

ROBERTSON, BENJAMIN, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (chief sec. to chief commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at the Inst., Elgin, the Gr. Sch. and Univ. of Aberdeen, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 28th Nov., 1885, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; transf'd. to Central Prov. as provl. supt. of census operations, April, 1890; asst. commr., Oct., 1892; commr. of excise, Oct., 1894; dep. commr., Nov., 1894; C.I.E., Jan., 1893; chief sec. to chief commr., May, 1902, to March, 1903, and from Dec., 1903.

ROBERTSON, CHARLES, M.D., Dep. Surgn.-Gen., late Madras Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., 4th Aug., 1856; arrived, 17th Dec., 1856; served under the mil. dept. and also as zillah surgn., Masulipatam (1862-63); from May, 1874, served as zillah surgn., and supt. of jail, Cuddalore; ret'd., Dec., 1885.

ROBERTSON, CHARLES LONSDALE, Major R.E. Survey of India (dep. supt.).—First commn., 24th July, 1886; joined the survey

dept. as assist. supt., May, 1894; dep. supt., May, 1898.

ROBERTSON, SIR DONALD, K.C.S.I., Lieut.-Col., Indian Army, late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Ensign, royal Scots fusiliers, Aug., 1865; arrived, Oct., 1865; served from Nov., 1869, as asst. commr. in the Central Prov.; transf. to Rajputana, June, 1872; cantonmt. mag., Nasirabad, May, 1877; offg. asst. commr., Merwara and Ajmir, 1877-78; offg. supt., Jhalawar, 1879; 1st asst. agent, Indore, 1881; pol. agent, Bhopawar, Feb., 1885; pol. agent, Bundelkhand, May, 1885; asst. to the rest. in Mysore, and sec. to the chief commr. of Coorg, Dec., 1886; pol. agent in Baghelkhand, and supt. of the Rewa state, Dec., 1888; rest., Gwalior, Feb., 1894; offg. govr.-gen.'s agent, Central India, April, 1895; rest., Mysore, and chief commr., Coorg, Dec., 1896; C.S.I., Dec., 1898; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1903; ret'd. from foreign dept., Oct., 1903.

ROBERTSON, DOUGLAS GALTON, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll. and the Univ., Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 14th Dec., 1903, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

ROBERTSON, ELPHINSTONE POURTALES, late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 27th March, 1851, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., dep. commr. of customs, salt, and opium, and sub-collr. and joint mag.; senr. collr. and mag., Dharwar, Jan., 1873; rev. and police commr., Nov., 1876; commr. of the central divn., Jan., 1878; ret'd., 1886.

ROBERTSON, FREDERICK ALEXANDER, Barr. (1887), Indian C.S. (judge, chief court, Punjab).—Educ. at King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 5th Dec., 1876, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settlt. officer; special duty, Maler Kotla settlt. operations, Nov., 1888; dir. of land records and agric., Dec., 1889; divl. judge, Nov., 1896; temp. addl. judge, chief court, March, 1902; judge, chief court, June, 1904; author of 'Customary Law of Rawalpindi District,' and of reports on the revised settlt. and forest settlt. of that district.

ROBERTSON, FREDERICK WILLIAM ROWNTREE, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Christchurch Sch., London Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

ROBERTSON, SIR GEORGE SCOTT, K.C.S.I., M.P., Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Service.—Educ. at Westminster Hosp.; joined the service, 30th March, 1878; served through the Afghan war, 1879-80; civil surg., N.W. Prov., 1880-88; on depts. to Gilgit, June, 1888; agency surg., Gilgit, July, 1889; on special duty in Kafiristan, 1890-91; chief pol. officer, Hunza-Nagar expdn., 1891; C.S.I., May, 1892; offg. agent, Gilgit, May-Oct., 1892; mission to Gor and capture of Chilas, 1892; in charge, Chitral mission, 1893; agent, Gilgit, Jan., 1894; K.C.S.I., July, 1895, for his defence of Chitral fort; pol. officer in charge of Ayub Khan at Rawalpindi, Jan., 1899; ret'd., Oct., 1899; M.P. for Bradford (Central), Jan., 1906.

ROBERTSON, GRAHAM, Bengal Marine Dept. (shipwright surveyor, Calcutta).—Joined the service, 25th Feb., 1891; and marine

surveyor, Calcutta port, Nov., 1892; 1st engr. and shipwright surveyor, June, 1895.

ROBERTSON, JAMES ARTHUR, Finl. Dept., Govt. of India (enrolled list, class III).—Entered the dept., 29th Sept., 1885; asst. acct.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Sept., 1890; ditto, Bombay, Nov., 1890; ditto, Bengal, Nov., 1893; offg. dir.-gen. of statistics, May, 1898, Sept., 1901, and Sept., 1902 to Feb., 1905; offg. compt., India Treasuries, March, 1902.

ROBERTSON, JOHN HERBERT, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Sir Roger Cholmeley's Sch., Highgate, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 29th Nov., 1889, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; special asst., Oct., 1896; sub. collr. and joint mag., Nov., 1901; coll. and mag., Jan., 1905.

ROBERTSON, LAURENCE, Indian C.S. (under sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agri. dept.).—Educ. at Glasgow Acad., Glasgow Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1893, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; compiled general admn. report, 1896-7; 2nd asst., June, 1900; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agri. dept., Dec., 1903; confd., March, 1904.

ROBERTSON, LINDSAY JOHN, Barr. (1884).—Apptd. offg. reporter to high court, Bombay, June, 1903; confd., Nov., 1904.

ROBERTSON, LIONEL FRASER, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1884, and posted to the Punjab; exec. engr., Nov., 1897. (India medal and two clasps, 1897-98.)

ROBERTSON, ROBERT, M.B., C.M., Major, Ind. med. service (presy. surgn., Madras).—First commn., 21st March, 1887; military duty till March, 1891; surgn., genl. hosp., and prof'r., med. coll., Madras, July, 1892; dist. surgn., Coimbatore, April, 1893; addl. med. offr., genl. hosp., March, 1895; civil surgn., Tellicherry, Oct., 1899; surgn., 2nd dist., Madras, physn., genl. hosp., and prof'r., med. coll., July, 1901.

ROBERTSON, WILLIAM, India Office (2nd class clerk, Funds Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, India audit office, 27th Feb., 1886; transf. to funds dept., India office, 3rd Dec., 1894.

ROBINSON, ALEXANDER, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Royal Sch., Armagh, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 9th Nov., 1865, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and dep. commr.; mag. and collr., April, 1887; ret'd., Sept., 1892; author of 'Memoirs of the Ballia and Jaunpur Districts.'

ROBINSON, SYDNEY MADDOCK, Barr. (1888), (govt. advocate, Punjab).—Apptd. offg. junior govt. advocate, chief court, Punjab, Feb., 1895; govt. advocate, April, 1899.

ROBINSON, WILLIAM, B.A., India Office (senior clerk, finl. dept.).—Educ. at Carmichael Sch., Queen's Coll., Cork, and the Royal Univ. of Ireland; junr. clerk, 11th June, 1894; resdt. clerk, Dec., 1894-May, 1897; joint editor, India Office List, April, 1900, to Oct., 1901; senr. clerk, finl. dept., Oct., 1901.

ROBSON, SAMUEL, M.A., Indian Educ. Service (principal, Lahore Coll.).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; joined the Bengal educ. dept., 20th Dec., 1878, as prof'r., presidency coll.; transf. to Patna coll., June, 1880; and to

Hugli coll., Jan., 1883; again prof., Patna coll., Aug., 1888; principal, Dacca coll., March, 1897; transf. to the Punjab as principal, Lahore coll., Feb., 1898.

ROE, BERNHARD OSWALD, Capt., Indian Army (dist. judge, Punjab).—First commn., 9th Nov., 1889; in mil. employ till Oct., 1894, when he was apptd. asst. commr. in the Punjab; dist. judge, June, 1903.

ROE, SIR CHARLES ARTHUR, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1863, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settl. officer; dep. commr., Nov., 1878; addl. commr., Lahore and Rawal Pindi divns., May, 1883; divl. judge, Nov., 1884; addl. judge, chief court, Nov., 1888; judge, April, 1889; chief judge, Dec., 1895; Kt., Jan., 1897; ret'd. May, 1898; author of 'Customary Law of the Multan District,' and of three settl. reports.

ROE, FRANCIS REGINALD, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 8th Nov., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1897; mag. and collr., May, 1902; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1904.

ROE, ROBERT BRADLEY, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surgn., Berar).—First commn., 1st Oct., 1881; apptd. civil. surgn., Berar, July, 1884.

ROE, WILLIAM ALEXANDER CRAWFORD, Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Service.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1869; in mil. employ till 22nd March, 1878, when he was apptd. offg. civil surgn., Jalandhar; in 1879 served with the Kuram valley field force; confd. as civil surgn., Sept., 1880; med. adviser to Maharaja of Kapurthala, April to Oct., 1885; sanitary commr., Punjab, May, 1894; ret'd., Feb., 1900.

ROGERS, ALEXANDER, late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1845; arrived, 9th Nov., 1845; served in the Bombay rev. and jud. dept., and was apptd. 1st asst. collr. and mag., Broach, Oct., 1851, and afterwards at Ahmadabad, Oct., 1852; settl. officer, Gujarat, Nov., 1852; collr. and mag., Ahmadabad, March, 1860, and afterwards at Surat; rev. and police commr., N. divn., April, 1865; member of council, Bombay, June, 1872; ret'd. in 1879; author of a transl. of three modern Persian plays, a 'History of the Land Rev. Settlt. of Bombay,' and a transl. of 'Yusuf and Zuleika'; has also edited the 'Bostan' of Saladi.

ROGERS, ERNEST, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 25th Nov., 1887, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Dec., 1890; dep. commr., Dec., 1896; mag. and collr., Nov., 1898.

ROGERS, PHILIP GRAHAM, Indian C.S. (pte. sec. to lieut.-govr. of E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital, and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived 18th Dec., 1901, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; pte. sec. to lieut.-govr. of E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

ROGERS, WALTER, India Public Works Dept. (special engr.).—Apptd. from the O. and R. rly. co. as special engr., 1st Jan., 1889, and posted to state rly.

ROGERS-HARRISON, AUGUSTUS NAPOLEON, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., late Madras Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1879, and was employed under the mil. dept. until Dec., 1880, when he was apptd. civil surgn. and supt. of jail and lunatic asylum, Vizagapatam; civil surgn., Salem, June, 1887; district med. and sanitary officer, Oct., 1895; ret'd. Mar., 1902.

ROLLAND, ALEXANDER TULLOCH, Col., Indian Army, late Police Dept., Madras.—Ensign, Madras infantry, April, 1856; served in the dept. from Dec., 1862, as asst. supt., and supt.; dep. inspr.-gen. of police, Feb., 1885; acted as commr. of police, Madras city, in 1889; reverted to mil. dept., Oct., 1893.

ROMESH CHUNDER DUTT (see Dutt, R.C.)

RONCA, EDWARD HENRY, India Office (2nd class clerk, registry and record dept.).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 3rd Feb., 1902.

RONSON, WILLIAM TOMLINSON, India Office (senior clerk, rev. and statistics dept.).—Writer, 1st Sept., 1873; clerk, 2nd class, Feb., 1879; junr. clerk, Feb., 1894; senior clerk, Oct., 1901.

ROOKE, ALFRED SHIPTON, Major, Indian Army (cantonmt. mag., United Prov.).—Joined the service, 10th Feb., 1881; apptd. cantonmt. mag., Dec., 1889, since when he has served in that capacity at various stations in the United Prov.

ROOS-KEPPEL, GEORGE OLOF, C.I.E., Major, Indian Army (pol. agent, Khyber, N.W. Frontier Prov.).—First commn., 25th Aug., 1886; joined the Indian Staff Corps, Sept., 1897; apptd. pol. agent, Khyber, Sept., 1899; C.I.E., Jan., 1900.

ROPER, CAMPBELL ROWLEY, Indian C.S. (2nd asst. Bombay).—Educ. at Shrewsbury Sch., Trinity Coll., Dublin, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 10th Dec., 1894, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. judge and sess. judge, Oct., 1899; 2nd asst. and asst. judge, Aug., 1901.

ROSE, ABRAHAM EDMUND, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; appointed to the dept., 14th Nov., 1874, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; asst. engr., Nov., 1875; transf. to Burma, Sept., 1890; exec. engr., Aug., 1893; asst. to chief engr. and under sec. to govt., Nov., 1900.

ROSE, BENJAMIN JAMES, India Office (clerk, rev. and statistics dept.).—Entered the India office, 23rd Feb., 1871; technical asst. in the rev. and statistics dept., June, 1882; also clerk to the special asst., 1891; clerk for special duties, Jan., 1899; hon. sec., Indian and Ceylon Committee, Royal Comm., Paris Exhibn., 1900, and in exec. charge of Indian section of the exhibn.; in charge of India trade enquiry office from 1902; awarded gold medal at Amsterdam exhibn. for statistical diagrams showing the condition and progress of British India; prepared abstract catalogue of the Indian section at the same exhibn.; reports on wheat and Madras tobacco; and offl. report on Indian section, Paris Exhibn., 1900.

ROSE, CHARLES STUART, Major, R.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—First commn. 14th Feb., 1883; joined the dept. as asst. engr., 15th March, 1888; served with Hazara expdn., Feb. to June, 1891; exec. engr., Aug., 1897; offg. consg. engr. for rlys., Lucknow, April, 1905.

ROSE, EDWARD, C.V.O., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Provs. and Oudh).—Educ. at Folsingham Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 7th Nov., 1868, and served in the N.W. Prov. and in Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and asst. commr.; mag. and collr., Aug., 1887; offg. commr., Gorakhpur, April-Oct., 1894; commr., Agra, June, 1896; Meerut, April, 1899; C.V.O., 1900; ret'd., Dec., 1901.

ROSE, EDWARD ERNEST PILKINGTON, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Shrewsbury, Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 26th Nov., 1896, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

ROSE, FRANCIS ERNEST, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 1st Sept., 1865, and posted to Central Prov.; transfid. to the Punjab, Nov., 1866; exec. engr., Jan., 1877; ret'd., 1899.

ROSE, FREDERICK CAMPBELL, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 24th Aug., 1886, and posted to the Punjab; exec., engr., Sept., 1897; under sec. to govt. (irrigation), March, 1904.

ROSE, GEORGE PRINGLE, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1877; posted to state railways, Nov., 1878; exec. engr., Feb., 1891; C.I.E., 1892; lent to Nizam's govt., April, 1895, and to Nizam's State Rly. Co., July, 1897, to Oct., 1900; junr. consg. engr. for rlys., Calcutta, Oct., 1902; ret'd., March, 1904.

ROSE, HORACE ARTHUR, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 4th Oct., 1888, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dep. commr., March, 1893; supt. of gaz. revision, 1902.

ROSS, ALLAN, late Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Beaumont Coll., Windsor, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 7th Jan., 1892, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; ret'd., June, 1904.

ROSS, CHARLES EDMONDSTONE, India Public Works Dept. (examiner).—Apptd. to the superior accounts estabt., April, 1882; asst. examiner, May, 1884; dep. examiner, July, 1887; examiner, April, 1896.

ROSS, DAVID (divl. judge, Burma).—Joined the service, March, 1886, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Jan., 1899; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1902; divl. judge, March, 1905.

ROSS, SIR EDWARD CHARLES, Kt., C.S.I., Col., Indian Army, late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Ensign, 3rd European regt., Bombay, May, 1855; served in Central India during the Mutiny (medal and clasp); asst. to pol. agent, Muscat, 1863; asst. pol. agent, Gwadur, 1869; pol. agent and consul, Muscat, April, 1871; pol.

rest., Persian Gulf, Oct., 1872; also consul-gen. for Fars and the Persian Gulf, Nov., 1878; offg. rest., Hyderabad, April-Oct., 1886; reverted to mil. dept., 1891; Kt., Jan., 1892; author of several reports and memoranda on subjects connected with the Persian Gulf.

ROSS, GEORGE CUMBERLAND, Surgn.-Col., late Bengal Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 2nd Oct., 1865; arrived, 11th April, 1866; served from Nov., 1867, as civil surgn. in various stations in the Punjab; administrative med. officer and sanitary commr., Central Prov., Jan., 1894; inspr.-gen. of civil hosps., Bengal, March, 1895; ret'd. Oct., 1897.

ROSS, HERCULES GREY, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1856; arrived, 16th Nov., 1856; from April, 1858, served in the N.W. Prov. and in Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., asst. commr., and city mag. at Lucknow; supt., Dehra Dun, Dec., 1869; mag. and collr., Benares, Oct., 1882; settl. officer, Dehra Dun, Oct., 1883; commr., Kumaon, April, 1885; ret'd., Nov., 1888.

ROSS, JOHN ARCHIBALD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Winchester Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 11th Dec., 1900, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; transfid. to the Punjab, Sept., 1905.

ROSS, ROBERT LINDSAY, Indian C.S. (under sec. to govt., Bengal).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ. and Trin. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 7th Dec., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., genl. and rev. depts., Dec., 1904.

ROSSITER, CHARLES ARTHUR, India Office (supplemental clerk, registry and record dept.).—Apptd. supplemental clerk, 1st Jan., 1893.

ROTHFELD, OTTO, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 17th Nov., 1899, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

ROTTON, HORACE HERBERT GEORGE, Punjab Police Dept. (inspr.-genl. N.W. Frontier Prov.).—Joined the service, 23rd Feb., 1875, as offg. asst. dist. supt.; confid., Aug., 1876; dist. supt., Aug., 1885; asst. inspr.-gen., rly. police, Nov., 1901; transfid. to N.W. Frontier Prov., Jan., 1904; inspr.-genl., Sept., 1904.

ROUSE, ROBERT, Dep. Surgn.-Gen., late Med. Dept., Punjab.—Joined the Indian med. dept. in 1854; from May, 1869, served as civil surgn. and offg. supt. of jail in the Punjab; ret'd., Sept., 1885.

ROUTH, REGINALD SAMUEL JOHN, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; appt. 1st October, 1876, and posted to State Railways as asst. engr.; exec. engr., June, 1889; ret'd., Dec., 1903.

ROUTH, RUPERT JOHN AIRY, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Charterhouse Sch., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 12th Dec., 1904, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

ROWE, FREDERICK JAMES, M.A., late Educ. Dept., Bengal.—Joined the service, 15th June, 1870, and served as profr. at Krishnagar, Dacca, and Hugli colls., also as inspr. of schs.; profr.,

presidency coll., July, 1880; principal, presidency coll., Jan., 1899; retd., June, 1899; joint author of various educ. works, &c.

ROWE, JOSIAH EBENEZER, late Punjab Commn.—Joined the service, 20th March, 1858, and served in the Punjab in various capacities; extra asst. commr., Oct., 1865; small cause court judge in 1874, 1875, and 1881-84; extra jud. asst., Nov., 1884; retd., Nov., 1894.

ROWLAND, ALFRED, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1883, and posted to State rlys.; exec. engr., Feb., 1894; dep. consg. engr. for rlys., Lucknow, May, 1896, to Nov., 1897; lent to Bhavnagar-Gondal rly., from Aug., 1898.

ROXBURGH, JAMES, India Office (2nd class clerk, finl. dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 30th Dec., 1895.

ROY, JNANENDRA NATH, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., Univ. Coll., London, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 7th Dec., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfid. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905, as offg. dist. and sess. judge.

ROY, JOTINDRA NATH, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 25th Dec., 1899, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

ROY, K. N. (see Kedar Nath Roy).

ROYLE, JOSEPH R. E. J., C.I.E.—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., London; entered Indian army from Addiscombe, 7th June, 1861; retd., Jan., 1867; on the extra staff of the India museum, 1st Jan., 1867; sec., Indian commn. for Vienna exhibn., 1873; supt., India museum, 1874; clerk to the special asst. in the statistics and commerce dept., India office, 1879-91; exec. commr. for govt. of India, Amsterdam exhibn., 1883; asst. sec. for India to royal commn. for the Colonial and Indian exhibn., 1886, and official agent for the govt. of India; C.I.E., 1886; curator of Indian section, Imperial Inst., 1891; author of the official reports on the Indian sections of the Amsterdam and Colonial and Indian exhibns.; also of a lecture before the Society of Arts, June, 1882, on the 'Production of Tea, Cinchona, Wild Silks, and Rhea in India.'

RUDKIN, GEORGE DRURY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 5th Dec., 1903, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

RUNDALL, FRANCIS HORNBLow, C.S.I., Gen., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Madras engrs., 1847; asst. civil engr., 1844; civil engr., public works dept., Ganjam, 1854; district engr., Rajamahendri, May, 1855; consulting engr., Madras irrign. and canal co., Oct., 1859; chief engr. of irrign. and joint sec. to govt. of Bengal, July, 1867; chief engr., 1st class, Aug., 1869; inspr.-gen. of irrign. and dep. sec. to govt. of India, April, 1872, to April, 1874; C.S.I., Dec., 1875; to U.S. list, July 1881.

RUNDALL, JAMES WILLIAM, M.I.C.E., late Public Works Dept., Madras.—Joined the dept. as supernumerary engr., 2nd Aug., 1855; exec. engr., July, 1863; supt. of works, Kistna, Aug., 1880; suptg. engr., Oct., 1881; retd., 1889.

RUNDLE, CUBITT SINDALL, M.B., C.M., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surgn., Burma).—

First commn., 31st March, 1880; in mil. employ till Oct., 1882, since which date has held the appt. of civil surgn. at various stations in Burma.

RUSHTON, WILLIAM HAYWARD, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1882, and posted to Bengal; transfid. to N.W. Provs. and Oudh, June, 1892; exec. engr., May, 1894.

RUSSELL, ALEXANDER SHERWOOD, late Madras Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1875; asst. to chief engr. and under sec. to govt., irrign. branch, in 1882; exec. engr., April, 1886; retd., June, 1899.

RUSSELL, CHARLES LENNOX SOMERVILLE, Indian C.S. (under sec., foreign dept.).—Educ. at Rugby, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 1st Dec. 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec., jud., &c., depts., July, 1898; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1901; asst. to resdt. in Mysore, and sec. to chief commr., Coorg, Jan., 1902; under sec. to Govt. of India, foreign dept., March, 1903.

RUSSELL, EDGAR GEER, M.B., B.Sc., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872, and served on gen. and mil. duty, and in various offg. appts.; civil surgn., Patna, March, 1890; Darjiling, April, 1892; Dacca, March, 1894; profr., Calcutta med. coll., Sept., 1896; med. inspr. of emigrants, Jan., 1897; retd., Aug., 1900; author of a work on 'Malaria and Injuries of Spleen.'

RUSSELL, GEORGE WILLIAM ERSKINE, LL.D.—Educ. at Harrow, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; M.P., Aylesbury, 1880-85; parly. sec. to local govt. board, 1883-85; alderman of the county of London, Feb., 1889; M.P. for N. Bedfordshire, 1892-95; under sec. of state for India, Aug., 1892; under sec. of state, home dept., March, 1894, to July, 1895; LL.D., St. Andrew's, 1899; Alderman, London County Council, 1889-95; author of a 'Life of Mr. Gladstone,' and of 'Collections and Recollections,' and editor of 'The Letters of Matthew Arnold.'

RUSSELL, LOUIS PITMAN, Barr. (1875).—Apptd. judge, Bombay high court, Dec., 1898.

RUSSELL, REGINALD PEMBERTON, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1882, and posted to Punjab Irrigation Branch; exec. engr., Oct., 1894; under sec. to govt. Punjab, Feb., 1899, to March, 1904.

RUSSELL, ROBERT VANE, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Winchester, and Trinity Coll. Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 5th Sept., 1893, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; supt. of ethnography, March, 1902; ditto, and supt. of gazetteer revision, June, 1902; dep. commr., and ditto, ditto, March, 1903.

RUSSELL, SAMUEL, B.A., LL.B., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Santry Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 7th Nov., 1877, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. officer; special asst., Ganjam, Feb., 1885; sub-collr. and joint mag., April, 1890; district and sessions judge, Feb., 1893; retd., June, 1904.

RUSSICK LAL DUTT (see Dutt, R. L.).

RUSTAMJI DHANJIBHAI SETHNA, B.A., LL.B., Barr. (1884) (registrar, Bombay high court).—Joined the service, 27th March, 1886, as acting prof., Bombay law sch.; confd., Feb., 1886; registrar of the high court, appellate side, offg., Nov., 1893; confd., July, 1895.

RUSTAMJI MERWANJI PATEL, Khan Bahadur, M.A., LL.B. (judge, Bombay small cause court).—Joined the service, 1st Jan., 1894, as 2nd judge, Bombay small cause court; acting chief judge on various occasions.

RUSTOMJEE, C. (see Cursetjee Rustomjee).

RUTHERFURD, JAMES, Col. late Mysore Rev. Survey.—First commn., ensign, 4th Aug., 1859; joined the Mysore rev. survey dept., Jan., 1865; dep. supt., April, 1881; reverted to mil. dept., Nov., 1892; ret'd., Sept., 1898.

RYDER, CHARLES HENRY DUDLEY, Major, R.E., Survey of India (dep. supt.).—First commn., 24th July, 1886; apptd. to survey of India as asst. supt., Nov., 1891; dep. supt., March, 1897; served in China campaign, 1900 (medal and clasp); in charge of survey detachment with Tibet mission, 1903-04.

RYLAND, WILLIAM HENRY, late Jud. Dept., Bengal.—From 10th Dec., 1852, served in the subord. exec. service, Bengal; offg. collr. of stamps, and supt. of abkari rev., March, 1868; offg. asst. sec., govt. of Bengal, May, 1869; from Dec., 1874, served in the jud. dept. and was apptd. judge of the small cause court at Sealcah. April, 1875; offg. supt. of stamps and stationery, April, 1880; ret'd., April, 1887.

RYVES, ALFRED EDWARD, B.A., Barr. (1888).—Educ. at Clifton and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. govt. advocate, United Prov., 14th Nov., 1901.

RYVES, THOMAS JAMES, Local Capt., late Police Dept., N.W. Prov.—Served as a volunteer in the Mutiny (medal); adjutant of mil. police, Binjaur, 1859; asst. inspr.-gen., Rohilkhand, April, 1861; from Aug., 1861, served as supt. of police in various districts; asst. inspr.-gen., railway police, June, 1888; dep. inspr.-gen., June, 1892; ret'd., Jan., 1895.

SABONADIERE, ALFRED, Indian C.S. (dist. and sessions judge, United Prov.).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 19th Jan., 1888, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., and asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., March, 1893; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1898.

SACHSE, FREDERIC ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Liverpool Coll. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 15th Dec., 1902, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

SADLER, JAMES HAYES, C.B., Lieut.-Col., Indian Staff Corps, late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 5th July, 1870; mil. duty to July, 1877; offg. asst. pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, July, 1877; asst. pol. agent, Baroda, May, 1881; pol. agent, Muskat, Nov., 1892; asst. sec., govt. of India, foreign dept., April, 1895; asst. pol. rest., Aden, and pol. agent for Somali coast, Jan., 1897; ret'd., March, 1899; commr. in Uganda, Dec., 1901; C.B., June, 1902; commr. and commander-in-chief, British E. Africa, 1905.

SAHGAL, B. M. (see Buta Mal Sahgal).

ST. CLAIR, THE HON. LOCKHART MATHEW, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1876, and posted to Central Prov.; exec. engr., Jan., 1884; services lent to Nepal state, 1889-92; transf. to Assam, April, 1894; on special duty, office of chief commr. public works dept., Central Prov., April, 1897; supt. of works, Nagpur, Mar., 1900; supt.-engr. and sec. to chief commr., Central Prov., Feb., 1902; C.I.E., June, 1902; ret'd., July, 1904.

ST. QUINTIN, WILLIAM ASTELL (senr. clerk, India Audit Office).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Blackheath Proprietary Sch.; temporary clerk, 31st Dec., 1866; junr. examr., Dec., 1868; senr. examr., Nov., 1878; designation changed to senr. clerk, Oct., 1892.

SALAMAN, SELIM MYER, M.A., M.D., M.Ch., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Bombay Jail Dept.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1869; civil surgn. and supt. of jail, Bijapur, Nov., 1870; from Feb., 1872, served under the mil. dept. until June, 1874, when he was apptd. offg. civil surgn. and supt. of jail, Shikarpur; supt., Yerauda central jail, May, 1878; in addn. supt., Poona city jail, Dec., 1878; retired, March, 1897.

SALE, EDWARD LITTLE, B.A., Indian C.S. (2nd asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Bury St. Edmunds Sch., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1894, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. officer; 2nd asst., Feb., 1902.

SALE, STEPHEN GEORGE, M.A., Barr. (1876) (judge, high court, Calcutta).—Entered the service as offg. judge, high court, Calcutta, March, 1893; confd., Jan., 1896.

SALKELD, HENRY LYDE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch. and Oxford Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived 9th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Assam as asst. commr., July, 1899.

SALMON, ALEXANDER McDONALD, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Madras Civil Engrg. Coll.; apptd. 24th Sept., 1869, and posted to Bengal; employed in Bombay on famine relief works, 1877; district engr., Patna, 1889-95; exec. engr., Aug., 1893; ret'd., 1899.

SALMON, WILLIAM ARTHUR, Col., Indian Army, late Pol. Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 11th May, 1859; served from March, 1867, in the pol. dept.; asst. pol. agent in Kathiawar, June, 1870; from Feb., 1879, served as asst. pol. agent, Janjira; joint administrator of Rajpipla, July, 1884; and of Sangli, Feb., 1885; asst. to the govr.-gen.'s agent, Baroda, in charge of the Okhamandal district, and commandant Waghir corps, Nov., 1887; pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, Jan., 1892; pol. supt., Palanpur, July, 1893; acting pol. agent, Kolhapur, June, 1894; reverted to mil. dept., Aug., 1895.

SAMI-ULLA KHAN, M. (see Muhammad Sami-Ulla Khan).

SAMMAN, HERBERT FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Edward VI.'s Sch., Stratford-on-Avon, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 4th Nov., 1892, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Assam as asst. commr., April, 1894; retransf. to Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., Mar., 1897; joint mag.

and collr., July, 1899; mag. and collr., May, 1904; author of a monograph on the cotton fabrics of Assam.

SAMS, HUBERT ARTHUR, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Peterhouse, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 29th Dec., 1899, and served in the Punjab and N.W. Frontier Prov. as asst. commr.

SAMUELLS, CHARLES ARCHIBALD, Barr. (1869), late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., Blackheath Proprietary Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 25th Nov., 1869, and served in Bengal as asst. mag., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; on famine duty at Champaran, March, 1874; offg. port commr., Calcutta, April, 1886; mag. and collr., Oct., 1886; offg. commr. of excise, Bengal, April to Aug., 1892; ret'd., March, 1895.

SAMUELLS, WILLIAM LEYCESTER, Col., late I.S.C.—First commn., 20th Nov., 1857; served from 18th Dec., 1864, in the non-regulation commn., Bengal, as asst. commr. and dep. commr.; special boundary commr. (Rewa and Chota Nagpur), 1868-71; offg. cantonmt. mag. and small cause court judge, Barrackpur, April, 1873; offg. pol. agent, Hill Tipperah, April, 1875; ret'd., April, 1890.

SANDELL, JOSEPH WILLIAM HENRY, late Indian Postal Dept.—Joined the service, 1st March, 1862; chief asst. to dep. acctnt.-gen., Assam, Dec., 1878; chief supt., fin. dept., Jan., 1881; transfd. to office of acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Sept., 1881; comptr., Calcutta post office, Jan., 1890; ret'd., April, 1897.

SANDEMAN, JOHN EDWARD, Col., Indian Army, late Survey of India Dept.—Joined at Addiscombe, 1860; ensign, Bengal infantry, 7th June, 1861; studied at Rurki Coll., and obtained certificate qualifying for engr., public works dept., Sept., 1864; apptd. to the survey dept., Oct., 1864; served with the Hazara expedn., 1868 (medal); dep. supt., Jan., 1879; supt. (new scale), Aug., 1892; dir. of Bengal surveys, April, 1892; promoted to dep. surveyor-gen. (in charge, rev. surveys), in addn., March, 1895; returned to Europe, Sept., 1898.

SANDERS, EDWIN, Surgn.-Major, late Bengal Med. Dept.—Served under the govt. of Bengal from 1st Nov., 1869, to May, 1871; offg. civil surgn., Chittagong, March, 1881; civil surgn., Murshidabad, June, 1889; ret'd., Jan., 1891.

SANDERS, HENRY CHARLES, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1882, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; transfd. to the Punjab, Dec., 1893; exec. engr., Jan., 1897.

SANDERS, JAMES, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Prov.).—Educ. at the Coll. Sch. and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1879, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; offg. city mag., and wasika officer, Lucknow, 1886, 1888 and 1889; joint mag., March, 1888; dep. commr., April, 1894; dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1896.

SANDERS, RICHARD CARELESS, M.D., F.R.C.S., Lieut.-Col., late Bengal Med. Service.—First commn., 1869; in mil. employ till July, 1874, from which date he served as surgn. and civil surgn. at various stations in the

N.W. Prov.; ophthalmic surgn. and prof'r., med. coll., Calcutta, Aug., 1886; ret'd., July, 1900.

SANDHURST (BARON), RIGHT HON. WILLIAM MANSFIELD, G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E.—Born, 1855; educ. at Rugby; sub-lieut., Coldstream guards, 1875; succeeded his father (who had been chief of the staff during the Indian Mutiny, commander-in-chief, Bombay, 1860-65, commander-in-chief in India, 1865-70, and commander-in-chief, Ireland, 1870-75) in the title in 1876; resigned his commn., 1879; acted as a lord in waiting to H.M. the Queen from Sept., 1880, to June, 1885; under sec. of state for war, Feb.-Aug., 1886, and again from Aug., 1892; govr. of Bombay, Feb., 1895, to Feb., 1900; G.C.S.I., Mar., 1900.

SANDIFORD, CHARLES THOMAS, C.B., M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept. from Sind, Punjab, and Delhi railway as locomotive supt., N.W. state railway, Jan., 1886; ret'd., 1899; C.B., June, 1903.

SANDILANDS, PERCIVAL ARTHUR, Bengal Police Dept. (dist. supt. of police).—Apptd. offg. asst. supt. of police, 17th Aug., 1875; confd., Nov., 1881; dist. supt., Dec., 1889.

SANJANA, K.C. (see Kavasji Cursetji Sanjana).

SANKEY, SIR RICHARD HIERAM, K.C.B., Lieut.-Gen., late R.E.—Ensign, 11th Dec., 1846; arrived, Nov., 1848; offg. suptg. engr., Nagpursubsidiary force, 1850-53; supt. of the east coast canal, 1856-57; addl. under sec., govt. of India, public works dept., March, 1857; on outbreak of Mutiny proceeded with special appt. to Allahabad, acting for a time as suptg. engr. and consulting engr. for railways, N.W. Prov., acted as field engr. at Cawnpore, and was subsequently senr. engr. officer with the Gurkha force under Jung Bahadur; exec. engr. and supt. of convicts, Maulmain, Jan.-Nov., 1859, and subsequently garrison engr., Fort William; asst. to chief engr., Mysore, June, 1861; chief engr., Mysore, 1866-77; services placed at disposal of govt. of Victoria for reporting on the waterworks of the colony, 1871; dep. sec. to govt. of India, Sept., 1877; commanding engr. of the S. Afghanistan expedny. force under Lieut.-Gen. Stewart, C.B., 1878-79; sec. to govt. of Madras, public works dept., April, 1879; fellow of Madras univ., March, 1881; resigned the public works dept. in 1883; ret'd. from the army, Jan., 1884; chairman, board of works, Dublin, 1884-1896.

SANSBURY, WILLIAM HENRY, India Office (2nd class clerk, registry and record dept.).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 20th Nov., 1902.

SARADA CHARAN MITRA, M.A., B.L. (judge, high court, Calcutta).—Offd. as judge, high court, Calcutta, Feb. to Sept., 1902, March to Sept., 1903; confd. in that appt., 29th April, 1904.

SARAT KUMAR GHOSE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at the Metrop. instn. and the Univ., Calcutta, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

SARGEAUNT, RICHARD ARTHUR, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., Dec., 1861; joined the Bombay public works dept., May, 1864; asst. consulting engr. for

railways, Nov., 1864; served in the Abyssinian expdn. of 1868 (medal); examr. of railway accts., Bombay, April, 1872; dep. consulting engr. for guaranteed railways, Calcutta, May, 1877; sec. to railway conference, 1879; rev. asst., railway branch, public works secretariat, govt. of India, 1880; manager, N. Bengal state railway, July, 1882; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, railway branch, Feb., 1883; offg. manager, Punjab N. state railway, June, 1884; offg. manager of E. Bengal railway, June, 1887; offg. consulting engr. for railways, and joint sec., railway dept., Bombay, May, 1888; manager, Oudh and Rohilkhand railway, Jan., 1889; dir.-gen. of railways, and dep. sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., May, 1892; ret'd., Aug., 1895.

SASTRI, C. V. K. (see Kumaraswami Sastri, C. V.)

SATIS CHANDRA MUKERJEE (see Mukerjee, S. C.)

SATYENDRA CHANDRA MALLIK, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 8th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

SATYENDRA PRASANNA SINHA (Barr., 1886).—Apptd. standing counsel, Calcutta, 24th Dec., 1903.

SATYENDRANATH TAGORE, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Presidency Coll., and Calcutta Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 12th Dec., 1864, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., asst. judge, and judge and sessions judge; judge, Sholapur-Bijapur, Aug., 1883; ditto Satara, April, 1896; ret'd., Jan., 1897.

SAUNDERS, ARTHUR LESLIE, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at the Oratory Sch., Edgbaston, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1850; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1852, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to the Central Prov., Oct., 1885; asst. commr., Oct., 1888; judge, small cause court, March, 1892; commr. of excise, April, 1892; dep. commr., Feb., 1893; also second sec. to chief commr., Feb., 1894; acting chief sec., Nov., 1894, and May, 1895; acting commr., April to July, 1899; transf. to N.W. Prov. and Oudh, March, 1901; mag. and collr., Mar., 1901; dep. commr., Oct., 1902.

SAUNDERS, LESLIE HARRY, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 30th Nov., 1889, and served in Burma as asst. commr., and offg. under sec.; offg. supt. N. Shan states, Sept., 1893, to May, 1894; dep. commr., April, 1901.

SAVAGE, HENRY, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (member, board of rev., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Liverpool Inst.; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 23rd Oct., 1874, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Sept., 1885; mag. and collr., Jan., 1891; commr., Dacca, Nov., 1900; C.S.I., June, 1904; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam as member of board of rev., Oct., 1905.

SAVILLE, GEORGE ABREO, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1877, and posted to state railways as asst. engr.; lent to B. B. and C. I. Rly. from Jan., 1885, exec. engr., Sept., 1891; ret'd., Nov., 1904.

SAVORY, HENRY GEORGE SCOTT, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1879, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., Sept., 1891; dep. consulting engr. to govt. of India for railways, Lucknow, April, 1898; ditto, Calcutta, April, 1902, to Oct., 1903.

SCAMP, EDWARD, India Office (2nd class clerk, store dept.).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, 1st May, 1900.

SCHLICH, WILLIAM, C.I.E., Ph.D., late Forest Dept., Govt. of India.—Apptd. asst. conservator of forests, Dec., 1866; conservator, Bengal, Dec., 1872; transf. to the Punjab, 1880; inspr.-gen. of forests, April, 1883; ret'd., 1888, and was apptd. principal protr. of forestry at Cooper's Hill Coll.; author of 'A Manual of Forestry,' &c.

SCHOFIELD, SIDNEY CHARLES APPS, India Office (second class clerk, Acct.-Gen.'s dept.).—Educ. at Alleyn's Sch., Dulwich; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, acct.-gen.'s dept., 8th Nov., 1901.

SCHÖNEMANN, FRANK WAVERLING, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. apprentice engr., 4th May, 1887, and posted to the Punjab; asst. engr., Aug., 1889; served in Assam, May, 1894, to Nov., 1895; exec. engr., April, 1899.

SCHURR, HENRY SAMUEL, Bengal Police Dept. (dist. supt. of police).—Joined the service, 28th Sept., 1877, as offg. asst. supt. of police; confd., May, 1880; dist. supt., April, 1888.

SCOBIE, DONALD MACKAY, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1881, and posted to Hyderabad; transf. to Burma, Nov., 1895; exec. engr., Oct., 1899; ret'd., Oct., 1904.

SCOBIE, MACKAY JOHN, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1876, and posted to Hyderabad as assist. engr.; exec. engr., Aug., 1886; transf. to Burma, April, 1889; suptg. engr., Sept., 1903; transf. to Central Prov., Oct., 1903.

SCOBLE, RT. HON. SIR ANDREW RICHARD, P.C., K.C.S.I., K.C.—Called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, Jan., 1856; held the offices of clerk of the crown and commr. for taking accts. and for local investigations, under the high court, Bombay, 1863-66; advocate of the high court, 1866-76, and successively held the offices of sheriff, 1866-67, legal remembrancer, 1869-72, and advocate-gen., 1872-77; conducted the prosecution of the Gaekwar of Baroda, 1875; was also member of the legisl. council of Bombay, 1870-76; Q.C., 1876; legal member of the govr.-gen.'s council, 1886-91; C.S.I., Jan., 1889; K.C.S.I., May, 1890; M.P. for Central Hackney from 1892 to 1900; member of royal commn. on Indian expenditure, May, 1895; treasurer of Lincoln's Inn, 1899; member of judl. committee of the Privy Council, Dec., 1901.

SCONCE, GIDEON COLQUHOUN, Barr. (1864), late Jud. Dept., Bengal.—Offg. mag., Calcutta, 12th Nov., 1866; clerk of the Calcutta court of small causes, Dec., 1870; judge of the court, Jan., 1874; chief judge, Oct., 1889; ret'd., Oct., 1893.

SCONCE, JAMES, Col., Indian Army, late Survey Dept.—Joined at Addiscombe, Feb., 1852; lieut., Bengal artillery, Dec., 1853; served during the Mutiny (medal and clasps); served from Oct., 1859, as asst. and rev.

surveyor in Bundelkhand, Central Prov., and Bengal; dep. surveyor-gen. (rev. survey branch), Jan., 1879; offd. as surveyor-gen. of India; reverted to mil. duty, April, 1891.

SCOTSON, JOHN THOMAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Middlesborough High Sch. and Peterhouse Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived 23rd Nov., 1902, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

SCOTT, ARTHUR GUILLEM, India Office (acctnt.-gen.).—Educ. at the Royal Agricl. Coll., Cirencester, and at private schs.; East India house writer, old establ., 7th Nov., 1857; junr. clerk, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., India office, Aug., 1860; senr. clerk, Jan., 1872; assessor of income-tax, 1892; dep. acctnt.-gen., June, 1893; acctnt.-gen., May, 1900; clerk to commrs. of income tax, May, 1902.

SCOTT, ARTHUR TRAYER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov.).—Educ. at Rugby Sch. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1901, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; transfed. to N.W. Frontier Prov., Aug., 1905.

SCOTT, BASIL, B.A., Judicial Dept., Bombay (advocate-general).—Apptd. advocate-general, Bombay, 4th Feb., 1902.

SCOTT, SIR BUCHANAN, K.C.I.E., Col., R.E.—Joined the service, 2nd Aug., 1871; from Oct., 1874, served as asst. engr. in the Punjab; on field service in the Afghan war, 1878-79 (medal); exec. engr., May, 1883; supt. of works and engr.-in-chief, Zhob valley survey, Aug., 1888; acted as mint master, Calcutta, 1891; mint master, Bombay, March, 1895; ditto, Calcutta, April, 1897, to May, 1904; K.C.I.E., June, 1904; ret'd., Jan., 1905.

SCOTT, CHARLES HENRY, C.B., Major-Genl., R.A. (member of govr.-genl.'s council).—First commn., 8th Jan., 1868; served in the ordnance dept. in India from Nov., 1878 to Nov., 1892; ord. consg. offr. for India, Nov., 1892; dep. dir.-genl. of ord., India, April, 1895; insp.-genl. of ord., India, June, 1895; served in the Tirah expedn., 1897-98; C.B., May, 1898; again ord. consg. offr. for India, April, 1900; dir.-genl. of ord., India, March, 1902; member of govr.-genl.'s council, Nov., 1905.

SCOTT, EDWIN, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., Balliol Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1834; arrived, 15th Jan., 1887, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., July, 1893; sub-collr. and joint mag., May, 1898; collr. and mag., March, 1904.

SCOTT, FREDERICK WILLIAM MORTIMER, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1879, and posted to Central India; exec. engr., Feb., 1890; transfed. to Central Prov., Nov., 1890.

SCOTT, GAVIN, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Glasgow Univ., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 20th Dec., 1899, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and asst. settlmt. offr.

SCOTT, GEORGE BATLEY, late Survey of India Dept.—Joined the service, 1st May, 1863; asst. supt., survey dept., Nov., 1884; dep. supt., April, 1890; supt. of land record surveys, N.W.

Prov. and Oudh, Dec., 1894; ret'd., July, 1901; served in the Black Mountain expedn., Jowaki-Afridi expedn., Afghan war, Zhob valley expedn., &c.; author of a Pushto vocabulary, 'Pathans of the N.W. Frontier,' reports on various parts of Afghanistan, &c.

SCOTT, SIR JAMES GEORGE, K.C.I.E., Barr. (1896), Pol. Dept., Burma (supdt. Southern Shan States).—Apptd. asst. commr., 9th April, 1886; on duty in the Shan states, Dec., 1886; asst. supt., Shan states, Dec., 1888; on duty with Anglo-Siamese boundary commn., 1889-90; supt., N. Shan states, July, 1892; chargé d'affaires at Bangkok, Nov., 1893; on depn. with Anglo-French buffer state commn., Oct., 1894; on duty with Burma-China boundary commn., Nov., 1898; dep. commr., Aug., 1900; K.C.I.E., Nov., 1901; supdt. S. Shan states, March, 1902; author of 'The Burman, His Life and Notions,' 'France and Tong-king,' 'Burma as it was, as it is, and as it will be,' 'Burma, the Eastern Country, and the Race of the Bramahs,' and 'The Hill Slopes of Tong-king.'

SCOTT, MATHEW HENRY, late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 16th Dec., 1863, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and asst. judge and sessions judge; judge, Khandesh, Feb., 1881; judge and sessions judge, Ahmadnagar, May, 1882; district judge, Satara, April, 1892; ret'd., May, 1893.

SCOTT, ROSS, M.A., Barr. (1878), Indian C.S. (judl. commr., Oudh).—Educ. at Omagh Acad., the Royal Acadl. Instn. and Seminary, Belfast, and Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 26th Oct., 1873, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Nov., 1886; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1890; judl. commr., Oudh, March, 1901.

SCOTT, THOMAS AUGUSTUS, Col., Indian Army, late Central Prov. Commn.—First commn., June, 1857; arrived, 29th June, 1857; from Jan., 1862, served in the police dept., Central Prov., and was apptd. district supt., March, 1864; from June, 1865, served as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Dec., 1882; offd. as commr., 1890-91; reverted to mil. dept., Aug., 1894.

SCOTT, WALTER LAWRENCE, M.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Canterbury Coll., New Zealand; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived 12th Dec., 1904, and served in Bengal as asst. and collr.

SCOTT, VEN. WILLIAM EDWARD, M.A., Bombay Ecclesi. Establ. (archdeacon of Bombay).—Joined the service, 28th Oct., 1885, as junior chaplain; senior chaplain, Oct., 1895; ditto, and archdeacon and commissary, Bombay, April, 1897.

SCOTT-MONCRIEFF, SIR COLIN CAMPBELL, K.C.M.G., K.C.S.I., Col., R.E., ret'd.—Joined the service as 2nd lieut., Bengal engrs., 13th June, 1856; arrived, 10th June, 1858; served with the Bengal sappers and miners until Feb., 1859, through the campaign in Oudh (medal); joined public works dept., Feb., 1859, and served in the N.W. Prov. in the irrig. branch; principal, Thomason civil engrg. coll., Sept., 1861; asst. to chief engr., irrig. branch, April, 1869; from Nov., 1869, served on the Ganges canal as exec. and suptg. engr.; in 1877 was sent on depn. to Mysore to serve as chief engr. and famine commr.; C.S.I., May, 1878; again joined the

irrign. dept. of the N.W. Prov., Feb., 1879; offd. as sec. to the chief commr. of British Burma, May, 1881; ret'd., Jan., 1883; K.C.M.G., 1887; under sec., public works ministry, Egypt, to 1892, when he was apptd. under sec. for Scotland; president of Indian irrig. commn., 1901; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1903; member of board of visitors, R.I.E. Coll., Jan., 1903.

SCOTT-MONCRIEFF, GEORGE KENNETH, C.I.E., Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commr., Sept., 1873; apptd. to the Public Works Dept. as asst. engr., Feb., 1878; exec. engr., 1884; reverted to imperial duty 1886; reappointed to public works dept., Punjab, Dec., 1898; on field service with China expedy. force, Aug., 1900; C.I.E., July, 1901; sec. to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., public works dept., 1901; comdg. R.E. Curragh, April, 1904.

SCOTT-O'CONNOR, VINCENT CLARENCE, B.A., Fin. Dept., Govt. of India (dep. acctnt.-genl., E. Bengal and Assam).—Joined the dept., 19th May, 1890; asst. comptr., Burma, April, 1892; inspr. of local fund acctnts., Burma, Oct., 1893; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Dec., 1897; examr. of local fund acents., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Jan., 1898; asst. acctnt.-gen., Burma, Dec., 1899; comptr., Assam, July, 1902; dep. acctnt.-genl., E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

SCOVELL, CHARLES THORNTON RENNIE, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1885, and posted to state rlys.; dep. consg. engr. for rlys., Madras, March, 1893; exec. engr., Sept., 1895; dep. consg. engr. for rlys., Bombay, April, 1899.

SCRATCHLEY, ARTHUR JAMES, India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1880, and posted to the Punjab (irrigation); exec. engr., Aug., 1893; suptg. engr. Aug., 1905.

SCROOPE, ARTHUR EDGAR, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Clongowes Wood Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 12th Dec., 1904, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

SCROOPE, HENRY WILLIAM PAUL, M.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Stonyhurst Coll., London Univ., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 24th Dec., 1895, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1903.

SCUDAMORE, WALTER VICTOR, Major R.E., Indian Educl. Service (principal and prof. of civil engrng., coll. of science, Poona).—First commn., 16th Sept., 1885; asst. engr., mil. works dept., Jan., 1888; ditto, Mu Valley rly., Nov., 1889; ditto, Bombay public works dept., March, 1892; exec. engr., July, 1898; employed on rly. works in S. Africa, 1899-1902; principal and prof. of civil engrng., Coll. of Science, Poona, April, 1903.

SCULLY, JOHN, Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Service.—Joined the dept., 1872; served as med. officer, Kashgarh pol. agency, 1874-76; residency surgn., Nipal, 1876-78; med. officer, Gilgit, 1878-80; acting dep. assay master, Calcutta mint, 1882-83; dep. assay master of the Bombay mint, May, 1884; assay master of the Calcutta mint, May, 1886; ret'd., April, 1899.

SEABROOKE, JAMES HERBERT, B.A., India Office (asst. sec., rev. and statistics dept.).—Educ. at King's Coll., London, and Queen's Coll., Oxford (scholar, 1871-74); junr. clerk, finl. dept., 13th Jan., 1876; mil. dept., 1877; senr. clerk, June, 1887; asst. sec., rev. and statistics dept., April, 1900.

SEAL, B. K. (see Brojendra Koomar Seal).

SEALY, CHARLES WILLIAM HENRY, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army.—First commn., 17th July, 1866; cantonmt. mag., Aden, May, 1875; 2nd asst. to rest., Oct., 1885; asst. pol. agent, S. Mahratia country, May, 1889; 2nd asst. to pol. agent, Kathiawar, Sept., 1889; 1st asst. to rest., Aden, and pol. agent for the Somali coast, Aug., 1893; acting pol. rest., Aden, May-July, 1894; pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, March, 1896; alienation settlt. officer, Junaghad, Feb., 1897; pol. supt., Palanpur, Mar., 1898; pol. agent, Kolhapur, June, 1900 to April 1902; joint compiler of an account of the Arab tribes in the vicinity of Aden, and has transld. Van Den Berg's work on Hadhramuth, and part of Carsten Niebuhr's description of Arabia.

SEARIGHT, GRAVES LEMPRIER, India Public Works dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 29th Jan., 1886, and posted to Bengal; exec. engr., Sept., 1896; under sec. to govt., June, 1905.

SEATON, WILLIAM JOHN, Col., Indian Army, late Forest Dept., Burma.—Ensign, Madras infantry, Sept., 1855; asst. supt. in the forest dept., Dec., 1860; dep. conservator, 1st class, April, 1865; conservator, Nov., 1867; reverted to mil. duty, 1892.

SEDDON, CHARLES NORMAN, Indian C.S. (settlmt. commr., Baroda).—Educ. at Liverpool Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 25th Nov., 1891, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., April, 1897; admtr. of Navanagar State, May, 1901; 1st asst., March, 1903; asst. resdt., Baroda, Sept., 1903, to May, 1904; settlmt. commr., Baroda, Nov., 1904.

SELBY, FRANCIS GUY, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (principal, Deccan Coll.).—Educl. at Durham Sch., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; arrived, 7th Feb., 1877, and served from that date as prof. of logic and moral philosophy in the Deccan coll., Poona; principal and ditto, Deccan coll., Dec., 1890; offg. director of public instruction, Bombay, June, 1902, to May, 1903.

SELLS, ARTHUR, B.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1859; arrived, 28th Nov., 1860; from Nov., 1861, served the in N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; mag. and collr., Muzaffarnagar, Aug., 1878, and civil and sessions judge, Cawnpore, April, 1882; district and sessions judge, Meerut, Oct., 1886; ret'd., May, 1890.

SEN, A.C. (see Ambica Charan Sen).

SEN, BIRENDRA CHANDRA, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Dacca Coll., Presidency Coll., and the Univ., Calcutta, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 17th Nov., 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., June, 1900.

SEN, D. C. (see Durga Charan Sen).

SEN, L. G. (see Lal Gopal Sen).

SEN, P. N. (see Preo Nath Sen).

SETHNA, R. D. (see Rustamji Dhanjibhai Sethna).

SETON, MALCOLM COTTER CARISTON, B.A., India Office (senior clerk, finl. dept.)—Educ. at Repton Sch., and Oriel Coll., Oxford (classical scholar); selected candidate, Indian Civil Service, Sept., 1895; junior clerk, judl. and pub. dept., India Office, May, 1898; resdt. clerk, Nov., 1898, to April, 1901; temporarily transf. for duty in the mil. dept., July to Oct., 1900; priv. sec. to parly. under sec. of state, March, 1901; senior clerk, finl. dept., Nov. 1902.

SETON-KARR, WALTER SCOTT, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Rugby; apptd. from Haileybury in 1842; arrived, 12th Dec., 1842, and served in Bengal as asst. and joint mag. and dep. collr.; mag. of Jessore, 1847; under sec., govt. of Bengal, 1847-53; collr. of Tirhut, 1857; civil and sessions judge, Jessore, 1858-60; prest. of indigo commn., 1860; sec. to govt. of Bengal, 1860-61; legisl. council of India, 1861; commr. of rev. and circuit, Chittagong, and member of provl. legisl. council, 1862; judge, high court, Calcutta, 1862-68; foreign sec., govt. of India, 1868; ret'd., 1870; vice-chancellor of the univ. of Calcutta in 1868 and 1869.

SEWELL, EBENEZER JAMES, late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam of 1870; arrived, 1st Nov., 1872, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on famine duty, 1877-78; senr. asst., Ganjam, June, 1882; sub-collr. and joint mag., Dec., 1887; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1889; collr. and mag., Feb., 1890; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1891; ret'd., July, 1897.

SEWELL, HENRY, M.A., late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Radley, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1861; served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr., acting under sec., and sub-collr.; collr. and mag., Trichinopoly, May, 1875; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1886; collr. and mag., S. Arcot, Nov., 1887; ret'd., Jan., 1891.

SEWELL, ROBERT, late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at St. Peter's Coll., Radley; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 19th Dec., 1868, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on famine duty, Oct., 1877, and on special duty in connection with the archaeological survey of the Madras presidency, Jan., 1881, and again, Jan., 1883; sub-collr. and joint mag., Tinnevelly, Sept., 1884; fellow of Madras univ., 1887; from Sept., 1888, served as district and sessions judge, and collr. and mag.; ret'd., Dec., 1894; author of 'A Report on the Amaravati Tope, and Excavations on its Site,' 'Chronological Tables for S. India from the 6th Century, A.D.,' 'A Sketch of the Dynasties of S. India,' vols. I. and II. of the 'Archaeological Survey Series of S. India,' 'Early Buddhist Symbolism,' &c.

SHACKLETON, WILLIAM, F.R.A.S., India office (asst. inspr. of scientific supplies, India Store depôt).—Educ. at Royal Coll. of Science, London (associate); elected fellow of Royal Astronom. Soc., 1893; spectroscopist, govt. expdn. to Brazil, 1893; accompanied Sir G. Baden-Powell's expdn. to Nova Zembla, 1896; asst., solar physics observatory, 1892-1900; asst., physics divn., Royal Coll. of Science, 1903-1905; asst. inspr. of scientific supplies, India Store depôt, 15th Aug., 1905.

SHADBOLT, ERNEST IFILL, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (dir. of railway construction).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1874, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., May, 1883; services lent to Bhavnagar and Gondal states, 1887-91; engr.-in-chief, Bezvada-Madras railway survey, Feb., 1894; employed in same capacity on Madura-Pamban and Tinnevelly-Quilon railway surveys, Dec., 1894; supt. of works, Kotri-Rohri railway, Dec., 1895; suptg. engr., May, 1899; offg. dir. of rly. construction, Jan., 1904; confd., May, 1905; chief engr., Nov., 1904.

SHAKESPEAR, ALEXANDER MUIRSON WAKE, Indian C.S. (commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Harrow; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 17th Nov., 1878, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Aug., 1887; private sec. to lieut.-govr., March, 1888; asst. commr., Jan., 1890; dep. commr., Feb., 1894; mag. and collr., Nov., 1895; dist. and sess. judge, April, 1896; mag. and collr., 1st grade, March, 1897; commr., April, 1903.

SHAKESPEAR, JOHN, C.I.E., D.S.O., Major, Indn. Army (pol. agent, Manipur).—First commn., 22nd Jan., 1881; asst. pol. offr., Chittagong Hills, May, 1890; supt., S. Lushai Hills, April, 1891; C.I.E., May, 1896; asst. commr., April, 1896; dep. commr., Nov., 1897; ditto and supt., Lushai Hills, April, 1898; ditto, and pol. agent, Manipur, March, 1905.

SHAM NATH, RAI BAHADUR, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. apprentice engr., 17th Oct., 1877, and posted to Central India; served in N.W.P. and O., 1877-1879; asst. engr., Aug., 1882; exec. engr., Jan., 1896; K-i-H. medal, 2nd class, Jan., 1905.

SHANKER MADHO CHITNAVIS, B.A., Statutory C.S. (dep. commr., Central Prov.).—Apptd. prob. for the civil service under the statutory rules, July, 1885; confd. as asst. commr., 5th Oct., 1887; dep. commr., Dec., 1896; K-i-H. medal, Nov., 1901.

SHARP, FREDERICK, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1875, and posted to Mysore; on famine duty, 1877; transf. to Coorg, April, 1881; exec. engr., May, 1883; transf. to Central Prov., Dec., 1886; and to Burma, March, 1887; ret'd., May, 1897.

SHARP, FREDERICK WILLIAM, India Office, (supervisor, Store Dept.).—Educ. at Milton Coll., Ullesthorpe; clerk, 2nd class, India store depôt, March, 1885; supervisor, 2nd grade, Oct., 1901.

SHARP, HENRY, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (inspr. of schools, Central Prov.).—Apptd. princpl., High Sch., Jubbulpore, Central Prov., 15th Oct., 1894; inspr. of sch., Dec., 1898; K-i-H. medal, May, 1900; author of 'An Outline of Indian History.'

SHARP, HENRY GRANVILLE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Horncastle Gr. Sch., Marlborough Coll., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 27th Jan., 1868; served in the rev. and jud. depts. in Bengal; on famine duty at Madras, 1877; joint mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1878; mag. and collr., Oct., 1884; ret'd., Jan., 1894.

SHARP, WILLIAM HASTINGS, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (prof. of logic and moral philosophy, Elphinstone Coll., Bombay).—Educ.

at Marlborough Sch. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; entered the service, 4th May, 1891, and received the appointment he now holds.

SHARPIN, VEN. FREDERICK LLOYD, M.A., late Eccles. Dept., Bombay.—Educ. at Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, Aug., 1865; arrived, 13th Oct., 1865; served as chaplain at various stations in Bombay and Sind; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1875; senr. presidency chaplain, June, 1880; archdeacon and commissary, June, 1886, also chaplain of Satara; ret'd., Oct., 1888.

SHAW, ARTHUR TREVOR AMBROSE, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 13th Dec., 1883, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Burma as asst. commr., Feb., 1886; dep. commr., Oct., 1890.

SHAW, CHARLES G., late India Office.—East India House writer, old estabt., 17th Jan., 1855; junr. clerk, store dept., July, 1855; senr. clerk, June, 1860; asst. to dir.-gen. of stores, Nov., 1876; ret'd., Nov., 1889.

SHAW, GEORGE WATSON, Indian C.S. (judl. commr., Upper Burma).—Educ. at Hertford Gr. Sch., and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 20th Dec., 1879, and served in British Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., May, 1888; commr., March, 1901; offg. judl. commr., Upper Burma, June, 1904; confd., May, 1905.

SHAW, WILLIAM ROBERT, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1880, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., June, 1891; dep. consg. engr. for rlys., Bombay, May, 1896, and March, 1901; ret'd., Dec., 1903.
