SHAW-STEWART, JOHN HERON MAXWELL, Col., R.E., late Public Works Dept., Madras.—First commn. as lieut., Madras engrs., June, 1851; arrived, 31st Dec., 1853; served in the dept. from Jan., 1854, as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; served in the China campaign, 1860 (medal and two clasps); dep. sec. to govt., public works dept., Dec., 1863; under sec., Oct., 1866; compt., public works accts., Madras, May, 1867; consulting engr. for railways, Madras, Oct., 1867; chief engr., Oct., 1870; joint sec. to govt., railway branch, Feb., 1877; sec. to govt., public works dept., Sept., 1883; member of legisl. council, Madras, Sept., 1883; ret'd., Sept., 1886.

SHEARME, DAVID, Indian C.S. (asst. dir. of statistics).—Educ. at Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1898, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to govt., Feb., 1902; under sec. to govt., April, 1903; private sec. to lieut.-govr., Dec., 1904; asst. dir. of statistics, govt. of India, Aug., 1905.

SHEEPSHANKS, RICHARD, B.A., Barr. (1895), Indian C.S. (dep. sec. to govt. of India, legisl. dept.).—Educ. at Winchester, and at St. John's Coll., Cambridge, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 27th Dec., 1895, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., finl. and muncl. dept., May, 1899; registrar, high court, Calcutta, Sept., 1901; dep. sec. to govt. of India, legisl. dept., Jan., 1905.

SHELLEY, ARCHIBALD DOUGLAS GRAHAM, Major, R.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec.

engr.).—First commn., 26th July, 1881; apptd. to mil. works dept. as asst. engr., Jan., 1885; transf. to state rlys., May, 1888; dep. consg. engr. for rlys., and under sec. to govt., Madras, Jan., 1894; exec. engr., Sept., 1894; lent to South Indian Rly. Co. from Oct., 1895.

SHEPARD, ARTHUR WILLIAM, Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; entered the service, 29th Jan., 1885, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Nov., 1898.

SHEPHARD, HORATIO HALE, M.A., Barr. (1867), India Office (legal adviser to sec. of state).—Profr. of law at Presidency Coll., Madras, 1st Oct., 1873; fellow of Madras univ., 1881; apptd. govt. pleader, June, 1881; member of Madras legisl. council, March, 1885; advocate-gen. of Madras and offg. judge of high court, Jan., 1888; confd. as judge of high court, July, 1889; offg. chief justice, May, 1898; ret'd. July, 1901; apptd. legal adviser and solicitor to sec. of state, 27th April, 1902.

SHEPHERD, CHARLES EDWARD, Col., Indian Army, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd., 26th Sept., 1864, and posted to Central Prov.; exec. engr., Sept., 1868; transf. to mil. works, Dec., 1872; and to state railways, Nov., 1874; dep. consulting engr. for railways, Lucknow, May, 1891; ret'd. to Europe, March, 1898.

SHEPHERD, GEORGE EDWARD, India Office (second class clerk, judl. and public dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 4th Sept., 1893; transf. to judl. and public dept., Nov., 1905.

SHEPHERD, WALTER CURZON, B.A., Indian C.S. (2nd asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Sedbergh Sch. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 19th Nov., 1894, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., Nov. 1901; ditto, and asst. judge, Feb., 1902; ditto, and W. Bhil agent, Jan. 1904.

SHEPPARD, GEORGE FREDERICK, late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1854, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; collr. of salt rev., Bombay, Dec., 1871; collr. and mag., Kaira, Feb., 1873; senr. collr. and mag. and pol. agent, Kaira, Nov., 1875; commr., Oct., 1882; ret'd., May, 1889.

SHEPPARD, WILLIAM DIDSBURY, Indian C.S. (muncl. commr. for the city of Bombay).—Educ. at Hereford Cathedral Sch. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 13th Dec., 1886, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst. and forest settl. officer, Nov., 1893; 1st asst., March, 1897; K.-i-H. medal, May, 1900; junr. collr., Feb., 1902; ditto, and pol. agent, Bhur, March, 1904; muncl. commr. for city of Bombay, March, 1905.

SHERER, JOHN WALTER, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1846; served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. and joint mag., and asst. sec. to govt.; on special duty to report on reh. efflorescence, W. Jumna canal, 1856-57; mag. and collr. of Cawnpore, 1857; civil officer with Gen. Havelock's column in 1857, in the advance on Cawnpore; commr. to enquire into claims against ex-king of Oudh, 1864; judge of Allahabad, 1871, and afterwards of Azimgarh and Mirzapur; ret'd., 1877.

SHERER, WALTER MOYLE, Police Dept., United Prov. (dep. inspr.-genl.).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1877, and acted as asst. dist.

supt.; confd., Sept., 1881; dist. supt., Feb., 1884; served in the S. Afghanistan campaign, 1880-81; dep. inspr.-genl., April, 1905.

SHERIDAN, CHARLES JAMES, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 1st April, 1872, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Feb., 1876; employed on state railways, 1876-78; ret'd., Oct., 1897.

SHERIDAN, PHILIP, late Post Office Dept., Govt. of India.—Served in the postal dept., Punjab, from March, 1861; chiefsupt., travelling post office, Allahabad, Dec., 1873; chief inspr. of post offices, Assam, Dec., 1873; asst. dir.-gen. of the post office of India, July, 1880; inspr. of railway and mail service, Nov., 1885; dep. dir.-gen. of the post-office of India, April, 1888; postmr.-gen., Punjab, July, 1891; ret'd., Jan., 1899.

SHERMAN, WILLIAM HANBURY PETTINGALL, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 31st July, 1866, and employed in the Central Prov.; transf'd. to state railways, April, 1874; exec. engr., Jan., 1877; services lent to S. Indian railway co., Feb., 1880, and to Gwalior durbar for Bhopal-Ujjain line, Feb., 1891; suptg. engr., Nov., 1892; ret'd., April, 1899.

SERRING, CHARLES ATMORE, M.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., United Prov.)—Educ. at Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 11th Nov., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; joint mag., July, 1898; dist. and sess. judge, March, 1900; dep. commr., April, 1903.

SERRING, FRANK BRODIE, LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.)—Educ. at Westminster and Trin. Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 21st Nov., 1898, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. settlmt. offr.

SHERRY, WALTER CAMPBELL, India Office (2nd class clerk, India store depôt)—Educ. at Dorchester Gr. Sch.; apptd. 2nd class clerk, 31st Aug., 1904.

SHETTLÉ, PHILIP SHAKESPEARE, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal)—Educ. at Oundle Sch., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 1st Dec., 1904, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

SHEWAN, ALEXANDER, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay)—Educ. at the Gymnasium, Aberdeen, and Univ. of Aberdeen; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 23rd Oct., 1873, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and asst. pol. agent; on famine duty in 1876 and 1877; from Jan., 1885, served as asst. judge and sessions judge, and acting under sec. to govt.; administrator of Rajpipla state, Nov., 1887; judge and sessions judge, July, 1894; ret'd., Dec., 1897.

SHIPLEY, REGINALD HEBER, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras)—Educ. at Winchester and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 20th Dec., 1883, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; special asst., July, 1890; sub. collr. and joint mag., Sept., 1895; collr. and mag., Aug., 1898.

SHIRRES, LESLIE PITTENDRIGH, B.A., Indian C.S. (sec. to govt., fin. and municipal depts., Bengal)—Educ. at Aberdeen and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 18th Dec., 1882, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., Jan., 1888; offd. as under sec. to the govt. of India,

dept. of fin. and commerce, in Nov., 1887, and again from April, 1888; joint mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1890; mag. and collr., Feb., 1896; sec. to govt., fin. and municipal depts., May, 1903; author of 'An Analysis of the Ideas of Economics.'

SHOPLAND, EDWIN REW, Comdr., late R.I.M.—Joined the service 18th Feb., 1867; comdr., 1st April, 1887; port officer, Karachi, Sept., 1895; ret'd., March, 1897.

SHORE, ROBERT, M.D., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (resdy. surgn., Mewar)—First commn., 29th Sept., 1883; offd. as agency surgn., Kotah and Jhalawar, in 1887, 1888, 1889 and 1891, and as resdy. surgn., Nepal, in 1888 and 1890; med. offr., Mewar Bhil Corps., March, 1893; resdy. surgn., Mewar, June, 1898; civil surgn., Quetta, Sept., 1900; ditto, Alwar, May, 1901; again resdy. surgn., Mewar, May, 1902; K-i-H. medal, Jan., 1906.

SHOWERS, EDWARD MELIAN, late Bengal Police Dept.—Entered the service, Feb., 1867, and served as asst. supt. and dist. supt. of police; ret'd., Sept., 1901.

SHOWERS, HERBERT LIONEL, C.I.E., Major, Indian Army, Pol. Dept., Govt. of India (pol. agent, Kalat)—First commn., May, 1883; entered pol. dept., July, 1890; asst. to the pol. agent in Baluchistan, Nov., 1896; pol. agent and dep. commr., Quetta and Peshin, Dec., 1899; pol. agent, Kalat, in charge of Bolan Pass, Dec., 1900; C.I.E., June, 1902.

SHIRAM BHIKAJI JATAR, C.I.E., B.A., Rao Bahadur, late Hyderabad Administration.—Served in the Bombay educ. dept. from Jan., 1859, and from July, 1867, in Berar as dep. educ. inspr. and educ. inspr.; dir. of public instrn., Hyderabad assigned districts, Aug., 1884; ret'd., Sept., 1894; C.I.E., May, 1894.

SHUCKBURGH, JOHN EVELYN, B.A., India Office (private sec. to permanent under-sec. of state for India)—Educ. at Eton, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. junior clerk, store dept., India Office, Oct., 1900; transf'd. to judl. and public dept., March, 1901; joint editor, 'India Office List,' Oct., 1901; private sec. to permanent under-sec. of state, July, 1902.

SHURLOCK, HENRY JAMES, India Office (second class clerk, store dept.)—Clerk, 2nd class, India store depôt, 5th Aug., 1886.

SHUTTLEWORTH, ALLEN THORNTON, late Forest Dept., Bombay.—Apptd. to the Indian marine, Nov., 1855; served in the Persian war (medal), and in the operations against the Waghirs in Okhamandal; from Nov., 1863, served in the dept. as asst. and dep. conservator, and was apptd. conservator of forests and supt. of botanical gardens, Sept., 1869; addl. member of Bombay legisl. council, July, 1895; on special famine duty, Nov., 1896-June, 1897; ret'd., April, 1899; while stationed at Alibag was instrumental in saving upwards of a hundred lives from different wrecks (Albert medal, medals of the Royal Humane Society, &c.).

SHUTTLEWORTH (BARON), RIGHT HON. SIR UGHTRED JAMES KAY-SHUTTLEWORTH.—Educ. at Harrow and London Univ.; M.P. for Hastings, 1869-80; served for two years on the London sch. board, and was also a member of the royal commn. on reformatory and industrial schools; from 1885, M.P. for the Clitheroe division of N.E. Lancashire; apptd. under sec. of state for India in Feb., 1886, and shortly

afterwards chancellor of the duchy of Lancaster; partly, sec., admiralty, Aug., 1892, to Aug., 1895; was for some time vice-prest. of Univ. Coll., London; created Baron 1902.

SIBOLD, ERNEST ALFRED, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 23rd Dec., 1864, and employed on irrig. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., Sept., 1872; in 1874 served in Bengal on famine relief works; ret'd., Feb., 1895.

SIRTHORPE, CHARLES, C.B., Surgn.-Gen., late Indian Med. Service, F.R.C.P.I., Madras Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., 1st April, 1870; arrived, 27th Dec., 1870; civil surgn. and supt. of jail, Banda, July, 1874; from May, 1875, to Nov., 1880, served as rest. surgn., gen. hosp., Madras, and prof. in the med. coll.; also served in the Afghan war, 1878-79 (medal), and in the Burma expdn., 1885-86; from Oct., 1882, served as fort surgn., and prof. in the med. coll.; fellow of Madras univ., 1884; surgn., gen. hosp., and prof., med. coll., Jan., 1888; reverted to mil. duty, May, 1890; surgn.-gen., Madras, May, 1894; C.B., June, 1897; author of a 'Clinical Manual,' and various papers on med. subjects; ret'd., Aug., 1900.

SIFTON, JAMES DAVID, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 27th Nov., 1902, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlm. off.

SILBERRAD, CHARLES ARTHUR, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Aldenham Gr. Sch., St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge, and London Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 10th Dec., 1894, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and joint mag.; asst. commr., Oct., 1904; author of a monograph on cotton fabrics.

SILCOCK, ALEXANDER, M.D., Lieut.-Col., Indian M.S. (civil surg., Cent. Prov.).—Joined the service, 2nd April, 1881; civil surg., Bilaspur, June, 1888; ditto, Raipur, Sept., 1903.

SILCOCK, HENRY FOSTER, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Drogheda Gr. Sch. and Kingstown Sch., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 24th Nov., 1874, and served in Bombay as asst. mag. and collr., and forest settlm. officer; on famine duty in 1876 and 1877; 2nd asst., April, 1887; 1st asst., April, 1890; collr. and mag., Aug., 1893; acting commr., May, 1898; ret'd., Oct., 1900.

SILCOCK, JAMES GREER, B.A., B.L., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Armagh Royal Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 16th Nov., 1872, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; district judge, June-Nov., 1888, and March, 1889; dep. commr., April, 1889; ret'd., June, 1903.

SILK, ALBERT EDWARD, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works dept. (supg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1882, and posted to Bengal; exec. engr., May, 1894; sany. engr., Bengal, Dec., 1895; supg. engr., March, 1903.

SILLERY, CECIL CHARLES ARCHIBALD, Major, Indian Army (commndt., mil. police, Burma).—First commn. 10th March, 1883; in mil. employ till Dec., 1892, when he joined

Burma police dept., as asst. commdt. of mil. police; commdt., June, 1896.

SIM, GEORGE GALL, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Aberdeen Univ., and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1901, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.

SIM, HENRY ALEXANDER, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (member, rev. board, Madras).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 19th Nov., 1878, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; from Jan., 1883, served as dep. conservator of forests, Karnul, and forest settlm. officer; special asst., Malabar, Feb., 1886; employed under govt. of India as offg. dep. acctnt. gen., Aug.-Nov., 1887; dep. acctnt.-gen., Madras, Jan., 1888; acting sec. to the commrs. of land rev., 1888-90, and again, 1890-92; sub-collr. and joint mag., July, 1890; collr. and mag., April, 1895; dist. and sess. judge, March, 1896; private sec. to govr. of Madras, Oct., 1897-Jan., 1901; collr. and mag., Oct., 1900; C.I.E., Jan., 1901; member, rev. board, and commr. of land rev., May, 1901; ditto, and inam commr., May, 1902; addl. member of legisl. council, Madras, Sept., 1901, Sept., 1903, and Dec., 1904; member, rev. board, and commr. of land rev., March, 1904.

SIMCOX, ARTHUR HENRY ADDENBROOKE, B.A., Indian C.S. (2nd asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Winchester and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 19th Nov., 1894, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; and asst. pol. agent, Surana; K.-i.-H. medal, May, 1900; 2nd asst. and Western Bhil agent, Dec., 1902 to Aug., 1903.

SIME, JOHN, C.I.E., M.A., LL.D., late Punjab Educ. Dept.—Prof., govt. coll., Agra, April, 1869; from Sept., 1870, served in the Punjab as principal, Delhi coll., till 1877, and subsequently as prof., Lahore coll.; inspr. of schs., Aug., 1887; tutor to the Maharaja of Patiala, May, 1885; dir. of public instrn., Punjab, and under sec. to govt. in the educ. dept., Oct., 1890; C.I.E., Dec., 1898; ret'd., Nov., 1901; author of 'Man and his Duty,' and of works on arithmetic and mensuration.

SIMPSON, SIR BENJAMIN, K.C.I.E., M.D., Surgn.-Gen., late Bengal Med. Service.—Joined the Indian med. dept., 1853; served in civil employ from 1860, as asst. surgn. in Bengal, and was in med. charge of the Bhutan mission in 1863; on special duty on N.E. frontier, 1867-68; civil surgn., Darjiling, April, 1868; from Jan., 1871, served as civil surgn. of Patna with charge of the Mitapur jail; on special duty to the cinchona commn., Feb., 1871; guardian to the Maharaja of Kuch Behar during his visit to Europe, March, 1878; dep. surgn.-gen., S. Afghanistan field force, 1881; principal med. officer, Quetta, 1881; dep. surgn.-gen., March, 1882; sanitary commr., Central Prov., 1882; surgn.-gen. in the Punjab, 1883-84; surgn.-gen., Bengal, 1884-85; sanitary commr. and surgn.-gen. with the govt. of India, March, 1885; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1887; ret'd. March, 1890.

SIMPSON, DAVID, M.A., M.B., Major, I.M.S. (prof., med. coll., Madras).—First commn., 31st March, 1887; in mil. employ till July, 1891, from which date he acted in Madras as 2nd surgn., gen. hosp.; civil surgn.; surgn.,

3rd dist.; again as 2nd surgn., gen. hosp. and prof., med. coll.; sec. to the surgn.-gen. with the govt. of Madras, and surgn., 2nd dist.; dist. med. and sany. off., Sept., 1898; prof. of anatomy, med. coll., 2nd surgn., gen. hosp., and port and marine surgn., Dec., 1903; prof. of midwifery, med. coll., and supt. of maternity hosp., April, 1905.

SIMPSON, FRANK DOUGLAS, M.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag., United Prov.).—Educ. at Aberdeen Univ. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 10th Dec., 1894, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Dec., 1901.

SIMPSON, JAMES THOMAS, B.C.E., M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd., 4th May, 1869, and posted to Bengal; exec. engr., March, 1880; transf. to Burma, June, 1887; suptg. engr., Oct., 1897; retd., June, 1904.

SIMPSON, JOHN HOPE, Indian C.S. (regr., co-operative credit societies, United Prov.).—Educ. at foreign schs. and at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 30th Oct., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Nov., 1895; asst. commr., July, 1898; dep. commr., Dec., 1901; joint sec., board of revenue, Jan., 1903; regr., co-operative credit societies, Nov., 1904.

SIMPSON, MAURICE GEORGE, A.I.E.E., India Tel. Dept. (supt.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. supt., 1st Oct., 1887; supt., Feb., 1901.

SIMPSON, STUART JOHN, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. 1st April, 1872, as asst. engr., and posted to Oudh; exec. engr., Jan., 1885; retd., 1890.

SINCLAIR, DAVID, C.S.I., M.B., C.M., surgn.-genl., late Indian Med. Service.—Joined the Indian med. dept., 1st Oct., 1869; arrived, 14th May, 1870; from 1871, served in British Burma, and was employed in the analysis of potable waters in cantonmts., 1871-72; served subsequently as jail supt., offg. civil surgn., and offg. inspr.-gen. of prisons; inspr.-gen. of jails, sanitary commr., supt. of vaccn., and chief of civil med. dept., Burma, Jan., 1884; C.S.I., Dec., 1898; surgn.-genl. and princ. med. offr., Madras comd., Oct., 1899; retd. Nov., 1904.

SINGH, B. (see Bharat Singh).

SINGH, B. R. (see Bakhshi Ram Singh).

SINGH, C. N. (see Chunder Narayan Singh).

SINHA, S. P. (see Satyendra Prasanna Sinha).

SKEMP, FRANK WHITTINGHAM, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Owen's Coll., Manchester; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 10th Dec., 1904, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

SKINNER, JOHN ALLAN CLEVELAND, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Wellington Coll., and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 28th Oct., 1885, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr., and registrar, jud. commr.'s court; dep. commr., Oct., 1894; divl. judge, July, 1898, to Sept., 1900.

SKINNER, RUSSELL MORLAND, Col., I.S.C., late Police Dept., Bengal.—First comm., 7th Dec., 1855; served through the Mutiny; joined

the dept. as asst. supt., April, 1862; district supt., Aug., 1863; retd., April, 1888.

SKINNER, STEPHEN STUART, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Eton and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 6th Dec., 1896, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; transf. to Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., Jan., 1902.

SKRINE, FRANCIS HENRY, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Blackheath Proprietary Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1870, and served as asst. mag. and collr. in Bengal; on famine relief duty, 1874 (Behar) and 1877-78 (Madras); joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1884; mag. and collr., Aug., 1887; offg. commr., Bhagalpur, 1893-94; offg. collr. of customs, Calcutta, April, 1895; confd., Oct., 1895; offg. commr., Chittagong, April, 1896; retd., April, 1897; joint author of 'The Heart of Asia'; author of 'The Life of Sir W. W. Hunter,' 'The Expansion of Russia,' &c.

SLACKE, FRANCIS ALEXANDER, B.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Bengal).—Educ. at Taunton Coll. Sch., Univ. Coll., Oxford, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 8th Dec., 1876, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; settl. officer of wards' and attached estates in Chota Nagpur; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1888; offd. as commr., Chittagong, Sept., 1890; mag. and collr., May, 1892; sec. to board of rev., Nov., 1895; sec. to govt. of Bengal, genl. depts., June, 1900; temp. commr., Feb., 1903; confd., Oct., 1903.

SLADEN, FRANCIS FARQUHAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at the Charterhouse, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 12th Dec., 1899, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.

SLADEN, JOSEPH, B.A., Indian C.S. (junr. collr., Bombay).—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 19th Nov., 1888, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; and in Sind as asst. commr., and Sindhi translr.; acting collr. and mag., May, 1898; 1st asst., Nov., 1900; junr. collr., July, 1903; offg. sec. to govt., genl., &c., depts., Dec., 1903.

SLATER, ALFRED WILLIAM, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 6th Nov., 1872, and employed on irrign. works in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; on famine relief duty in Madras, 1877-78; transf. to buildings and roads branch, Feb., 1881; exec. engr., Nov., 1881.

SLATER, JOHN SAMUEL, late Bengal Educ. Dept.—From Sept., 1870, served in the public works dept., Punjab, on the Bari Doab and Indus canals; from Nov., 1875, served in the Bengal educn. dept. as prof., civil engrg. dept., presidency coll., and, from April, 1880, as prof. of the Howrah engrg. coll.; principal, Sibpur engrg. coll., Dec., 1891; retd., June, 1904.

SLATER, JOHN SANDERS, B.A., Barr. (1872) (administrator-genl., Bombay).—Joined the service, 23rd Jan., 1888, as prof. Elphinstone Coll.; apptd. acting chief presidency mag. and rev. judge, Bombay, Aug., 1891; confd., March, 1895; administrator-genl., Bombay, Aug., 1905.

SLATER, SAMUEL HENRY, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at King Edward VII. Gr. Sch., Louth, and Oxford Univ.;

apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1904, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

SLIGHT, ANDREW MURE, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras).—Educ. at Royal Coll., Mauritius, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 12th Nov., 1889, and served as asst. collr. and mag.; dist. mag., Mar., 1896; head asst., May, 1899; sub collr. and joint mag., Dec., 1901; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1904.

SLIGO, MARQUIS OF (see Browne, Lord Henry Ulick).

SLOCOCK, FRANCIS SAMUEL ALFRED, Indian C.S. (2nd sec. to chief commr., Cent. Prov.).—Educ. at Marlborough and Trinity Coll., Oxford, apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 10th Dec., 1891, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; transfed. to Cent. Prov., April, 1893; asst. commr., July, 1898; dep. commr., July, 1900; 2nd sec. to chief commr., Jan., 1904.

SLOCOMBE, CHARLES, India Office (2nd class clerk, Indo-European Telegraph, Public Works Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, acctnt.-gen'l's. dept., 22nd July, 1892; transfed. to Indo-European Telegraph Dept., June, 1900.

SLY, FRANK GEORGE, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Cent. Prov.).—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 16th Dec., 1887, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr. and settlt. officer; under sec. to govt. of India, dept. of rev. and agric., Aug., 1895; dep. commr., Feb., 1898; pol. agent, Chatisgarh feudatories, Feb., 1899; commr. of settlts. and agric., April, 1901; offg. inspr.-genl. of agric., Nov., 1904.

SMALLMAN, HENRY FRANCIS, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 3rd Nov., 1868, and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., Jan., 1880; services lent to Patiala state, 1885-87; ret'd., Aug., 1868.

SMART, ALEXANDER WILLIAM, Col., R.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 15th July, 1868; from March, 1871, served in Madras as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; asst. chief engr. for irrign., and under sec. to govt., 1885-86; acting principal, coll. of engrg., 1891-92, and in 1894; supg. engr., July, 1895; chief engr. and joint sec. to govt., irrign. branch, Oct., 1902; transfed. to unemployed list, Oct., 1903.

SMART, WILFRID WILMOT, Indian C.S. (settltnt. offr., Baluchistan).—Educ. at Dulwich Sch. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 16th Nov., 1900, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. offr.; settlmt. offr., Baluchistan, Oct., 1905.

SMEATON, DONALD MACKENZIE, C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at the Abbey Park Inst., St. Andrews, and St. Andrews Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1867, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; from May, 1870, served in the settlt. dept.; transfed. to British Burma as settlt. sec. to chief commr. April, 1879; sec. to chief commr., Burma, in the land rev. and agricl. dept., and dir. of agric., May, 1882; dir. of agric. and commerce, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, May, 1886; offg. chief sec. to chief commr., Burma, April, 1887; commr., central divn., Upper Burma, May, 1888; finl. commr., March, 1891; in May, 1892,

and in 1896 offd. as chief commr.; C.S.I., Jan., 1895; member of viceroy's legisl. council, Nov., 1898, and Dec., 1900; K.-i.-H. medal, May, 1900; ret'd., Oct., 1902; author of an annotated edition of the N.W. Prov. rev. act of 1873, 'The Currency of India,' 'The Karens of Burma,' &c.

SMEATON, ROBERT, M.A., LL.D., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the Abbey Park Instn., St. Andrew's, and St. Andrew's Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 5th Dec., 1868, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlt. officer; first asst. sec. to govt., July, 1876; under sec., May, 1877; junr. sec., Aug., 1877; sec., finl. dept., May, 1884; member of provl. legisl. council, Dec., 1891; ret'd., April, 1894.

SMITH, ALAN KENYON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Uppingham Sch. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 28th Nov., 1902, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.

SMITH, ALBERT CHARLES, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Arrived, 5th Jan., 1864; joined the dept., Sept., 1864, and was apptd. exec. engr., March, 1870; suptg. engr., Sept., 1889; offg. joint sec. in the public works dept., April-July, 1891; ret'd., Jan., 1894.

SMITH, BOTELER CHERNOCKE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 6th Dec., 1897, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

SMITH, CECIL ARCHIBALD, A.M.I.C.E., Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1880; exec. engr., April, 1893.

SMITH, CHARLES JOHN, Col., R.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—First commn., Madras engrs., Dec., 1855; asst. engr., Jan., 1858; exec. engr., Nov., 1865; dep. consulting engr. for railways, 1870-83; under sec. to govt., public works dept., May, 1872; suptg. engr., 1st class, Sept., 1883; consulting engr. and joint sec. to govt., railway branch, 1883; chief engr., Dec., 1885; re-apptd. consulting engr., July, 1889; reverted to mil. duty, July, 1891; ret'd., May, 1892.

SMITH, CHARLES MICHIE, B.Sc. (director, Kodaikanal and Madras Observatories).—Apptd. govt. astronomer, Madras, 4th July, 1891; director, Kodaikanal and Madras observatories, April, 1899.

SMITH, FRANCIS ST. GEORGE MANNERS, India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1879, and posted to Rajputana; exec. engr. Nov., 1889; K.-i.-H. medal, May, 1900; suptg. engr., Feb., 1905.

SMITH, FRANCIS WHITMORE, C.I.E., India Office (asst. secretary, Mil. Dept.).—Educ. at Bedford Sch. and Cheltenham Coll.; temporary clerk, 23rd Jan., 1864; junr. clerk, mil. dept., July, 1866; senr. clerk, Jan., 1880; asst. sec., mil. dep., May, 1895; C.I.E., Jan., 1900.

SMITH, HENRY ALEXANDER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Clifton Coll. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after

exam. of 1891; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1900, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

SMITH, HENRY ANDREW FELTON, late Police Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the service, 7th Aug., 1861; inspr. of police, April, 1866; asst. district supt., July, 1867; district supt., March, 1876; ret'd., April, 1897.

SMITH, HENRY BABINGTON, C.B., C.S.I., M.A., late Private Sec. to the Viceroy of India (sec. of G.P.O., London).—Educ. at Eton and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; examr. in the educn. dept. (England), Dec., 1887; private sec. to the chancellor of the exchequer (Mr. Goschen), May, 1891; clerk in the treasury, Aug., 1892; sec. to the British delegates at the Brussels monetary conference, Nov., 1892; private sec. to the viceroy, Feb., 1894-Dec., 1898; C.S.I., June, 1897; member of the Council of the Ottoman debt at Constantinople, 1900; presdt. of the same, 1901; sec. of G.P.O., London, Sept., 1903; C.B., June, 1905.

SMITH, HENRY GEORGE FREDERICK, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. apprentice engr., 11th Mar., 1874, and posted to Bengal; asst. engr., Mar., 1875; transfid. to state rlys., July, 1875; exec. engr., July, 1891.

SMITH, HENRY MONCRIEFF, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1897, and served in the N.W. Provs. as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and col'r.

SMITH, HENRY SCOTT, Indian C.S. (divl. judge, Punjab).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Stratford-on-Avon, and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 4th Dec., 1886, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dist. judge, June, 1894; divl. judge, Jan., 1900.

SMITH, HENRY WHITBY, Indo-European Telegraph dept. (director, Persian Gulf section).—Entered the Indian Telegraph dept., 22nd Sept., 1879, and served as asst. supt., and supt.; transfid. to I. E. Tel. dept., Jun. 1900, as dir., Persian Gulf section.

SMITH, JAMES COWLISHAW, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. settmt. officer, United Prov.).—Educ. at Derby, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 7th Dec., 1896, and served in the N.W.P. and Oudh as asst. commr., asst. mag. and col'r., and asst. settmt. officer.

SMITH, JOHN HERBERT MONK, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1884, and posted to state rlys.; exec. engr., Aug., 1895; dep. consgr. engr. for rlys., Lucknow, Oct., 1898 to Nov., 1901.

SMITH, JOHN GEORGE, B.A., Barr. (1884), Jud. Dept., Madras (chief judge, small cause court).—After offg. as judge, small cause court, 1893; as Indian Law reporter, 1896; as crown prosecutor, 1898; as chief judge, small cause court, 1899, 1900, and 1902; was confd. in the latter appt., 14th Sept., 1903.

SMITH, JOHN MACDONALD, Col., Indian Army, late Jail Dept., Madras.—First commn., Madras infantry, 20th April, 1855; served in the China expedition; asst. to the agent in Vizagapatam, 1861-68; junr. asst. col'r., Ganjam, 1870-78; supt. of jails, Rajamahendri, 1880-82; from June, 1882, served as paymaster of pensioners, Ban-

galore, and also acted as district mag. and prest. of the municipality; chief presidency mag., Madras, Dec., 1885; inspr.-gen. of jails, Mar., 1891; reverted to mil. dept., Aug., 1892; author of a hand-book in the Khond language.

SMITH, JOHN MANNERS, V.C., C.I.E., Major, Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (pol. agent).—Joined the service, 10th March 1883; attaché in the foreign dept., Nov., 1887; on special duty to Sikkim, in connection with the negotiations with the Chinese Amban, Nov., 1888; asst. to the officer on special duty at Gilgit, June, 1889; asst. to agent, Rajputana, Sept., 1892; on depn. to Kotah state, Oct., 1892; on duty under the orders of the British agent at Gilgit, April, 1893; on depn. with Kabul mission, Aug.-Dec., 1893; C.I.E., Jan., 1894; pol. agent, Bundelkhand, July, 1894; first asst. to agent in Baluchistan, Nov., 1894; asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, Nov., 1896; in charge of ex-Amir, Oct., 1897; pol. agent in Haraoti and Tonk, April, 1898; pol. agent, Gilgit, October, 1898; pol. agent, Bikaner, Nov., 1901; pol. agent, Malwa, April, 1903; pol. agent, Bhopal, June, 1904; offg. resdt. in Nepal, April, 1905.

SMITH, JOSEPH, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 25th Sept., 1861; from Sept., 1862, served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and col'r., and joint mag.; mag. and col'r., Aug., 1881; offg. district and sessions judge, July, 1886; ret'd., April, 1887.

SMITH, JULIAN CARTER CARINGTON, M.B., C.M., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg'n. Meerut).—First commn., 31st March, 1879; has held the appt. of civil surg'n. at various stations in the United Prov. since Dec., 1890; Meerut, May, 1902.

SMITH, SAMUEL GEORGE, India Office (senior clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept.).—Junr. clerk, 6th Jan., 1883; joint editor, India Office List, July, 1902, to April, 1905; senr. clerk, April, 1905.

SMITH, SYDNEY, late Punjab Police Dept.—Entered the service, July, 1870, as asst. dist. supt. of police; dist. supt., May, 1881; asst. inspr.-genl., railway police, Lahore, May, 1900; dep. inspr.-gen., Nov., 1901; ret'd., Aug., 1905.

SMITH, THOMAS DENROCHE, B.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and col'r., asst. commr., and dep. commr.; apptd. district and sessions judge, Tipperah, Dec., 1877, and served subsequently in the jud. dept.; ret'd., Jan., 1888.

SMITH, THOMAS WILLIAM, India Office (2nd class clerk, store dept.).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, India store depot, 23rd May, 1888.

SMITH, TURTON, late Police Dept., Punjab.—From 26th March, 1863, served in the dept. as asst. supt. and district supt.; between Dec., 1871, and Sept., 1878, also served in the postal dept. as offg. asst. dir.-gen. of post offices, and as offg. postmr.-gen. at Bombay, Aug., 1876, and at Madras, May, 1878; dep. inspr.-gen. of police, Aug., 1892; inspr.-gen. and under-sec. to Govt., July, 1898; ret'd., July, 1901.

SMITH, VINCENT ARTHUR, M.A., late Indian C.S., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 2nd Nov., 1871, and served in

the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; from Nov., 1874, served in the settl. dept.; on special duty in the Allahabad district, Nov., 1880; served subsequently as asst. mag. and joint mag., and was also settl. officer of Basti, 1883-84; mag. and collr., June, 1889; district and sessions judge, March, 1895; judge, 1st grade, Feb., 1897; off. chief sec. to govt., May, 1898; commr., Oct., 1898; ret'd., July, 1900; author of 'The Settlement Officer's Manual,' 'Coinage of the Early Gupta Dynasty,' 'Græco-Roman Influence on the Civilization of Ancient India,' 'History and Conquests of Samudra Gupta,' 'Asoka, the Buddhist Emperor of India,' 'The Jain Stupa and other Antiquities of Mathurâ,' 'The Early History of India,' and editor of Sleeman's 'Rambles and Recollections.'

SMITHE, EDMUND DU CANE, India Public Works Dept. (chief engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1875, and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., Dec., 1883; transfed. to buildings and road branch, Oct., 1894; under sec. to govt., Punjab, Jan., 1897; suptg. engr. and sanitary engr. to govt., Punjab, Oct., 1900; sec. to govt., Punjab, buildings and roads, Oct., 1903; chief engr., Dec., 1903.

SMITHER, MAURICE, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Aldenham Gr. Sch. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 9th Jan., 1895, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and offg. cantonmt. mag.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1900.

SMYTH, JOHN, M.D., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (surgn.-in-charge, Victoria hosp., Bangalore).—First commn., 1st Oct., 1881; in mil. employ till Dec., 1885, from which date he held offg. appts. in the med. coll. and gen. hosp., Madras, and acted as civil surgn., Vizagapatam; resdt. surgn., gen. hosp., and profg. of pathology, med. coll., Madras, Dec., 1887; med. offr. with the Maharaja of Mysore, April, 1897; surgn.-in-charge, Victoria hosp., Bangalore, 1903.

SMYTH, JOHN WATT, Barr. (1871), late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1857; arrived, 29th Dec., 1857, and was apptd. asst. mag. and collr., Agra; transfed. to Punjab, May, 1859, and served as asst. commr., and as personal asst.; registrar of the chief court, Feb., 1866; dep. commr., Oct., 1869; judge, chief court, March, 1880; ret'd., 1885.

SMYTH, JOHN WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Sind).—Educ. at Hymer's Coll., Hull, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 25th Nov., 1904, and served in Sind as asst. collr. and mag.

SMYTH, RALPH GRAHAM, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Lieut., Bengal engrs., 7th Dec., 1855; from 6th Nov., 1857, served in the dept. in the Central and N.W. Prov. as asst. engr.; served in the Mutiny (medal and clasp); offg. garrison engr., Lucknow, June, 1859; on mil. duty, May, 1861; re-apptd., April, 1862, and served in Bengal as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; suptg. engr., July, 1876; transfed. to Hyderabad, Dec., 1881, as sec. to rest. in the public works dept.; offg. chief engr. and sec. to chief commr., Burma, 1885; ret'd., Dec., 1889.

SMYTH, SAMUEL ANDREW, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Londonderry Sch. and Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 10th Dec., 1901, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

SMYTH, THOMAS WATT, M.A., Barr. (1872), late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 2nd Feb., 1862, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and small cause court judge; registrar of the chief court, 1867-70; dep. commr., April, 1877; commr., April, 1883; divl. judge, Nov., 1884; judge, chief court, Jan., 1887; ret'd., April, 1889; author of 'Acts and Regulations applicable to the Punjab.'

SMYTH, THOMAS WILLIAM SCORE, Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 24th Aug., 1886; exec. engr., Aug., 1897; dep. chief engr., and under sec. to govt., July, 1904; asst. to chief engr. and under sec. to govt. (irrigation), Oct., 1904.

SMYTH, WILLIAM JOHN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Bath Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 1st Jan., 1894, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

SMYTHIES, ARTHUR, B.A., late India Forest Service.—Apptd. to the dept. as asst. conservator, Sept., 1873, and served in the Cent. Provs., N.W. Provs., Bengal, and Burma; dep. conservator, Sept., 1883; conservator, July, 1897; ret'd., Dec., 1902.

SNELL, FRANCIS WILLIAM, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army, late Bombay Pol. Dept.—First commn., 23rd Jan., 1869; mil. duty to Jan., 1885; asst. pol. rest., Perim, Jan., 1885; Aden, Aug., 1886; served also at Savantvadi and Baroda; admr., Rajpipla State, Aug., 1895; admr., Porbandar State, Nov., 1897; asst. pol. agent, S. Mahratta country, Oct., 1900; asst. pol. rest., Aden, Nov., 1901; offg. pol. agent, Cutch, Oct., 1900, to July, 1903; to U.S. list, Aug., 1903.

SNOW, PHILIP CHICHELEY HYDE, C.I.E., Barr. (1876), late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Haileybury and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 11th Nov., 1876; asst. commr., Delhi, Nov., 1876; from March, 1877, served in British Burma as asst. commr., asst. cantonmt. mag., &c.; from Nov., 1881, served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and district registrar; forest settl. officer, Dec., 1885; acting under sec. to govt., pol., &c., depts., Sept., 1886; junr. collr. and mag., June, 1894; municipal commr., Bombay, Mar., 1896; C.I.E., May, 1898; acting commr., Dec., 1898; addl. member of Govr.'s Council, Feb., 1899; collr. and mag., and dist. registrar, Poona, May, 1900; ret'd., Dec., 1901.

SOLIS, ENRIQUE JUAN CHRISTOBAL, India Office (2nd class clerk, registry and record dept.).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 2nd Aug., 1904.

SORABJI PADAMJI PESTANJI, Statutory C.S. (joint judge, and sess. judge, Bombay).—Probr. under the native civil service rules, 14th Jan., 1880; confd., March, 1882; served as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settl. officer; 2nd asst., May, 1889; 1st asst., March, 1893; asst. judge and sess. judge, March, 1899; joint judge and addl. sess. judge, April, 1902.

SORABSHAW HORMASJI DANTRA, M.D., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service, late Burma Med. Dept.—First commn., Mar., 1876; civil surgn., Tharrawaddy, Aug., 1878; civil surgn., Mandalay, April, 1890; ret'd., July, 1903.

SOUTER, CHARLES ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Arbroath High Sch., Edinburgh Univ., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 25th Dec., 1901, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

SPANKIE, GEORGE THEOPHILUS.—Called to the bar (Lincoln's Inn), 6th June, 1871; apptd. addl. jud. commr. in Oudh, 11th Nov., 1892; ret'd., Oct., 1904.

SPEARMAN, HORACE RALPH, Col., Indian Army, late Burma Commn.—Ensign, 91st Highlanders, 18th Dec., 1857; proby. asst. engr., public works dept., 1862; from Dec., 1862, served in the Burma commn. as asst. commr. and asst. sec. to the chief commr.; offg. asst. pol. agent, Bhamo, March, 1871; dep. commr., Jan., 1877; prest., Rangoon municipal committee, 1883 and 1886, and of the Akyab municipal committee, 1884; commr., April, 1890; returned to Europe, Dec., 1895; author of 'British Burma Gazetteer.'

SPENCE, JAMES KNOX, C.S.I., late Indian C.S., Bombay.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll. and Royal Mil. Acad., Woolwich; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 29th Oct., 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; from May, 1878, to Oct., 1880, served in the postal dept.; junr. collr. and mag., and pol. agent, Kolaba, June, 1885; senr. collr. and collr. of land rev., &c., Bombay, Nov., 1888; acting commr. of customs, &c., Dec., 1889; also British delegate for the Portuguese treaty; commr., Nov., 1891; member of legisl. council, July, 1895, and again, July, 1897, and June, 1898; acted as member of council, April–July, 1896, and again, Nov., 1898; C.S.I., Jan., 1898; member of govr.-gen.'s legisl. council, Jan.–Mar., 1899; ret'd., Aug., 1900.

SPENCE, LUMLEY HOLLAND, Bombay Police Dept. (supt. of police).—Joined the dept. as probationer, 7th Jan., 1882; asst. supt., June, 1886; supt., Jan., 1889; 1st grade, and asst. inspr.-genl. of police, Poona, June, 1905.

SPENCE, PHILIP TREVOR AUGUSTINE, Major, Indian Army (pol. agent, Alwar).—First commn., 29th Aug., 1885; pol. asst., May, 1894; asst. sec. to govt. of India, Foreign dept., Nov., 1899; asst. commr., Ajmer, Nov., 1900, to May, 1902; pol. agent, Bikaner, Nov., 1903; ditto, Alwar, June, 1904.

SPENCER CHARLES GORDON, Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and joint mag., Madras).—Educ. at Marlborough and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 26th Nov., 1890, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Nov., 1896; sub-collr. and joint mag., May, 1903.

SPENCER, HUGH, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Bristol Gr. Sch., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 17th Dec., 1888, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Nov., 1893; dep. commr., April, 1900; mag. and collr., April, 1903; dep. commr., 1st grade, Sept., 1903.

SPENCER, LIONEL DIXON, C.B., M.D., Surgn.-Gen., Indian Med. Service.—Asst.

surgn., Bengal med. establ., March, 1865; served under the civil dept. in the Central Prov., and with the Central India horse; from Oct., 1871, served as agency surgn., E. states, Rajputana; on famine duty, 1877, and acted as pol. agent, Karauli, 1877, and Bhartpur, 1878; in 1879 was in med. charge of the Marwar and Jaisalmer agency; from March, 1881, served as agency surgn., Rajputana, and supt.-gen. of dispensaries and vaccn.; reverted to mil. dept., July, 1892; apptd. princ. med. offr., Punjab Frontier Force, Oct., 1892, to Oct., 1897; C.B., Aug., 1895; princ. med. officer, Punjab comd., Oct., 1898; ret'd., June, 1902.

SPILSBURY, EDGAR CHARLES, Major, R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to Bombay public works dept. as asst. engr., 17th Aug., 1876; served in the Afghan war, 1879–80 (medal); employed on Bombay defences, March, 1883; exec. engr., March, 1886; transfld. to Burma, Dec., 1893; to Rajputana and Central India, Sept., 1895; to Mount Abu divn., April, 1899; sec., public works dept., to agents of govr.-gen. for Rajputana and Central India; suptg. engr., Feb., 1901; ret'd., Aug., 1902.

SPRING, FRANCIS JOSEPH EDWARD, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. 4th Aug., 1870, and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab; transfld. to state railways, May, 1873; exec. engr., Jan., 1880; under sec. to govt. of Bengal, railway branch, Sept., 1884; employed on N.W. line, March, 1888; engr.-in-chief, E. Coast railway, Nov., 1890; offg. consulting engr. for railways, Bombay, and sec. to govt., Aug., 1893; employed on Bezwada-Madras railway survey, Nov., 1893; consulting engr. to govt. of India for railways, Assam, Jan., 1894; C.I.E., May, 1894; rank of suptg. engr., July, 1894; consulting engr. for railways, Madras, and joint sec. to govt. public works dept., March, 1897, and Feb., 1901; chief engr., Nov., 1899; ret'd., Jan., 1904.

SPROTT, FREDERICK LAWRENCE, Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 23rd Sept., 1884; asst. engr., Nov., 1885; exec. engr., Jan., 1897; principal and profir. of civil engrg., coll. of science, Poona, Nov., 1899 to April, 1903; engr. and sec., Indus River Commn., April, 1904.

SPRY, HENRY ERNEST, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 17th Dec., 1904, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

SRI LAL, Pandit, Barr. (1889), Statutory C.S. (joint mag., United Prov.).—Apptd. by the govt. of India a probir. under the statutory civil service rules; asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, 30th March, 1889; confid., 21st May, 1891; asst. settl. officer, Jan., 1894; joint mag., May, 1896; author of two agric. pamphlets.

SRI NATH PAL, B.L., late Bengal Judl. Dept.—Apptd. munsif, Feb., 1874; subordinate judge, Nov. 1895, to Feb., 1903.

SRINIVASA AIYAR, RISHIYUR VENKATA, Diwan Bahadur (inspr.-gen. of registration, Madras).—Joined the service as asst. in the rev. settl. dept., Madras, March, 1873; apptd. sec. to commr. of rev. settl., and to the dir. of the dept. of land records and agric., Madras,

July, 1896; addtl. member of legisl. council, Madras, Nov., 1902, and Oct., 1903; inspr.-gen. of registration, Dec., 1903.

STACK, GEORGE AUGUSTINE, late Bengal Educ. Dept.—Served from 1st Jan., 1868, as prof. at the Agra, Bareilly, and Dacca colls.; prof., Patna coll., Jan., 1883; presidency coll., Feb., 1888; ret. April, 1897.

STACK, OLIVER STOKES, late Police Dept., Bengal.—From 8th June, 1859, served as dep. mag. and dep. collr.; from May, 1862, served in the police dept., and was apptd. district supt., 1st grade, Oct., 1878; dep. inspr.-gen., July, 1885; offg. inspr.-gen., June, 1886; ret. Oct., 1890.

STALEY, ALFRED EVELYN, Barr. (1889), late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., Concord, U.S.A., and St. Peter's Coll., Radley; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 20th Oct., 1875, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Bengal, Jan., 1880; offg. under sec. to govt., 1884 and 1885; joint mag., Aug., 1886; acting junr. sec. to rev. board, March, 1890; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1891; ret. June, 1904.

STANDEN, BERTRAM PRIOR, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (commr. of settlmnt. and agric. Central Prov.).—Educ. at Uppingham, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 5th Dec., 1888, and served in Burma and the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; registrar, jud. commr.'s court, Nagpur, March, 1891; settlt. officer, Dec., 1894; dep. commr., Aug., 1897; C.I.E., Jan., 1901; commr. of settlmnt. and agric., Sept., 1904.

STANLEY, EDWARD GOWER, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 24th Aug. 1886, and posted to Hyderabad; transf. to Burma, Oct., 1891; exec. engr., Jan., 1897; asst. to chief engr. and under sec. to govt., Burma, Feb., 1898, and April, 1900, to April, 1903; offg. under sec. to govt. of India (civil works), April, 1903.

STANLEY, SIR JOHN, K.C., Barr. (chief justice, N.W. Prov.).—Apptd. judge, high court, Calcutta, Nov., 1898; chief justice, N.W. Prov., Aug. 1901.

STANTIALL, WILLIAM, India Office (senior clerk, public works dept.).—Educ. at the City of London Sch.; clerk, 2nd class, 25th Sept., 1882; junior clerk, July, 1893; senior clerk, Oct., 1903.

STANYON, HENRY JOHN, C.I.E., Barr. (1881), (divl. judge, Central Prov.).—Apptd. divl. judge, Nagpur, March, 1897; dep. commr., and ditto, Oct., 1901; C.I.E., May, 1895.

STARKY, WALTER BAYNTUN, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1883, and posted to Bengal; transf. to Baluchistan, Sept., 1885, and to Central Prov., Jan., 1889; exec. engr., Jan., 1898; under sec. to chief commr., May, 1905.

STAWELL, GEORGE COOPER, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1884, and posted to Bengal; exec. engr., July, 1896; transf. to Burma, July, 1905.

STEAD, VEN. SAMUEL, M.A. (Oxon.), late Ecclesi. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the service, March, 1863; arrived, 24th April, 1863, and served as chaplain at Karachi, Sholapur, Belgaum, and Poona; senr. chaplain, April, 1873;

archdeacon and commissary, Nov., 1878; held charge of the diocese of Bombay, 1877-78, and 1883-84; ret. June, 1886.

STEAVERSON, JOSEPH, B.A., late Jud. Dept., Madras.—Served from 25th Feb., 1878, as acting registrar of the high court (original side), Madras, and was confd. in this appt., Feb., 1880.

STEDMAN, SIR EDWARD, K.C.B., K.C.I.E., Gen., Indian Army (mil. sec., India Office).—First commn., 19th Dec. 1860; served in the Hazara campaign, 1868 (medal with clasp); also in the Afghan war of 1878-80 (medal with clasp and bronze star); served in the Burma expdn. of 1886-89, and commanded the operations in the Shan states (two clasps; C.B.); inspr.-gen. of police, Upper Burma, May, 1887; and of Burma, Oct., 1888; quartermaster-gen. in India, March, 1892; second in comd. of Chitral relief expdn. and general of communications, March, 1895 (medal and clasp); comdg. Burma dist., Nov., 1895 to Jan., 1899; K.C.I.E., June, 1897; sec. mil. dept., India Office, May, 1899; K.C.B., June, 1902.

STEEDMAN, EDWARD BRITTAIN, late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Shrewsbury; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 31st Oct., 1871, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settlt. officer; dir. of settlts. and rev. records, May, 1887; designation changed to dir. of land records and agric.; dep. commr., Dec., 1889; in 1892 offd. as dir. of land records and agric., and rev. sec. to govt.; ret. March, 1898; author of 'Report on the Settlement of the Jhang District.'

STEEL, CHARLES DENTON, B.A., Barr. (1899), Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Prov.).—Educ. at the Wesleyan Connexional Sch. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 13th Nov., 1876, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; asst. supt. and judge, small cause court, Dehra Dun, in 1888-89; asst. commr., April, 1889; joint mag., Jan., 1890; district and sessions judge, April, 1891; dep. commr., April, 1892; mag. and collr., March, 1894; dist. and sess. judge, March, 1900; author of 'Allahabad Gazetteer.'

STEEL, HENRY WILLIAM, late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 10th Nov., 1862, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and dep. commr.; employed in connection with Punjab N. state railway, July, 1874; jud. asst., Dec., 1878; dep. commr., Nov., 1884; ret. May, 1889.

STEEL, JOHN PHILIP, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Lieut., Bengal engrs., Dec., 1855; joined the dept., March, 1860, as asst. engr., Bengal; exec. engr., April, 1868; offg. under sec., govt. of India, public works dept., 1873-75, and again Feb., 1877; suptg. engr. and sec. to agent, Rajputana, May, 1879; offg. chief engr. and sec. to govt., Punjab, March, 1887; offg. sec. to chief commr., Central Prov., Oct., 1887; chief engr. and joint sec. to govt., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, 1888; sec., Dec., 1889; ret. March, 1892.

STEELE, CHARLES EDWARD STAFFORD, late Sind Commn.—Educ. at Victoria Coll., Jersey; joined the service, 11th June, 1869, and served in Sind as supernumerary asst. settlt. officer; supernumerary dep. collr., Dec., 1871; employed under the Transvaal govt. as fin. and rev. commr., 1879-81; served subsequently as asst. collr.

and mag., manager of encumbered estates, acting city mag., Karachi, and acting conservator of forests; head asst. collr., Feb., 1892; dep. commr., upper Sind frontier, Feb., 1894; dep. commr., Thar and Parkar, Nov., 1895; collr. and mag., May, 1893; ret'd., May, 1903.

STEEVENS, SIR JOHN, K.C.B., Major-Gen., late Army Ordnance Dept. (inspr. of mil. equipment and clothing, India Office).—First appt., 1st April, 1874, in control dept. of Army; dep. commy., 29th Nov., 1879; dep. asst. commy. genl., Jan., 1880; asst. commy. genl., Nov., 1882; dep. commy. genl., Aug., 1894; ordce. offr., 1st class, April, 1896; asst. inspr. genl. of ordce., Dec., 1893 to March 1898; C.B., June, 1897; principal ordce. offr., April, 1898; served in the Zulu campaign, 1879, and the Egyptian expedn., 1882; K.C.B., June, 1902; ret'd. from ordce. dept., 1st April, 1903, on appt. as inspr. of equipment and ordce. stores, War Office; inspr. of mil. equipment and clothing, India Office, 21st July, 1905.

STEIN, M. A., Indian Educl. Service (inspr.-gen. of Educn., N.W. Frontier Prov. and Baluchistan).—Educ. at Univ. of Tübingen; principal, Oriental Coll., Lahore, and regr. of the Punjab Univ. from 1886 to March, 1899, when he was apptd. principal of the Calcutta Madrasa; in 1900-01 proceeded on tour of archl. exploration in E. Turkestan; inspr. of schools, Punjab, Nov., 1901; inspr.-genl. of educn. and archl. surveyor N.W. Frontier Prov. and Baluchistan, Jan., 1904; author of "Sand-buried Ruins of Khotan" and a preliminary report on his explorations.

STEINBELT, JEAN MARIE CONRAD, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861; from Jan., 1863, served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., asst. commr., and joint mag.; from April, 1881, served as civil and sessions judge in Banda, and afterwards at Azamgarh; ret'd., April, 1887.

STEPHEN, ARTHUR, M.A., M.B., Col., late Indian Med. Service, Med. Dept., Assam.—Joined the service as prob. at Netley, 30th Sept., 1867; served under the mil. dept. in Abyssinia and the Punjab as asst. surgn. and surgn.; acted at various periods as civil surgn., jail supt., and chemical examr. to the Punjab govt.; supt. of jail, Lahore, Sept., 1875; statistical officer to govt. of India, sanitary and med. depts., Nov., 1880; sanitary commr., Punjab, Nov., 1886; acting principal med. officer and sanitary commr., Assam, March, 1894; ret'd. May, 1899.

STEPHEN, HARRY LUSHINGTON, Barr. (1885) (judge, high court, Calcutta).—Apptd. judge, high court, Calcutta, 18th Nov., 1901.

STEPHEN, KENT HUME, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 24th Sept., 1878, and employed on irrign. works in Bengal; exec. engr., March, 1889; under sec. to govt. of Bengal, public works dept., March, 1892, to March, 1895; suptg. engr., Feb., 1901; ret'd., April, 1903.

STEPHENSON, HUGH LANSDOWN, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Westminster and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 14th Dec., 1895, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt. of Bengal,

judl., etc., dept., Feb., 1901, to May, 1903; joint mag. and dep. coll., May, 1904; pte. sec. to lieut.-govr., Nov., 1904, to April, 1905.

STEVENS, SIR CHARLES CECIL, K.C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Univ. of Melbourne; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 2nd Feb., 1862; served in Bengal, and was apptd. mag. and collr., Oct., 1873; offg. sec., board of rev., April, 1882; commr., Aug., 1885; offg. chief sec. to govt. of Bengal, 1890; member of rev. board, Dec., 1891; member of supreme legisl. council, Jan., 1893; C.S.I., Jan., 1895; offg. lieut.-gov. of Bengal, June, 1897; ret'd., April, 1898; K.C.S.I., Dec., 1898.

STEVENS, SIR JOHN FOSTER, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Birmingham Gr. Sch., at Versailles, and at King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 16th Oct., 1865, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., May, 1876; district and sessions judge, Sept. 1884; jud. commr., Central Prov., April, 1894; acting judge, high court, Calcutta, Feb. to Sept., 1895, and from April, 1897; confd. Dec., 1897; ret'd., March, 1904; Kt., Jan., 1905.

STEVENSON, EDWARD SNEADE BOYD, C.I.E., late Madras Police Dept.—Entered the service, Oct., 1870, and served as inspr., asst. supt. and supt. of police; dep. inspr.-gen., Nov., 1895; ret'd., Sept., 1904; C.I.E., Jan., 1905.

STEVENSON, FRANCIS, India Office (supplemental clerk, registry and record dept.).—Apptd. supplemental clerk, 1st Jan., 1893.

STEVENSON, GEORGE, late Indian C.S., Bengal.—Educ. at King's Coll., Aberdeen, and Glasgow Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 11th Nov., 1872, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and in Assam as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Sylhet, Jan., 1885; returned to Bengal, June, 1886, as joint mag.; mag. and collr., July, 1889; acted as commr., Orissa, in 1895, and at Burdwan, July, 1896, and March, 1897; commr., Jan., 1898; ret'd., June, 1900.

STEVENSON, HENRY LOUIS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., London, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1898, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and asst. settl. offr.

STEVENSON, HENRY WICKHAM, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Bombay (civil surgn.).—First commn., 2nd April, 1881; in mil. employ till Sept., 1891; supt., Nov., 1891; civil surgn., May, 1894.

STEVENSON - MOORE, CHARLES JAMES, Indian C.S. (inspr.-genl. of police, Bengal).—Educ. at Felsted Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 10th Dec., 1887, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1894; also asst. settl. officer, Dec., 1894; settl. officer, Feb., 1896; mag. and collr., Jan., 1898; inspr.-genl. of police, Oct., 1904.

STEVINSON, JOHN SANDBACH, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag., United Prov.).—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Taunton; Pemb. Coll. Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 8th Nov., 1895, and served in N.W.P. and Oudh as asst. commr., and asst. mag. and collr.; asst. settl. offr., Dec., 1899; city mag., Gonda, Dec., 1900, to March, 1902; joint mag., Jan., 1904.

STEWART, ALFRED JOSEPH, India Office (second class clerk, registry and record dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, pol. dept., 27th Sept., 1892; transf. to registry and record dept., Jan., 1896.

STEWART, DAVID MACFARLANE, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Hutcheson's Grammar Sch., Glasgow, Glasgow Univ., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1903, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.

STEWART, ROBERT BARTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (inspr.-genl. of police, Bombay).—Educ. at Dulwich and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 27th Dec., 1886, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., Feb., 1893; 1st asst., June, 1896; on plague duty, Nasik, Oct., 1897; junr. collr. and collr. and mag. and pol. agent, Rewa Kantha, Aug., 1900; ditto, and pol. agent, Dangsa, Dec., 1901; ditto, and pol. agent, Surgana, May, 1902; K-i-H. medal, Nov., 1901; senr. collr., April, 1905; inspr.-genl. of police, Bombay, Aug., 1905.

STEWART, SAMUEL FINDLATER, M.A., India Office (junior clerk, military dept.).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. junior clerk, mil. dept., 19th Oct., 1903.

STEWART, WILLIAM HALL MACKINTOSH, Major, Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (consul at Kerman).—First commn., 25th May, 1878; from June, 1889, acted as attaché, foreign dept., and asst. sec. to govt.; confd. in former post, Oct., 1890; asst. agent, Indore, Jan., 1891; asst. rest., Kashmir, July, 1891; asst. pol. agent, Gilgit, April, 1893; pol. agent, Bhopawar, Dec., 1897; ditto, Malwa, June, 1899; pol. offr. with Sirdar Ayub Khan, Feb., 1900; pol. agent, Gilgit, Oct., 1901; consul at Kerman, Oct., 1905.

STEWART + WILSON, CHARLES, B.A., Barr. (1886), Indian C.S. (postmr.-gen., Punjab).—Educ. at Edinburgh High Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1886, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Dec., 1890; under sec. to govt., Aug., 1891; from April, 1892, served as offg. postmr.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Bengal (1893-94), and Bombay (1894); offg. dep. dir. of post office, Oct., 1894, and again April, 1897; postmr.-gen., Calcutta, April, 1896; ditto, Punjab, Jan., 1899; offg. dir. gen. of post office, Sept. to Dec., 1903.

STIBBS, JAMES CHARLES, India Office (second class clerk, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.).—Educ. at Plymouth Corp. Gr. Sch., Penzance Coll. Sch., and King's Coll., London; clerk, 2nd class, 5th Nov., 1897.

STIFFE, ARTHUR W., Captain, late R.I.M.—First commn., Indian Navy, 24th Feb., 1849; served in Persian war, 1856-57; on special survey for telegraph cable in Persian Gulf, 1862; on special duty in Turkish Arabia, 1863; engr., Persian Gulf telegraphs, and commanding 'Amberwitch,' Oct., 1863; offg. dir., Persian Gulf telegraphs, 1866 and 1869-71; port officer, Calcutta, Aug., 1870; offg. supt. of marine, July, 1880; ex-officio shipping master of Calcutta, Feb., 1882; has brought out new charts and pilots of the Persian Gulf and Mekran coast; also a new chart of Maskat, and a description of the Hugli; ret'd., 1888.

STIFFE, NORMAN CECIL, B.A., Indian C.S. (city mag., Lucknow).—Apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 16th Dec., 1896, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; city mag., Lucknow, Dec., 1903.

STIMPSON, EDWARD JOHN, India Office (second class clerk, store dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 21st May, 1873; resigned, 24th June, 1879; reapptd., 10th July, 1880.

STINTON, SELBY EDWIN, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Godolphin Sch. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived 2nd Dec., 1901, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Assam as asst. commr., Jan., 1905.

STIRLING, GEORGE CLAUDIUS BERESFORD, Burma Pol. dept. (supt., N. Shan states).—Entered the dept., 7th March, 1888; extra asst. commr., Feb., 1889; asst. commr. and asst. pol. offr., July, 1896; supt., N. Shan States, June, 1900.

STODART, JAMES CARLYLE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Campbell Co'l., Belfast, Brasenose Coll., Oxford, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 30th Nov., 1904, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

STOGDON, ABRAHAM WILLIAM, late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Uppingham Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 11th Nov., 1864, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; jud. asst., Sept., 1877; services placed temporarily at disposal of govt. of India, mil. dept., Feb., 1881; dep. commr., Aug., 1883; divl. judge, Nov., 1884; judge of the chief court, July, 1894; ret'd., Oct., 1898.

STOKER, GEORGE HERBERT, B.A., India Office (junior clerk, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.).—Educ. at the City of London Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (scholar, 1893-97); junr. clerk, 11th Oct., 1897.

STOKER, THOMAS, C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Rathmines Sch. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 14th Nov., 1872, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; dep. supt. of family domains of Maharaja of Benares, March, 1878; 1st asst. sec. to govt., April, 1884; under sec., May, 1884; settl. officer, Bulandshahr, Sept., 1886; inspr.-gen. of registrn., and commr. of excise and stamps, Nov., 1891; sec. to govt., scarcity dept., Jan., 1897; C.S.I., May, 1898; commr., July, 1898; ret'd., Jan., 1899; author of 'A Gold Standard for India,' and of the Bulandshahr settl. report.

STOKES, GABRIEL, C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S. (member of council, Madras).—Educ. at Kilkenny Coll., Royal Sch., Armagh, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 28th Oct., 1871, and served in Bombay and Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; acting addl. under sec. to govt. of Madras in the rev. (famine) dept., 1877-79; acting Kanaresetranslr. to govt., Feb., 1880; dep. supt. of census operations, Sept., 1880; sub-collr. and joint mag., May, 1886; collr. and mag., Aug., 1889; govt. agent, Tanjore, Nov., 1895; acting sec. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1896; sec. to govt., eduel., &c., depts., Nov., 1896; addl. member of legisl. council, Dec., 1896, June, 1898, June, 1900, and March, 1902; sec. to govt., rev. dept., May, 1898; chief sec. to govt., Sept., 1898; C.S.I.,

Jan., 1903; ordinary member of council, Madras, Nov., 1903; acting govr. of Madras, Feb., 1906.

STOKES, SIR HENRY EDWARD, K.C.S.I., late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Minehead Diocesan Sch., Kilkenny Coll., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 4th Jan., 1864, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; under sec. to govt. in the depts. under the chief sec., Aug., 1869; acting collr. of sea customs and protector of emigrants, Madras, Jan., 1871; fellow of the univ. of Madras, 1875; sub-sec., board of rev., July, 1875; on special duty with the Madras salt commn., 1876; sec., board of rev., Jan., 1877, to Feb., 1878, and addl. sec. on famine duties, Feb., 1877; district and sessions judge, S. Malabar, Oct., 1879; collr. and mag., Salem, June, 1881; sec. to govt. in the rev. dept., Jan., 1884; member of legisl. council, Oct., 1886; chief sec. to govt., Oct., 1887; temporary member of govr.'s council, July, 1888; ordinary member, Nov., 1888; C.S.I., May, 1889; K.C.S.I., May, 1892; ret'd., Nov., 1893.

STOKES, HENRY JOHN, B.A., late Madras C.S.—Educ. at St. Columba Coll., Rathfarnham, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1861, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; served in Bombay from Oct., 1864, to Jan., 1870, and subsequently in Madras; principal asst., July, 1871; district and sessions judge, June, 1877; collr. and mag., June, 1879; on famine duty, Madura, Aug., 1877; district and sessions judge, Madura, Jan., 1884; collr. and mag., N. Arcot, Oct., 1884; district and sessions judge, Coimbatore, May, 1886; member of the public service commn., Nov., 1886; ret'd., July, 1889.

STOKES, HOPETOUN GABRIEL, B.A., Indian C.S. (under sec. to govt. of India, Home dept.)—Educ. at Clifton and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, Home dept., July, 1903; confd., Jan., 1904.

STOKES, WHITLEY, C.S.I., C.I.E., Hon. D.C.L. (Oxon.), LL.D. (Dublin), Barr. (1855), late Legisl. Dept., Govt. of India.—Reporter to the high court and acting administrator-gen., Madras, 1863-64; served subsequently as asst. sec. to govt. of India, legisl., branch, sec. to legisl. council, and sec. in the legisl. dept.; law member of the council of the govr.-gen., May, 1877, to May, 1882; prest. of Indian law commn., 1879; in 1868 framed a scheme for collecting, copying, and cataloguing the Sanskrit MSS. preserved in India; author of 'Hindu Law Books,' 1865; the 'Indian Succession Act, with a Commentary,' 1865; the 'Indian Companies Act, 1866, with Notes,' the 'Older Statutes in Force in India, with Notes,' 1874; the 'Unrepealed General Acts of the Governor-General of India, with Chronological Tables, &c., 1875-76; the 'Anglo-Indian Codes,' 1887-88, and other works.

STONE, JOSEPH HENRY, M.A., Indian Educ. Service (inspr. of schools, Madras).—Apptd. principal, Teachers' Coll., Madras, Jan., 1886; prof. of history, Presy. Coll., Feb., 1887; principal, Kumbakonam Coll., Aug., 1895; inspr. of schools, April, 1903.

STONE, SAMUEL JAMES, late Police Dept., Punjab.—Apptd. asst. district supt. of police, 10th March, 1861; district supt., Nov., 1872; served

in Assam, 1884-85; resumed his post in the Punjab, April, 1885; ret'd., Feb., 1894.

STONE, WILLIAM RICHARD, Capt., Indian Army (asst. commr., Burma).—First commn., 16th Nov., 1887; in mil. employ till March, 1895, when he joined the Burma commn. as asst. commr.

STONIER, GEORGE ALFRED, C.E., A.R.S.M., F.G.S.—Apptd. to the geol. survey as specialist in mining and metallurgy, 4th Oct., 1899; provl. chief inspr. of mines in India, April, 1901; confd. in that appt., Jan., 1902; ret'd., April, 1904.

STOW, ALAN CHARLES, India Office (second-class clerk, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 7th Oct., 1893.

STOW, ALEXANDER MONTAGU, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Harrow, and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 17th Dec., 1896, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and asst. settlmt. offr.

STOWELL, VERE ARTHUR, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag., United Prov.).—Educ. at Rossall, and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 7th Dec., 1896, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.; dep. commr., Almora, Sept., 1902, to Nov., 1903; joint mag., Nov., 1904.

STRACHEY, SIR JOHN, G.C.S.I., C.I.E., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury in 1842; served in the N.W. Prov. in the grades of asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.; senr. asst., Kumaon, 1848; mag. and collr., Moradabad, 1854; offg. commr. of Kumaon, 1861; prest. of commn. to inquire into cholera epidemic of 1861; jud. commr., Central Prov., 1862; prest. of the sanitary commn. with govt. of India, 1864; chief commr., Oudh, 1866; member of govr.-gen.'s council, March, 1868; acting viceroy and govr.-gen. on death of Earl of Mayo, 9th to 23rd Feb., 1872; K.S.C.I., 1872; lieut.-govr., N.W. Prov., 7th April, 1874; ret'd. from the civil service, Dec., 1876; finl. member of the govr.-gen.'s council, Dec., 1876, to Dec., 1880; C.I.E., 1878; G.C.S.I., 1878; member of council of India, July, 1885, to July, 1895; joint author with Lieut.-Gen. R. Strachey of 'The Finances and Public Works of India,' 1882; author of 'India,' 1888 (2nd edn., 1894), and of 'Hastings and the Rohilla War,' 1892.

STRACHEY, SIR RICHARD, G.C.S.I., F.R.S., Lt.-Gen., R.E.—2nd lieut., Bengal engrs., June, 1836; was employed on irrign. works in the N.W. Prov. from 1840, and apptd. exec. engr., Ganges canal, July, 1843; under sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., 1857; sec. to govt., Central Prov., 1857; consulting engr., railway dept., Sept., 1858; sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., 1862; inspr.-gen. of irrign., and chief engr., 1st class, Dec., 1866; member of supreme legisl. council, 1869; inspg. engi. of machinery and stores for state railways, India office, 1871; member of council of India, Jan., 1875, to July, 1878; on special duty in India, 1878; offg. member of council of govr.-gen., Jan., 1879; prest. of the famine commn., 1880; re-apptd. member of council of India, March, 1879; prest., royal geographical society, 1885-1889; ret'd. from the council of India, on his appt. as chairman of the E. Indian railway company, Oct., 1889; apptd. a representative of India at

the international monetary conference, Brussels, 1892; member of Indian currency committee, 1892-93; joint author with Sir John Strachey of 'The Finances and Public Works of India,' 1882; G.C.S.I., June, 1897.

STRACHEY, RICHARD SHOLTO, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1881, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; transfed. to State rlys., Feb., 1887; lent to Assam-Bengal rly. co., Oct., 1892; exec. engr., Jan., 1894; reverted to govt. service, April, 1900; agent, Assam-Bengal Rly. Co. from Dec., 1901; ret'd., Oct., 1903.

STRAHAN, CHARLES, Major-Gen., R.E., late survey of India dept.—First commn., Bengal engrs., June, 1860; arrived, Oct., 1862; joined the survey dept. as mil. 2nd asst., Sept., 1863; served with the Bhutan field force in charge of reconnoitring party, 1865-66 (medal and clasp); surveyor, Dec., 1867; dep. supt., Sept., 1869; served with the Peshawar field force in Afghanistan, 1879 (medal); asst. surveyor-gen. in charge of cartographic and engraving branches, May, 1884; dep. surveyor-gen. (rev. branch), July, 1892; surveyor-gen. of India, March, 1895; ret'd. from survey dept., Sept., 1899.

STRAHAN, GEORGE, Col., R.E., late Survey of India Dept.—Obtained commn. from Addiscombe, Dec., 1853; arrived, Nov., 1860; served under the mil. and public works depts. till July, 1862, when he was apptd. to the survey dept.; served as asst. in the Gwalior and Central India survey, and afterwards held charges in the Rajputana, Mysore, and Khandesh surveys; dep. supt., Nov., 1867; offg. asst. surveyor-gen., in charge of engraving and cartographic branches, Calcutta, April-July, 1883; dep. surveyor-gen. (trigonol. branch), Oct., 1889; offd. as surveyor-gen. in 1892; reverted to mil. dept., May, 1894; ret'd., June, 1895.

STRAIGHT, SIR DOUGLAS, Kt., late Jud. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Called to the bar at the Middle Temple, Nov., 1865; counsel to London banks association from 1868; junr. counsel, treasury prosecution, from 1877; M.P. for Shrewsbury, 1870-74; apptd. one of the royal commrs. to inquire into corrupt election practices at Boston, 1875; puisne judge, high court, N.W. Prov., June, 1879; offd. as chief justice in 1883, 1884, and 1886; fellow of Calcutta univ., 1883, and of the Allahabad univ., 1887; ret'd., April, 1892; Kt., Aug., 1892.

STRANGE, WILLIAM LUMSDEN, M.I.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Nov., 1880; exec. engr., Oct., 1893; under sec. to govt. of India, July, 1901; employed in the Transvaal from March, 1903.

STRATTON, WALLACE CHRISTOPHER RAMSAY, Major, Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (resdt., W. Rajputana States).—First commn., 26th July, 1881; from May, 1885, acted as asst. agent, Rajputana, and asst. gen. supt., thagi and dakaiti; confd. as asst. agent, Mt. Abu, March, 1888; pol. agent and consul, Muscat, April, 1889; 1st asst. rest., Nov., 1889; transfed. to Bushire, Dec., 1889; asst. pol. agent and consul, Bassora, April, 1890; 1st asst. agent, Baluchistan, April, 1891; pol. agent, S.E. Baluchistan, Oct., 1894; author of a narrative

of Muscat affairs, 1885-88; offg. pol. agent, Kotah, April, 1897; confd., June, 1899; first asst. to gov.-gen.'s agent, Central India, Nov., 1900; pol. agent, Eastern Rajputana States, Feb., 1901; ditto, Bikaner, Oct., 1904; resdtd., W. Rajputana States, April, 1905.

STREATFEILD, CLAUDE ARTHUR CECIL, Indian C.S. (joint mag., United Prov.).—Educ. at Marlborough and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 21st Nov., 1891, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; joint mag., Nov., 1896; supt., Dehra Dun, May, 1898, to June, 1899.

STREATFEILD, HENRY CUTHBERT, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Marlborough and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 29th Dec., 1837, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt. in the finl. and municipal depts., Nov., 1890; and in the jud., &c., depts., Oct., 1892; sec. to the jury commn., 1893; joint mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1894; mag. and collr., Nov., 1898.

STREET, ALFRED WILLIAM FREDERICK, D.S.O., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service, late Bombay Sanitary Dept.—Joined the service, May, 1878; on mil. duty till June, 1888; dep. sany. commr., June, 1888; member of plague comm., July, 1897; dep. sany. commr., Bombay, July, 1898; ret'd., April, 1903.

STREET, ASHTON, M.B., Major, I.M.S., Bombay (presy. surgeon).—First commn., 31st March, 1860; on mil. duty till Nov., 1895, when he was apptd. to officiate as profir. of anatomy, Grant med. coll., and 2nd surgn., J. J. hosp.; offg. ditto and presy. surgn., April, 1903; 2nd surgn., J. J. hosp., presy. surgn., and profir., Grant med. coll., Nov., 1903.

STREET, BROOKS HENRY GEORGE, India Office (supervisor, store dept.).—Writer, 30th June, 1874; clerk, 2nd class, Sept., 1878; supervisor, 2nd grade, India store depôt, Oct., 1883; 1st grade, Aug., 1900.

STREET, HENRY ALFRED, Capt., late R.I.M.—Commander of 'Tenasserim,' Dec., 1876, to June, 1830; asst. sec. to govt. of India, mil. (marine) dept., June, 1880, to March, 1882; on duty in Bombay, 1882-84; asst. dir., Indian marine, April, 1884; port officer, Madras, Dec., 1887; presidency port officer, Madras, Aug., 1888, to Jan., 1893, and again from Feb., 1896; offg. in the same post, 1893-94; and at Calcutta, April, 1895; ret'd., May, 1899.

STRICKLAND, WILLIAM ALEXANDER WICKEDE, Major, Indian Army (dep. commr., Burma).—First commn., Oct., 1880; employed in Burma as asst. commr., Aug., 1886; dep. commr., Jan., 1896.

STRINGER, JOHN ALFRED, India Office, (second class clerk, India audit office).—Educ. at Macclesfield Gr. Sch.; clerk, second class, 26th March, 1897.

STRONG, FREDERICK WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., E. Bengal and Assam.).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., Selwyn Coll., Cambridge, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 9th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and coll; transfed. to Assam as asst. commr. and sub-divl. offr., May, 1899; under sec. to chief commr., April, 1904, to Oct., 1905.

STUART, ANDREW EDMUND CASTLE-STUART, Indian C.S.
